item_10001_Description: This bottle is full of fertilizer.
item_10003_Description: They cannot grow anymore, but they can help other plants grow as fertilizer.
item_1001_Description: This handful of dirt is weird and smelly and damp.

item_1005_Description: A small amount of smelly sulfur.
item_1006_Description: A tiny pinch of saltpeter, a strange substance with many magical uses.

item_10101_Description: A plantable potato seedling. So easy that anyone can plant it!

item_1019_Description: Stick-like and covered in tiny hairs, this is not particularly pleasant to touch.

Elves gain +10% Combat XP when clean and -10% Combat XP when filthy. Duration: 4 hours.

Once deployed, display lasts 5 minutes and can be used by any number of players that have at least Nature Appreciation 60.

item_109_Description: Recovers 100 power instantly, and recovers 120 more over 60 seconds
item_11005_Description: A delicious but potentially deadly mushroom.
item_11010_Description: An inedible mushroom, used by alchemists.
item_11012_Description: A variety of morel, tasty in mushroom dishes and potent in alchemy as well.
item_11013_Description: A variety of mycena mushroom with an especially ... unique... taste.
item_11014_Description: A mildly poisonous toadstool which can be eaten after boiling.
item_11015_Description: A colony of thin tube-like fungus that collects ambient magical energy and can release that energy in an explosive burst.
item_11016_Description: A pretty mushroom that's quite toxic unless it's specially prepared.
item_11017_Description: A fungus that can spit a noxious cloud of poison.
item_11018_Description: While it has a close resemblance to a fly amanita, this mushroom is exceptionally poisonous and never edible.
item_11019_Description: A pretty blue mushroom. Some believe the name comes from its vague resemblance to a wizard's hat, while others believe it's due to the tendency for the mushrooms to explode into freezing electricity when touched.
item_1101_Description: This lovely goblet is teeming with history and you should probably treasure it forever.
item_11020_Description: A thick, fairly toxic mushroom that can be made edible through care.
item_11021_Description: A mushroom that grows in high-ectoplasmic areas.
item_1102_Description: An old brown plate, the kind vendors really like for some reason. Maybe there's a collectibles market you're unaware of.
item_1103_Description: A scrawled note that says, "Please, we need food within a week or our entire village will surely starve. A king's ransom for anyone who comes to our aid!" Sadly, the date is from several years ago.
item_1104_Description: A volume of elven verse. The rhymes are forced and the lines scan poorly, but hey, it's elven verse -- somebody will want this junk.
item_1105_Description: Who knows what this ancient document says? The language has been lost to the ages. Judging by the accompanying stick-figure pornographic art, however, it's probably not really worth dwelling on.
item_1106_Description: It's empty, but the box itself is quite beautiful.
item_1107_Description: A gross antique spoon. Not worth much... but perhaps you can find a spoon collector or something.
item_110_Description: Recovers 20 power per second for 30 seconds.
item_11101_Description: A pile of flaked Parasol mushroom bits.
item_11102_Description: A pile of flaked Mycena mushroom bits.
item_11103_Description: A pile of flaked Boletus mushroom bits.
item_11104_Description: A pile of flaked Field mushrooms.
item_11105_Description: A pile of flaked Blusher mushrooms.
item_11106_Description: A pile of powdered Milk Cap mushrooms.
item_11107_Description: A pile of powdered Blood mushrooms.
item_11108_Description: A pile of powdered Coral mushrooms.
item_11109_Description: A pile of powdered Iocaine mushrooms. This is a deadly toxin to almost all life forms.
item_11110_Description: A carefully tweaked dose of iocaine powder, designed to make you very ill but not kill you, allowing the body to build up resistance to the deadly toxin.
item_11111_Description: A pile of powdered Groxmax mushrooms.
item_11112_Description: A pile of flavored porcini mushrooms.
item_11113_Description: A pile of flavored black foot morel mushrooms.
item_11114_Description: A pile of pixie's parasol flakes. Quite magical and very poisonous.
item_11115_Description: A pile of fly amanita flakes. These are only the normal level of fatal when eaten (which is still 100%, but lower than some other mushrooms).
item_11116_Description: A pile of flaked charged myceliums.
item_11117_Description: A pile of flaked granamurch mushrooms.
item_1111_Description: These spider mandibles seem to be made of ivory! What will nature think of next?
item_1112_Description: This rhino horn is made of ivory. You could have sworn rhino horns were made of hardened fat, but nope, clearly you were wrong.
item_1113_Description: An ornately jeweled hairpin used by goblins of both sexes.
item_1114_Description: A big canine from some animal. It's glowing, and is clearly enchanted. Fascinating.
item_1115_Description: A scrap of fur from a dead animal.
item_1117_Description: Yep, the snout of a pig.
item_1118_Description: A drop of some foul magical liquid.
item_1119_Description: A drop of sweet-smelling magical liquid.
item_111_Description: Delicious gel boosts your maximum power by 100 for two minutes.

item_1123_Description: A very large, well-made brass nail. Useful in Carpentry.
item_1124_Description: A thin metal chain, now broken. It wasn't very high-quality in the first place, either.
item_1125_Description: It's a chunk of glass. Pretty much worthless, although a few Alchemy recipes require bits of glass.
item_1126_Description: This is an especially round pebble. But it's still just a pebble, with probably very few uses.
item_1127_Description: A thin scroll of goblin verse, focusing on the beauty of hard work, the wonders of nature, and the fairness of slavery. It has little value.
item_1128_Description: It's a gross ivory spoon. Worth something to somebody... but not to you.
item_1129_Description: An antique plate with careful metalwork. It's tarnished and dented, but still valuable.
item_11301_Description: Fertilizer for a mushroom box farm.
item_11302_Description: Fertilizer for a mushroom box farm.

item_1130_Description:
"I don't know who you are, but you've got a lot of nerve using my storage box without permission. I sold all your stuff."

 - Ivyn
item_1131_Description: Velkort's masterpiece. Apparently the most difficult potion he's ever concocted. It tastes like beetle urine and it smells like burnt toast, and if you shake it too hard, you might blow up the countryside. But aside from those quirks... a masterpiece.
item_1132_Description: A tarnished ancient coin. It feels heavy and strangely cold.
item_1133_Description: A tarnished ancient coin. It feels heavy and strangely cold.
item_1134_Description: A small lobe from a Brain Bug. Wait, did... did it just pulse? Did you see that? No... probably your imagination.
item_1135_Description: A brass gear the size of your hand. It has no obvious purpose.
item_1136_Description: A small statue of a goblin. It was probably originally part of a child's toy army. Looks antique.
item_1137_Description: A lump of green poisonous ooze.
item_1138_Description: The mandible of a giant insect or arthropod.
item_1139_Description: A clump of feathers from a midsized bird.

item_1140_Description: The beak of a midsized bird.

Two were banished to the mire."

When placed in a damp cave, this box can be used to grow a large crop of mushrooms.

item_1147_Description: The preserved skull of a human.
item_1148_Description: A small fleck of hardened dinosaur skin.
item_1150_Description: A snippet of a family tree. Probably valuable to somebody... but not to most.
item_1151_Description: A poem written by a loved-one for a deceased family member, following an old Human custom. Since funerals are the only time these Humans ever wrote poetry in their lives, the quality is... not good.
item_1153_Description: An enchanted block of ice that can never melt.
item_1154_Description: Stamped with the seal of an official Council representative, this certificate is redeemable for 1000 councils at the Statehelm Bank.
item_1155_Description: A palm-sized chunk of hardened dinosaur hide.
item_1156_Description: A small bundle of grass from a dimension of darkness and despair. Useful for taming nightmare animals.
item_1157_Description: A small runestone used by summoners to ensure the creatures they bring forth can't return to their native dimension. Clever animal handlers have found alternate uses for its potent magic.
item_1158_Description: These are just simple line drawings, but they were done by someone with masterful skill.
item_1159_Description: It's a small bar of pleasant-smelling soap. And so smooth! This is nice soap.
item_115_Description: Boost your maximum health by 200 for two minutes.
item_1161_Description: A small spindle of cotton string.
item_1162_Description: Made of painstakingly carved bone. It has very fine teeth for collecting insects out of hair.
item_1163_Description: These shackles would only fit small creatures, such as fey or possibly dwarves.
item_1164_Description: A small painted wooden chip, used in card and parlor games.
item_1165_Description: A small painted wooden chip, used in card and parlor games. It has tiny flecks of inlaid gemstone, making it slightly harder for rogues to slip counterfeits into a game.
item_1166_Description: A small painted wooden chip, used in card and parlor games. The paint is magical, and protects it from being replicated by illusion spells -- a common ploy amongst dwarven rogues and hustlers.
item_1167_Description: Magical runes have been etched into this claw to cause acidic explosions. Unfortunately the etchings weakened the chitin, and it's not suitable for use in constructing armor.
item_1168_Description: A big chunk of glass infused with intellect-improving magic. It could make animals smarter permanently. Or you could use it on yourself, but the effects would only last a while.
item_1169_Description: A large hook-shaped bone. Pretty sharp.
item_1171_Description: The fungal core of a myconian. It is inedible and has no obvious use, but it somehow feels useful. To somebody.
item_1172_Description: A thick greenish goo. It tastes delicious! Useful in brewing and cooking, and very occasionally in alchemy.
item_1173_Description: A fairly fragile bone that was part of some animal's rib cage.
item_1174_Description: A comb in the orcish style, made of bone and dipped in gold. Worn as jewelry.
item_1175_Description: Very old seeds that can't be planted anymore, but may have other uses due to their glowing magical nature. Perhaps druids or shamans would like them. Or seed collectors.
item_1176_Description: A petrified glass tube made when lightning strikes sand. Alchemists love these, and fletchers use them for some advanced arrows.
item_1177_Description: A sort of "cheat sheet" for ancient scribes. Perhaps it'd be useful in recreating lost Calligraphy secrets.
item_1178_Description: A bit of sea sponge that's been soaked in something weird. Smells like camphor and... something else you can't place. Supposedly these sponges absorb ambient magic and can be used in potions.
item_1179_Description: A small orcish utility knife, a little too short to be useful as a weapon. A few years ago, these were a fashionable accessory in Statehelm, but the fad has died out now.  ( Not to be confused with the orcish Khopesh, which is a very dangerous sword.)
item_117_Description: Recovers 14 health per second for 30 seconds.
item_1180_Description: This is the skull of some weird otherworldly creature. Neat.
item_1181_Description: A bit of mistletoe with a soft green glow. Probably pretty useful for druids. Exotic plant collectors would like it, too.
item_1182_Description: A hand-carved spoon made of bone. One the one hand, gross! On the other hand, it's a quality hand-made item, with a certain primitive charm. No, wait. No. It's just gross.
item_1183_Description:
This odd organic life stores solar energy in an unusual manner: it shunts it into a tiny pocket dimension. This allows the lichen to thrive in any sunlit environment, but it's most commonly found in freezing areas because there are fewer natural predators.

It is used by sages as a sort of battery for spells that require a large burst of power, and surveyors claim that the lichen can be used to find hidden caches of minerals in the area.
item_1184_Description: A small leather sack of weird powders and bones, useful for casting Orcish magics. It is worn prominently by Orcish wizards as a symbol of their power.
item_1185_Description: A conveniently-sized shovel, perfect for digging small graves. (With this in your inventory, you will have the option of burying the corpses of deceased monsters. Burying monsters will make replacement monsters appear more quickly.)
item_1186_Description: A magical scroll that lets you play a word game.
item_1187_Description: Ghost sweat, congealed into a slimy lump. Probably useful for crafting potions or something.
item_1188_Description: A rubbery eyestalk from a watcher.
item_1189_Description: The desiccated remains of a small animal that was caught and partially digested by a carnivorous plant or giant spider or something horrible like that.
item_118_Description: Recovers 20 health per second for 30 seconds.
item_1190_Description: The long tongue of a frog-like creature.
item_1191_Description: The broken-off wing of a dead fairy. Broken fairy wings are a horrific, nauseating sight to other fairies.
item_1192_Description: A lump of flesh from a troll. It automatically regenerates at the same rate that it rots.
item_1193_Description: The stinky guts of a gigantic snail.
item_1194_Description: A broken-off piece of a zombie. It stinks quite badly, but doesn't seem to be rotting too quickly...
item_1195_Description: Someone killed an ogre and took its stomach. That's not disturbing at all. Perfectly reasonable thing to do.
item_1196_Description: A small cloth satchel containing special powder that can be used to commune with the dead.
item_1197_Description: A small painted statue of an ogre. It was probably originally part of a child's toy army. Looks antique, but not very valuable.
item_1198_Description: A small painted statue of a goblin. It was probably originally part of a child's toy army. Looks antique, but not very valuable.
item_1199_Description: A small painted statue of a skeleton. It was probably originally part of a child's toy army. Looks antique, but not very valuable.
item_119_Description: This admin-created utility potion repairs all items in your inventory that are broken due to deaths while in Hardcore mode.
item_12001_Description: This unidentified pastoral seems to be in good condition.
item_12002_Description: This idyllic scene seems to have stood the passage of time quite well.
item_12003_Description: This painting depicts a long-lost monument. Rather pretty.
item_12004_Description: This nature scene was painted with vibrant colors.
item_12005_Description: There is a hint of melancholy behind this beautiful nature scene.
item_12006_Description: This pleasant abstract painting has a nice solid frame.
item_12007_Description: A nature scene with an incredible level of detail.
item_1200_Description: A small painted statue of a hag. It was probably originally part of a child's toy army. Looks antique, but not very valuable.
item_1201_Description: A small brass figurine of a hag. It was probably originally part of a child's toy army. Looks antique, and somewhat valuable.
item_1202_Description: A scale from a dragon! Hmm, it'd be a pretty small dragon... or, more likely, some dragon-kin creature.
item_12031_Description: A musty tome. Looks tedious, if you judge it by its cover.
item_12032_Description: A thin volume of poetry. No pictures at all.
item_1203_Description: A scale from a dragon! Hmm, it'd be a pretty small dragon... or, more likely, some dragon-kin creature.
item_1204_Description: A scale from a dragon! Hmm, it'd be a pretty small dragon... or, more likely, some dragon-kin creature.
item_1205_Description: A scale from a dragon! Hmm, it'd be a pretty small dragon... or, more likely, some dragon-kin creature.
item_1206_Description: It looks like a coin, and it's made of some strong metal, but it has no markings or denomination. Whatever it's for, it's very old.
item_1207_Description: This chunk of spider came off in one solid piece. It's probably useful for making armor, if you like wearing creepy ridiculous things as armor.
item_1208_Description: The end of a basilisk's tail, said to be a potent natural magic source.
item_1209_Description: The end of a manticore's tail, with the venom sack intact.
item_12101_Description: This is a novice students' attempt to mimic Landrisil's masterpiece, "The Forest Before Dusk".
item_12102_Description: The tell-tale signs of Newakis fatbrush-strokes indicate that this is a novice goblin painting, probably a teenagers' efforts.
item_12103_Description: This is the work of an elven artisan from approximately 300 years ago. It has a slight kitsch factor, and may look good on a wall, but it has little monetary value.
item_12104_Description: This nature scene depicts Traticus, the mythical elven hero, entering the Woods of Disdain. The subject matter dates the painting to the New Revisionist period, 150 years ago. By elven timelines this was very recent, making the painting extremely tacky. Perhaps it will gain value in 300 years, however.
item_12105_Description: This is clearly the work of a skilled goblin artisan. Made in the recent Three Hierarchies goblin style, it is probably very new.
item_12106_Description: A lovely forgery of "Snake Widow Lake" by Hermard Torsitan, the most esteemed living human painter.
item_12107_Description: This painting of a waterfall is bland and uninspired, but you can barely make out an older, better painting underneath. The older painting looks to have been a practice piece by a goblin expert.
item_12108_Description: A mass-produced replica of Sherig's "Ovallia". Tacky and somewhat low-quality, but it's currently popular among the nouveau riche.
item_12109_Description: This is an original numbered Ertrigard painting. The master is beloved for waterfall scenes like this one. However, thanks to his magic brush, he created over 40,000 paintings during his lifetime, so being "original" doesn't really make it too terribly special.
item_1210_Description: A saliva-covered ball of hair. The hair is unusual and perhaps valuable... it may have uses to alchemists.
item_12110_Description: The painting is a student effort by an untrained hand, but the frame is quite lovely.
item_12111_Description: This depiction of the Tower of Treason was made by an elven Phot craftsman, using their signature one-bristle brush.
item_12112_Description: A breathtaking scene of the old Council grounds of Ema. It seems to be 3000 years old. Sadly, however, there are traces of alteration magic in the paint, and it could well be a forgery.
item_12113_Description: This ugly piece has been preserved through the ages solely because it depicts Grigtekark, a goblin holy land.
item_12114_Description: A stunning find! This is a truly ugly painting, but the lack of depth and proportion suggest that it was made by someone in the pre-Council era, making it at least 10,000 years old.
item_12115_Description: A lovely scene. Perhaps it was created by Mirkym -- it has her signature approach to foliage -- but probably just created in the same style by an admirer of her work.
item_12116_Description: This piece is absolutely stunning... literally. Its magical pigments cause the viewer to very briefly lose their balance when viewing the painting. It's also a lovely painting and you're not sure why the artist bothered with the trickery.
item_12117_Description: A blurry watercolor without much to redeem it. However, it was made with highly magical elven pigments that can be reclaimed.
item_12118_Description: A gorgeous replica of Crea's "Esoteria", complete with all 88 hidden symbols. Most noticeable is the three-headed duck in one corner, representing the three states of nature.
item_12119_Description: An original work by an elven name you don't recognize, Errasteria. It's lovely.
item_1211_Description:
A small stone. It's said to be the final remains of a ghost who somehow found peace.

WARNING: Everyone knows that you should NEVER give a spirit stone to strange talking animals. In particular, you must never give, trade, or sell one to a talking fox!
item_12120_Description: As you glance into this painting, the details seem to melt away. It is a cursed forgery of Gobblenuk's Forger's Peril: the insane goblin artist concocted a magical painting that can never be replicated. Any attempt to do so results in this monstrous visual effect. On the other hand, the visual effect is pretty sweet.
item_12121_Description: It's the little-known masterpiece by dwarven artist Kot Bhenul. Note the beautiful use of real gold in the paint.
item_12122_Description: It's an extremely poor forgery of "Last Autumn Sunset" by Kot Bhenul. There isn't even any real gold in the gold paint!
item_12123_Description: A masterpiece in the vein of "Esoteria" or "The Cured Path", this symbol-laden mess of a painting contains over 400 secret meanings.
item_12124_Description: A recreation of the famous painting from the end of the Dwarven Period. The detail work really captures the ham-fisted spirit of dwarven painting techniques.
item_12125_Description: This quality replica of "Candlelight and Dawnlight" is overshadowed by its frame, a beautiful masterpiece without peer.
item_12126_Description: This scribbled mess is supposedly about pies, but it doesn't contain any recognizable shapes -- just ugly blobs. It's unsigned and unappealing.
item_12127_Description: A collection of rectangles on a blue background painted by Susarex, an elf that was turned into a snake in a tragic zoo accident. The perfectly straight rectangles took Susarex over 200 hours to complete.
item_12128_Description: Not inspired, but quite pleasant to look at, this abstract piece would look good hanging in a Council official's office. It lends a little class to any room it's in.
item_12129_Description: A signed and numbered copy of the famous painting by Go Hakomi, a dwarven master. He recently passed away, increasing this painting's value significantly.
item_1212_Description: A brain that's been preserved in some sort of alchemical soup.
item_12130_Description: You can kind of make out little orc faces and little stork bodies in this abstract painting, although you have to squint. It's bright and upbeat, but you've never heard of the painter.
item_12131_Description: A wintry woodland scene featuring a little elf girl crying. You don't recognize the artist's name, but it dates back at least 1300 years, so that's something.
item_12131_Name: "With The Wind On My Face I Can Never Smile"
item_12132_Description: A lovely beach scene depicting a gaggle of goblins crowded around a massive talking fish wearing a little hat. You recognize the subject matter: it's the goblin tale of The Bossy Fishlord. Painted by Agzak, a reasonably well-known goblin artist.
item_12133_Description: A depiction of Talerra, the spectral God of Depression and Knowing the Truth. Her ghostly arm beckons as if she has some bad news to deliver. This painting seems supernaturally resilient, and it has probably been blessed by one of Talerra's few monk-priests. However, it's way too creepy to display in a home.
item_12134_Description: A well-known painting by the dwarven artist Kot Bhenul. Signed and numbered! This is a great find.
item_12135_Description: An artist's depiction of what a fifth season might be like, if there was one between winter and spring. It's lovely, but it pretty much just looks like winter to you.
item_12136_Description: A portrait of a red-robed mage with a trained battle-rat. The mage is crying over a mug of spilled beer. There are no shields present in the picture. (Artist unknown.)
item_1214_Description: A jaggedly-carved stone block, warm to the touch. Dwarven safety runes indicate that it is explosive, poisonous, and ... there's one other danger symbol that you don't recognize.
item_1215_Description: Upon activation, this small stone explodes, typically killing the person activating it. These runes are used by paranoid merchants as anti-theft devices. Intermediate runecrafters and fire mages can also make use of them in their research.
item_1216_Description: It's stone, egg-shaped, and it came off of a gargoyle. Whether it's an actual embryonic gargoyle is not readily apparent.
item_1217_Description: A mostly-used-up candle made of lard.
item_1218_Description: THIS IS A LEGACY ITEM. Take it to the Legacy Item Helper golem in Serbule to trade it in for a current item.
item_121_Description: Dispels most curses, diseases, disfigurements, addictions, and botherments.
item_1220_Description: Salvaged from some ancient public works project, this magic nail gives extra resilience to complex carpentry items.
item_1221_Description:
This small bag of sand is somehow different from regular sand. You can't tell exactly how or why it's different, but you know it is.

Magic sand is coveted by crazy prospectors, who claim there's a way to use the sand to reveal vast mineral wealth.
item_1222_Description: What a find! This is an intact biped arm assembly: humerus, radius, ulna, even most of the phalanges!
item_1223_Description: This book threatens to crumble to dust if you so much as open the cover. It would take a master librarian to get any knowledge from this old specimen.
item_1224_Description: The weird symbols on the cover seem intriguing, but the pages are so faded and ruined that you can't make out any words. Perhaps an expert librarian could make some use of it.
item_1225_Description: A small statuette of a wizard in battle armor, his arm outstretched as he casts a spell. It was probably originally part of a child's toy set. Looks antique, but only moderately valuable.
item_1225_Name: Lead Figurine of a War Wizard
item_1226_Description: Two ordinary Council Screeds glued together by a prankster.
item_1227_Description: A small clay-fired pot. It has long cracks that make it less than ideal for holding water, but maybe somebody could patch it up or something. Just... not you.
item_1228_Description: A piece of an old clay pot. It might fetch a few coins from a vendor if you make up a history for this piece of junk.
item_1229_Description: Lac is secreted from some types of insects, most notably the lac insect. It is used in creating shellac, which is a versatile crafting resource.
item_122_Description: Dispels most curses, diseases, disfigurements, addictions, and botherments.
item_12301_Description: A musty tome of poetry from the dwarven master Gammo. It has both the original Dwarven rhyming couplets and a Human translation. Perfect for the poetry lover in your life.
item_12302_Description: The elven shock-poet Zini's famous work about how humankind might go extinct due to their prudishness, but will be saved by kind and promiscuous elves. It has a lot of depraved imagery, but also plenty of clever rhymes. A good gift for poetry lovers and those who appreciate a good porno. But not a good gift for especially uptight (or racist) Humans.
item_12303_Description: Written by an anonymous goblin poet, this melodramatic prose-poem is about worms that are used as food. It is not highly-acclaimed, but beginners to the art of poetry appreciation find it humorous.
item_12304_Description: A rollicking good tale about a bumbling oaf who finds a magic belt that makes him supernaturally strong. A book-length poem that is popular amongst commoners, but considered a bit too "low-brow" for the more discerning afficianado.
item_12305_Description: This is not a book of poetry, but a book on how to better appreciate poetry.
item_12306_Description: The well-known human philosopher Jim Hatcher tried his hand at poetry with this tacky volume. There are some hints of cleverness toward the end, for people who keep reading that long.
item_12306_Name: "Why Are There Bees" by Jim Hatcher
item_12307_Description: Written by an anonymous orc, this terse poem tells the story of the battle of Fok, where over a million orcs died. The author manages to deliver a glimpse into the horrors of war. It's said that the author was drawn and quartered for producing this work, and their works burned, but obviously some copies survived.
item_12308_Description:
The famous children's poem about Mort, the undead skeleton, who ultimately kills everyone he ever loved in life.

It's famous because the Council once declared it mandatory reading for all children at age 4. However, the sheer amount of crying and misery it caused eventually got them to relent.
item_12309_Description: The goblin poet Kaghat wrote this twee poem about the supposed "lost tribe" of goblins, noble creatures that were more in tune with nature. It's her least-popular work, but it's also so much shorter than her other works that many more copies exist.
item_1230_Description: Secreted from gigantic insects, this substance is used in making very high-quality shellac.
item_12310_Description: A late chapbook by the elven master Enst. This is quite a find! After his banishment, Enst is said to have only spoken his poems aloud, so that only the treants of the Harshland Forest know his later works. This tome has been written in a very sloppy hand... perhaps a treant wrote it?
item_12311_Description: An anonymous poem about farming. It's very, very long -- so long that it has become a cult classic in its own way, as bored elves challenge each other to memorize the entire thing. Then one partner will recite it during sex; the first person to finish loses the challenge.
item_12312_Description: A deep, difficult chapbook by the late human poet Avery Lancaster. Her imagery is hauntingly beautiful, yet profoundly stupid at the same time. That's the mark of a true master.
item_12313_Description: A hard-hitting book by the goblin poet Kaghat. It approaches the topic of slavery from every angle, and it pulls no punches. It's a very polarizing book in goblin culture. Kaghat herself claims that she was banished from her tribe because of this book, although her tribe vehemently denies this, saying that Kaghat just wandered into the desert one day and never came back.
item_12314_Description: A heavy volume of poems by the dwarven master Gammo. It even includes the poem "My Penis Is The Sunstone", which has been censored by the Dwarven Culture Group for over 500 years! This is a great find for poetry lovers, though dwarves may find it too embarrassing to own. Less prudish individuals tend to find Gammo's works uplifting and ennervating.
item_12315_Description: This is a real treasure -- an original hand-copied manuscript by Zuk-Ghu, the orcish poet of Kur! Mocked by his people, Zuk-Ghu was forced to write and distribute his poems in secrecy. They are truly beautiful, though... if you're fluent in Orc. If you only know pidgin-Orc like most people, these poems are largely inscrutable.
item_12316_Description: A long traditional poem in Ancient Elvish. Nobody speaks Ancient Elvish anymore, least of all elves, so a translation is provided. But the translation is terrible. It's a pity because Ir-Hetsu is the god of song and story, so this should have been a good read.
item_12317_Description: Another tiresome chapbook by Su-Moltik. This one is an exercise in metaphor over-extension, where the eponymous tonsils are described as being in the Throat of Belief, which is surrounded by the Neck Muscles of Close-Mindedness. It goes on like that for a very long time. Its unintentional hilarity has made it a cult classic, much to Su-Moltik's confusion and annoyance.
item_12318_Description: A relatively recent volume by the rakshasa poet Cursak. It's funny and light-hearted in the first half, then gets more and more serious. It needed a good compilation editor, in other words. But it's still fun.
item_12319_Description: Another volume by the dwarven master Gammo. Each dwarven god gets their own epic-length poem. One, the poem about Ormorek, is very good. The others are embarrassingly bad. But Gammo is long dead, so dwarven society seems to have agreed to just conveniently forget about the bad ones.
item_12320_Description: This collection of beautiful crone-language verses has been translated impeccably. A recent scandal has erupted over this book, insinuating that the powerful gorgon Rosedria didn't write it herself, but had a vast team of poet hags ghost-write it. The scandal has raised the value of this long-out-of-print book to absurd heights.
item_1236_Description: It's labeled "Evil Tasks For Today", but all the tasks involve shopping and housekeeping.
item_1237_Description: It's a blank form for requesting personal military items from the Onkara.
item_1238_Description: It's about nature. It's boring and it doesn't even rhyme.
item_1239_Description: It's a hand-scribbled note from one Rakshasa to another, inviting them to a coming-of-age party. It looks pretty old... the party probably happened long ago and this was kept as a memento.
item_12401_Description: This unidentified portrait is remarkably lifelike.
item_12402_Description: A portrait of Khyrulek's young daughter. She's a pale Human child, no more than five years old, and she looks miserable.
item_12403_Description: A thin volume of poetry by the goblin poet Gruzark. It has a homely, earthly feel. Which is poet-speak for "it sucks." It has some exciting bits, though. Better than nothing.
item_12405_Description: A pumpkin carved with a silly owl face.
item_12406_Description: A pleasant portrayal of an inn in an idyllic setting, signed by Samantha Tallow.
item_12407_Description: A pumpkin carved with a scary octopus face.
item_12408_Description: An enchanted teapot, useful for holding enchanted tea. (But this one is empty.)
item_1240_Description: Someone was practicing their calligraphy on this parchment. They're not bad.
item_12411_Description: A simple rocket that explodes in harmless light when used.
item_12412_Description: A small ball of paper that explodes into a colorful burst when lit.
item_12413_Description: A ball of paper that explodes into a colorful burst when lit.
item_12414_Description: A simple rocket that explodes in harmless light when used.
item_12415_Description: A ball of paper that explodes into a colorful burst when lit.
item_12416_Description: A simple rocket that explodes in harmless light when used.
item_12417_Description: A ball of paper that explodes into a colorful burst when lit.
item_1241_Description: Someone has meticulously drawn a geometric pattern on this scroll. It's unnaturally warm to the touch.
item_12430_Description: This barrel smells strongly of gunpowder. Light the fuse to release a swarm of confetti bomb rockets.
item_1243_Description: The broken-off tip of a fire wand. While it's now completely inert, it may still be a useful study aid for mages.
item_1244_Description: The broken-off tip of an ice wand. While it's now completely inert, it may still be a useful study aid for mages.
item_12450_Description: A thin tube of what you're pretty sure is magic. When you strike it against a hard surface it sparkles for a few seconds, and makes you feel oddly patriotic.
item_12451_Description: A thin tube of what you're pretty sure is magic. When you strike it against a hard surface it creates red sparkler for a few seconds, and makes you feel oddly patriotic.
item_12452_Description: A thin tube of what you're pretty sure is magic. When you strike it against a hard surface it creates green sparkles for a few seconds, and makes you feel oddly patriotic.
item_12453_Description: A thin tube of what you're pretty sure is magic. When you strike it against a hard surface it creates blue sparkles for a few seconds, and makes you feel oddly patriotic.
item_12454_Description: A thin tube of what you're pretty sure is magic. When you strike it against a hard surface it sparkles for a few seconds, and makes you feel oddly patriotic.
item_12455_Description: A jumbo version of a normal sparkler. When you strike it against a hard surface it sparkles for a few seconds, and makes you feel extra patriotic.
item_12456_Description:
A crate full of sparklers you can deploy for all to enjoy.

Can only be deployed once every two hours.
item_1245_Description: The broken-off tip of a lightning wand. While it's now completely inert, it may still be a useful study aid for mages.
item_1246_Description: A communication device common among Crone Hegemony officers, akin to a Sending Stone from the Council Lands... but much, much, creepier. This one seems inert.
item_1247_Description:
A lost notebook by the prolific Council scribe Notus, who spent years devising innovative ways to catalog the world's knowledge. How these notebooks came to be 'lost' is a mystery, but a persistent rumor suggests that Notus himself threw them out in the street in disgust: too many petitioners refused to do their own research despite his excellent indexes. He soon regretted his actions, but the notebooks were already gone.

Notus himself denies any such indiscretion and suggests that perhaps goblin cat-burglars stole the missing notebooks.
item_1248_Description: A bit of tentacle from a kraken.
item_1249_Description: The tail of a large rat.
item_12501_Description: A blob of dirty cotton, full of seeds. You could de-seed it by hand... but that would take forever. You could also plant the seeds in a garden.
item_12502_Description: Cotton that's been cleaned of seeds and is ready to become yarn.
item_12506_Description: High-quality cotton fibers that have been cleaned and prepared.
item_1250_Description: A broken-off part of a giant rat's tooth.
item_1252_Description: The hacked-off hoof of a pig or boar.
item_1253_Description: The bowels of a dead deer.
item_1254_Description: Part of a dead wolf's tail.
item_12551_Description: Cotton cloth that's extremely porous. Ideal for butter and certain cheeses.
item_12552_Description: Cotton cloth that's very porous. Ideal for use in creating mold cheeses.
item_12553_Description: Porous cotton that's resistant to heat. Ideal for creating hard cheeses.
item_12554_Description: Porous cotton that's resistant to heat. Ideal for creating mold cheeses that take a long time to ripen.
item_12555_Description: Porous cotton that's resistant to heat. Ideal for storing fresh bread.
item_12558_Description: A porous sticky cloth that's useful for some basic garments.
item_12559_Description: A sticky cloth that's ideal for certain types of clothing.
item_12560_Description: This strange cloth is smooth and cool to the touch.
item_12561_Description: This strange cloth is luxurious to the touch, yet incredibly strong.
item_12562_Description: This strange cloth is luxurious to the touch, yet incredibly strong.
item_12563_Description: A bit of gauze that helps keep flowers organized in a flower arrangement.
item_12564_Description: A bit of very specialized cloth that is used by expert flower arrangers.
item_12565_Description: Thin and uncomfortable, but warm enough to keep you alive.
item_12566_Description: A long piece of lovely decorative lace.
item_12570_Description: Thin yet warm. Perhaps magically so.
item_12575_Description: A thick blanket that gives extra padding to a saddle. (Can be used for warmth in an emergency.)
item_12576_Description: A thick blanket that gives extra padding to a saddle. (Can be used for warmth in an emergency.)
item_12577_Description: A thick blanket that gives extra padding to a saddle. (Can be used for warmth in an emergency.)
item_12578_Description: A ball of yarn made of an exotic material.
item_12579_Description: Harvested flax fiber. It needs to be retted before it can be turned into linen.
item_12580_Description: Rugged cloth with an innate magical insulation.
item_12581_Description: A bundle of flax, ready to be water retted. Place in deep water. Takes 30 minutes to soak.
item_12582_Description: Extra-strong cloth with innate magical properties.
item_12583_Description: Linen that has been augmented with fae power.
item_12589_Description: Flax that has been prepared for use in textiles. It can now be spun into linen.
item_1258_Description: The dismembered paw of a large bear.
item_12591_Description: A thick blanket that gives warmth and comfort to a mare during pregnancy. This blanket is suitable for mares with an Ice-Blooded temperament. Give to a stabled horse during gestation phase to accelerate gestation by 24 hours. (Can also be used for warmth in an emergency.)
item_12592_Description: A thick blanket that gives warmth and comfort to a mare during pregnancy. This blanket is suitable for mares with a Cold-Blooded temperament. Give to a stabled horse during gestation phase to accelerate gestation by 24 hours. (Can also be used for warmth in an
item_12593_Description: A thick blanket that gives warmth and comfort to a mare during pregnancy. This blanket is suitable for mares with a Cool-Blooded temperament. Give to a stabled horse during gestation phase to accelerate gestation by 24 hours. (Can also be used for warmth in an
item_12594_Description: A thick blanket that gives warmth and comfort to a mare during pregnancy. This blanket is suitable for mares with a Warm-Blooded temperament. Give to a stabled horse during gestation phase to accelerate gestation by 24 hours. (Can also be used for warmth in an
item_12595_Description: A thick blanket that gives warmth and comfort to a mare during pregnancy. This blanket is suitable for mares with a Hot-Blooded temperament. Give to a stabled horse during gestation phase to accelerate gestation by 24 hours. (Can also be used for warmth in an
item_12596_Description: A thick blanket that gives warmth and comfort to a mare during pregnancy. This blanket is suitable for mares with a Fire-Blooded temperament. Give to a stabled horse during gestation phase to accelerate gestation by 24 hours. (Can also be used for warmth in an
item_1259_Description: A hacked-out organ from a bear. Truly disgusting.
item_125_Description: It's unclear when this potion was made, but it's old. A tiny label declares it to be "the liquified essence of sharpness." It's mint flavored.
item_1260_Description: The razor-sharp talons of a dead dinosaur.
item_1261_Description: The bloody forelimb of a rabbit. It smells.
item_1263_Description: The beak of a cockatrice. Just touching it causes your hands to go numb momentarily. Creepy!
item_1264_Description: A lump of clay from a deceased golem. This clay was purified and enchanted years ago, but doesn't seem to have any magic in it now.
item_1265_Description: A mechanism used in large automatons.
item_1266_Description: Someone tried to map a cave, but seemed to get disoriented halfway through. The result is useless.
item_1267_Description: A list of gear brought on a long expedition. The paper is very old, but not antique-level old.
item_1268_Description: A patch of flesh and fur torn from a barghest.
item_1269_Description: A large feather from a hippogriff. It's very pretty.
item_1270_Description: It should be worth a few coins if you wash it up.
item_1272_Description: So-called "Dwarven Nails", these aren't useful for making houses or other sensible things. Instead, they're used in creating weird machines.
item_1273_Description: The grasping device of a deceased construct. These claws can be found in the weirdest places because they're designed to keep holding on even in death. Some people believe they're lucky charms, but otherwise they serve no obvious purpose.
item_1274_Description: Part of some self-aware gizmo. It may still be in working order.
item_1275_Description: Lightweight, but not very sturdy. It's not immediately clear what it might be good for.
item_1277_Description: A large brass hook, used in advanced ice-fishing.
item_1278_Description: A beautiful and well-made lens that makes objects seem a little bit larger. It doesn't seem very useful; maybe it's for old people.
item_1279_Description: Part of a specialized toolset for pulling the guts out of a body without making a mess. These are often carried by followers of Tast to show their contempt for death that occurs off the battlefield. (It's hard to explain the symbolism to outsiders.)
item_1280_Description: The sharp part of a spear. It's made of silver, so perhaps it was intended to be used against lycanthropes or worm-demons.
item_1282_Description: Popular with silversmiths and salad enthusiasts.
item_1283_Description: Used in the parlor game known as "featherball". This is a nice one!
item_1289_Description: Thick brass wire on a spool. It has no obvious use.
item_1290_Description: A brass... thing. It's small yet surprisingly heavy. It has no obvious use.
item_1291_Description: This brass device serves no obvious purpose. Perhaps it was part of an ancient machine.
item_1292_Description: A big spring made of silver. Wait, silver doesn't normally bend like that, does it? Weird. But useless.
item_1293_Description: A piece of enchanted cloth from a mummy.
item_1294_Description: This coin is made of Xogrite, a metal known only among the rakshasa. It is used by the city of Rahu to pay trade debts.
item_1295_Description: A lump of white poisonous ooze.
item_1296_Description: The ivory tooth of a hippopotamus.
item_1297_Description: The sharp bit of a scray's tail.
item_1298_Description: Well... it hasn't hatched... yet... hopefully it won't!
item_1299_Description: The weird water-and-air-processing organ of a scray.
item_129_Description: Recovers 300 health immediately, plus 20 health per second for 30 seconds.
item_13001_Description: A basic saw for cutting wood.
item_13003_Description: A handful of long wood nails.
item_13004_Description: A saw made of ice! It won't last too long, but it'll cut wood while it lasts.
item_1300_Description: It's been hacked off of a ranalon guardian. Grisly and disturbing, especially to ranalon.
item_1301_Description: A heavy container that's been magically sculpted from stone. It's far too cumbersome to be practical, but it makes a nice curio.
item_1302_Description: A decorative ranalan bracelet made of green glass beads.
item_1303_Description: It's a chunk of glass. Pretty much worthless, although a few Alchemy recipes require bits of glass.
item_1304_Description: A decorative ranalan bracelet made of purple glass beads.
item_1305_Description: A decorative ranalan bracelet made of incredibly beautiful pearls.
item_1306_Description: A chunk of a dead scray. It still glows faintly.
item_1307_Description: It's a chunk of glass. Pretty much worthless, although a few Alchemy recipes require bits of glass.
item_1308_Description: It's a chunk of glass. Pretty much worthless, although a few Alchemy recipes require bits of glass.
item_1309_Description: It's a chunk of glass. Pretty much worthless, although a few Alchemy recipes require bits of glass.
item_13101_Description: Useful generic wood! Good for cooking, building, whittling, and who knows what else. Wow! What a find!
item_13102_Description: Useful generic wood! Good for cooking, building, whittling, and who knows what else. Wow! What a find!
item_13103_Description: Useful generic wood! Good for cooking, building, whittling, and who knows what else. Wow! What a find!
item_13104_Description: Useful generic wood! Good for cooking, building, whittling, and who knows what else. Wow! What a find!
item_13105_Description: Useful generic wood! Good for cooking, building, whittling, and who knows what else. Wow! What a find!
item_1310_Description: It's a chunk of glass. Pretty much worthless, although a few Alchemy recipes require bits of glass.
item_1311_Description: It's a chunk of glass. Pretty much worthless, although a few Alchemy recipes require bits of glass.
item_1312_Description: It's a chunk of glass. Pretty much worthless, although a few Alchemy recipes require bits of glass.
item_1313_Description: Water made from freshly-melted snow, plus various impurities.
item_13151_Description: This piece of oak is an exemplar of its type! There are a few uses for this type of lumber, but often there is no need for such high quality. In that case, you can turn this into several pieces of typical-quality oak wood.
item_13152_Description: This piece of maple is an exemplar of its type! There are a few uses for this type of lumber, but often there is no need for such high quality. In that case, you can turn this into several pieces of typical-quality maple wood.
item_13153_Description: This piece of cedar is an exemplar of its type! There are a few uses for this type of lumber, but often there is no need for such high quality. In that case, you can turn this into several pieces of typical-quality cedar wood.
item_13154_Description: This piece of spruce is an exemplar of its type! There are a few uses for this type of lumber, but often there is no need for such high quality. In that case, you can turn this into several pieces of typical-quality spruce wood.
item_13155_Description: This piece of yew is an exemplar of its type! There are a few uses for this type of lumber, but often there is no need for such high quality. In that case, you can turn this into several pieces of typical-quality yew wood.
item_1317_Description: A leafy branch from a treant. An expert carpenter could easily prepare it for crafting.
item_1318_Description: At first glance it seems to be an acorn dipped in gold, but it's actually an acorn carved from solid gold. Pretty.
item_1319_Description: A still-alive branch covered in still-alive treant leaves. The leaves seem... annoyed? Perhaps this should be given to a druid leader.
item_13201_Description: Oak chips for smoking fish and meat.
item_13202_Description: Maple chips for smoking fish and meat.
item_13203_Description: Cedar chips for smoking fish and meat.
item_13204_Description: Spruce chips for smoking fish and meat.
item_13205_Description: Yew chips for smoking fish and meat.
item_1320_Description: These bone dice have been carefully weighted and lovingly polished.
item_1321_Description: Carried by ratkin as good-luck talismans.
item_1322_Description: It looks like lumps of dried curd on a string! Wait... it's actually metal shaped like cheese curds. Ratkin culture is weird.
item_1323_Description: A pile of femurs that have been positioned in a very specific way. What's it for? Eh, who cares... probably not important.
item_1324_Description: Part of an undead skeleton, this bone became so clogged with hatred that it glows with malice! Pretty neat.
item_13251_Description: A framework for building a mushroom grow-box.
item_13252_Description: A framework for building a mushroom grow-box.
item_13253_Description: A framework for building a mushroom grow-box.
item_13254_Description: A framework for building a mushroom grow-box.
item_1326_Description: A percussion instrument. Unfortunately it seems this one's been tuned to a hypersonic audio range... it doesn't sound pleasant to you!
item_1327_Description:
Ratkin have used this to smoke rattus root and tobacco.

(It's a pipe made out of pipes, but note that its name isn't a pun as far as ratkin are concerned, because those two kinds of pipes have completely unrelated words in both Goblinese and Rattic.)
item_13281_Description: A simple wooden mesh, useful for cages, pens, trellises, and so on.
item_13282_Description: A well-made wooden mesh, useful for cages, pens, trellises, and so on.
item_1328_Description: The antler-like growth from the head of a pixie.
item_1329_Description: This byproduct of pixie decomposition is prized for its magical properties.
item_13301_Description: A disposable stool for meditating. Use it near a Meditation Pillar to boost the duration of your meditation effects x2.
item_13302_Description: A disposable stool for meditating. Use it near a Meditation Pillar to boost the duration of your meditation effects x3.
item_13303_Description: A disposable stool for meditating. Use it near a Meditation Pillar to boost the duration of your meditation effects x4.
item_13304_Description: A disposable stool for meditating. Use it near a Meditation Pillar to boost the duration of your meditation effects x5.
item_13305_Description: A disposable stool for meditating. Use it near a Meditation Pillar to boost the duration of your meditation effects x6.
item_13306_Description: A disposable stool for meditating. Use it near a Meditation Pillar to boost the duration of your meditation effects x7.
item_13307_Description: A disposable stool for meditating. Use it near a Meditation Pillar to boost the duration of your meditation effects x8.
item_13308_Description: A disposable stool for meditating. Use it near a Meditation Pillar to boost the duration of your meditation effects x9.
item_13309_Description: A disposable stool for meditating. Use it near a Meditation Pillar to boost the duration of your meditation effects x10.
item_1330_Description: The wing of a giant bee or wasp.
item_13310_Description: A disposable stool for meditating. Use it near a Meditation Pillar to boost the duration of your meditation effects x11.
item_13311_Description: A disposable stool for meditating. Use it near a Meditation Pillar to boost the duration of your meditation effects x12.
item_1331_Description: The poison-injecting apparatus of a giant bee.
item_1332_Description: The wings of a dead shimmerwing butterfly.
item_13331_Description: A disposable bench for performing calligraphy. Deploy it to boost your Calligraphy durations x2.
item_13332_Description: A disposable bench for performing calligraphy. Deploy it to boost your Calligraphy durations x3.
item_13333_Description: A disposable bench for performing calligraphy. Deploy it to boost your Calligraphy durations x4.
item_13334_Description: A disposable bench for performing calligraphy. Deploy it to boost your Calligraphy durations x5.
item_13335_Description: A disposable bench for performing calligraphy. Deploy it to boost your Calligraphy durations x6.
item_13336_Description: A disposable bench for performing calligraphy. Deploy it to boost your Calligraphy durations x7.
item_13337_Description: A disposable bench for performing calligraphy. Deploy it to boost your Calligraphy durations x8.
item_13338_Description: A disposable bench for performing calligraphy. Deploy it to boost your Calligraphy durations x9.
item_13339_Description: A disposable bench for performing calligraphy. Deploy it to boost your Calligraphy durations x10.
item_1333_Description: The wings of a dead crimsonfelt butterfly.
item_13340_Description: A disposable bench for performing calligraphy. Deploy it to boost your Calligraphy durations x11.
item_13341_Description: A disposable bench for performing calligraphy. Deploy it to boost your Calligraphy durations x12.
item_1334_Description: The wings of a dead icebreeze moth.
item_1335_Description: A small piece of honeycomb from a fae beehive.
item_13361_Description: A somewhat-comfortable chair. Relaxing in it boosts your Armor Regeneration +3 for 30 minutes.
item_13362_Description: A somewhat-comfortable chair. Relaxing in it boosts your Armor Regeneration +6 for 30 minutes.
item_13363_Description: A somewhat-comfortable chair. Relaxing in it boosts your Armor Regeneration +9 for 30 minutes.
item_13364_Description: A somewhat-comfortable chair. Relaxing in it boosts your Armor Regeneration +12 for 30 minutes.
item_13365_Description: A comfortable chair. Relaxing in it boosts your Armor Regeneration +15 for 30 minutes.
item_13366_Description: A comfortable chair. Relaxing in it boosts your Armor Regeneration +18 for 30 minutes.
item_13367_Description: A comfortable chair. Relaxing in it boosts your Armor Regeneration +21 for 30 minutes.
item_13368_Description: A comfortable chair. Relaxing in it boosts your Armor Regeneration +24 for 30 minutes.
item_13369_Description: A comfortable chair. Relaxing in it boosts your Armor Regeneration +27 for 30 minutes.
item_1336_Description: A large chunk of honeycomb from a fae beehive.
item_13370_Description: A comfortable chair. Relaxing in it boosts your Armor Regeneration +30 for 30 minutes.
item_13371_Description: A comfortable chair. Relaxing in it boosts your Armor Regeneration +33 for 30 minutes.
item_1337_Description: Fae Bee royal jelly -- a potent magical substance used as a currency by many parts of the Fae Realm.
item_1338_Description: The giant stabbing part of a giant stabbing wasp.
item_13401_Description: A collection of 1000 dowels that are the right length for becoming arrows, if a fletcher prepared them.
item_13402_Description: A collection of 1000 dowels that are the right length for becoming arrows, if a fletcher prepared them.
item_13403_Description: A collection of 1000 dowels that are the right length for becoming arrows, if a fletcher prepared them.
item_13404_Description: Holds 1000 dowels. Suitable for use by fletchers. The box can only be used once.
item_13405_Description: A collection of 1000 dowels that are the right length for becoming arrows, if a fletcher prepared them.
item_13406_Description: A collection of 1000 dowels that are the right length for becoming arrows, if a fletcher prepared them.
item_1341_Description: Quite warm, and quite bright: whatever's inside is alive!
item_1342_Description: Some extremely dedicated Winter Court fae replace their biological heart with one of these. Presumably it makes them more powerful...
item_13431_Description: This small fire is warm enough to keep you from freezing to death, but not good enough for much else. Lasts 60 seconds once deployed.
item_13432_Description: A fire pit, good for cooking in the field or for keeping warm in the cold. Lasts 3 minutes once deployed.
item_13436_Description: A robotic fire pit, which uses advanced automaton techniques to spread the warmth further and keep it going longer. Lasts 10 minutes once deployed.
item_13437_Description:
A robotic fire pit, overloaded with a powerful source of energy. Lasts 10 minutes once deployed.

While standing near the deployed Dronefire, you receive the following:
+10% Cold Resistance
+10% Direct Fire Damage
+20% Fire Vulnerability
item_1343_Description: An animal has left these bones behind after eating a meal.
item_13452_Description: A small box for storing up to 4 items. Lasts for 5 minutes once deployed. (Each player that access the storage crate can store their own items. All storage crates allow access to the same items for that player.)
item_13455_Description: A box for storing up to 8 items. Lasts for 5 minutes once deployed. (Each player that access the storage crate can store their own items. All storage crates allow access to the same items for that player.)
item_13458_Description: A box for storing up to 12 items. Lasts for 5 minutes once deployed. (Each player that access the storage crate can store their own items. All storage crates allow access to the same items for that player.)
item_1345_Description: The shell of a small sea snail.
item_13461_Description: A box for storing up to 16 items. Lasts for 5 minutes once deployed. (Each player that access the storage crate can store their own items. All storage crates allow access to the same items for that player.)
item_13463_Description: A box for storing up to 20 items. Lasts for 5 minutes once deployed. (Each player that access the storage crate can store their own items. All storage crates allow access to the same items for that player.)
item_13464_Description: A box for storing up to 24 items. Lasts for 5 minutes once deployed. (Each player that access the storage crate can store their own items. All storage crates allow access to the same items for that player.)
item_13465_Description: A box for storing up to 28 items. Lasts for 5 minutes once deployed. (Each player that access the storage crate can store their own items. All storage crates allow access to the same items for that player.)
item_13466_Description: A box for storing up to 32 items. Lasts for 5 minutes once deployed. (Each player that access the storage crate can store their own items. All storage crates allow access to the same items for that player.)
item_1346_Description: The shell of a departed bivalve. It's pretty.
item_1347_Description: The shell of a deceased snail. It's pretty.
item_13481_Description: A small cask used in dairy and beer production.
item_13482_Description: A mid-sized cask used for food and drink production.
item_13483_Description: A large cask used for storing wet and dry goods.
item_13484_Description: A large cask used for aging wine.
item_1348_Description: The shell of a deceased snail. This is an especially large and pretty shell.
item_13501_Description: An elaborate contraption that can dispense free items. This model can dispense up to 10 of a single kind of item.
item_13502_Description: An elaborate contraption that can dispense free items. This model can dispense up to 1000 of a single kind of item.
item_13503_Description: An elaborate contraption that can dispense free items. This model can dispense up to 1000 of a single kind of item and has a place for a custom message that all visitors will see.
item_1350_Description: It's too waterlogged and slimy to be salvaged.
item_13513_Description: An elaborate contraption that can dispense items and collect currency. This model can sell up to 10 of a single kind of item.
item_13514_Description: An elaborate contraption that can dispense items and collect currency. This model can sell up to 1000 of a single kind of item.
item_13515_Description: An elaborate contraption that can dispense items and collect currency. This model can sell up to 1000 of a single kind of item and has a place for a custom message that all users will see.
item_13516_Description: For Guide use only! This is a Fancy Item Vendor that only dispenses to Demo players.
item_1351_Description: A handful of fragmented animal shells.
item_13521_Description:
An elaborate contraption that can dispense free raffle tickets and then deliver a reward to the raffle winner.

This model can dispense up to 10 free raffle tickets for a single item (or item stack).
item_13522_Description:
An elaborate contraption that can dispense free raffle tickets and then deliver a reward to the raffle winner.

This model can dispense up to 20 free raffle tickets for 1 or 2 items (or item stacks) and/or a small cash prize.
item_13523_Description:
An elaborate contraption that can dispense raffle tickets and then deliver a reward to the raffle winner.

This model can dispense up to 30 raffle tickets for up to 3 items (or item stacks) and/or a cash prize. The raffle tickets can be free or can cost Councils.
item_13524_Description:
An elaborate contraption that can dispense raffle tickets and then deliver a reward to the raffle winner.

This model can dispense up to 50 raffle tickets for up to 6 items (or item stacks) and/or a cash prize. The raffle tickets can be free or can cost Councils. This model has a place for an inscription that is displayed to each user.
item_13525_Description:
An elaborate contraption that can dispense raffle tickets and then deliver a reward to the raffle winner.

This model can dispense up to 100 raffle tickets for up to 8 items (or item stacks) and/or a cash prize. The raffle tickets can be free or can cost Councils. This model has a place for an inscription that is displayed to each user, and a message that is shown only to the winner. This model can also be configured to have a time limit of 1-120 minutes.
item_13526_Description:
An elaborate contraption that can dispense raffle tickets and then deliver a reward to the raffle winner.

This model can dispense up to 1000 raffle tickets for up to 10 items (or item stacks) and/or a cash prize. The raffle tickets can be free or can cost Councils. This model has a place for an inscription that is displayed to each user, and a message that is shown only to the winner. This model can also be configured to have a time limit of 1-120 minutes.
item_13527_Description: For Guide use only! This is an Ultra Item Raffler that only allows Demo players to take tickets.
item_13528_Description: A raffler with all standard options, plus the ability to attune the items to the winner. For admin use.
item_13541_Description: A simple spring-activated trap that will stun whatever walks over it. One-use.
item_1354_Description: The shell of a small sea snail. Some say this species comes from the fae realm, but it doesn't seem particularly unusual.
item_1355_Description: A particularly flat species of sea urchin. These shells are used as currency by the Ranalon.
item_13561_Description: A very basic animal cage designed for transferring pets, intended for extremely short-term use. This one-use container can hold any captive-bred (not wild-caught) Pet-Quality (non-breeding) animal for up to 48 hours. If the animal is not rehomed in a stable by then, it will escape and be lost forever. [You can use this item on a stabled pet by visiting a stablemaster. Look for the "Use Item..." button on the stable screen.]
item_13562_Description: Designed for longer transit periods, this travel cage provides basic sustenance and comfort for up to a week. This one-use container can hold any captive-bred (not wild-caught) Pet-Quality (non-breeding) animal for up to 7 days (168 hours). If the animal is not rehomed in a stable by then, it will escape and be lost forever. [You can use this item on a stabled pet by visiting a stablemaster. Look for the "Use Item..." button on the stable screen.]
item_13571_Description: A longer-term storage solution for pets. The built-in entertainment options raise the contained pet's Happiness by 10. This one-use container can hold any captive-bred (not wild-caught) Pet-Quality (non-breeding) animal for up to 14 days. If the animal is not rehomed in a stable by then, it will escape and be lost forever. [You can use this item on a stabled pet by visiting a stablemaster. Look for the "Use Item..." button on the stable screen.]
item_13572_Description: A long-term storage solution for pets. The built-in entertainment options raise the contained pets' Happiness by 20. This one-use container can hold any captive-bred (not wild-caught) Pet-Quality (non-breeding) animal for up to 28 days. If the animal is not rehomed in a stable by then, it will escape and be lost forever. [You can use this item on a stabled pet by visiting a stablemaster. Look for the "Use Item..." button on the stable screen.]
item_13573_Description: A magical freezing system for long-term pet storage. The pet is placed into suspended animation, unable to move, but still able to dream -- and to have nightmares, which cause the pet to lose all of its Happiness. This one-use container can hold any captive-bred (not wild-caught) Pet-Quality (non-breeding) animal for up to 90 days. Pets kept in this container for more than 90 days escape and are lost. [You can use this item on a stabled pet by visiting a stablemaster. Look for the "Use Item..." button on the stable screen.]
item_1358_Description: A lovely conch that has proven to be resilient and powerful. It's amazing for making magic weapons with!
item_13591_Description: A sack filled with strange substances that stay warm for a long time. (To use, give to a mother pet bee or wasp that is still guarding her larva in the 'gestation' stage. It will speed up incubation by 12 hours.)
item_13592_Description: A sack filled with strange substances that stay warm for a long time and smell reassuring to insects. (To use, give to a mother pet bee or wasp that is still guarding her larva in the 'gestation' stage. It will speed up incubation by 24 hours.)
item_1359_Description: A lovely conch that has proven to be resilient and powerful. It's perfect for making magic weapons with!
item_1360_Description: A tool used aboard Fae ships during the repair of hull damage. It's quite heavy!
item_1361_Description: This lightweight piece of... metal? Is used for... something? Anyway, it's green.
item_1362_Description: A few drops of residue from some type of cold elemental.
item_1363_Description: A crystallized honeycomb containing an audio message. Only fae seem to be able to access these documents, but in this case it's clearly humdrum day-to-day paperwork.
item_1364_Description: A crystallized honeycomb containing an audio message. Only fae seem to be able to access these documents, but in this case it's clearly humdrum day-to-day paperwork.
item_1365_Description: A crystallized honeycomb containing an audio message. Only fae seem to be able to access these documents, but in this case it's clearly humdrum day-to-day paperwork.
item_1366_Description: A small lump of gold with arrows carved into it. It seems to be a pawn for some sort of game.
item_1367_Description: A small shiny green rock with arrows carved into it. It seems to be a pawn for some sort of game.
item_1368_Description: It's a sealed pouch with a mineral inside.
item_1369_Description: It's a sealed pouch with something inside.
item_13703_Name: Wooden Figurine of a Black Bear
item_13705_Name: Wooden Figurine of a Barn Owl
item_13709_Name: Wooden Figurine of a Cave Bear
item_13711_Name: Wooden Figurine of a Horned Owl
item_13717_Name: Wooden Figurine of a Great Rhino
item_13723_Name: Wooden Figurine of a Grizzly Bear
item_13725_Name: Wooden Figurine of a Great Owl
item_13728_Name: Wooden Figurine of a Shaggy Camel
item_1374_Description: A small bit of xogrite, used as currency in distant rakshasa outposts. The process of making this alloy is a closely-guarded secret, but one of its ingredients seems to be xedrite.
item_1375_Description: The giant glowy sack from a dead tentakiller.
item_1376_Description: A giant rubbery tentacle, not unlike an octopus tentacle (if the octopus was nightmarishly large).
item_1377_Description: A slimy three-pronged tongue from a kuvou corpse.
item_1378_Description: A strange organ cut out of a kuvou corpse.
item_1379_Description: The sharp, bony appendage of a hexapus.
item_1380_Description: One of the rings of sharp teeth found in the "mouth" of a hexapus.
item_1381_Description: A decent-sized frog. It's still alive, for now. It is comfortable being held and may have been someone's pet.
item_1382_Description: Used by orcs to smoke raw (unrefined) gur-horta.
item_1383_Description: This large net has seen heavy use, and now needs significant repair... or, more likely, replacement.
item_1384_Description: This high-quality fishing hook was made by an expert blacksmith.
item_1385_Description: This strain of gur-horta is relatively weak, making it a poor choice for refining into combat potions. However, it's popular among orcs that smoke the leaves recreationally due to its mellow taste and low side-effects.
item_1386_Description: Parts of a broken sword blade. Looks like it was once an orcish khopesh, but it's not repairable.
item_1387_Description: Smithed from iron, these washers are used in making and maintaining orcish traps.
item_1388_Description: Carefully tailored to the eyes of a ratkin, these are worn when a ratkin must go outside during bright sun. The lenses are made of blue glass.
item_1389_Description: A huge, pointy, surprisingly old-looking bone.
item_1390_Description: The bones of an extinct creature's tail.
item_1391_Description: Part of an extinct species of great cat.
item_1392_Description: The slimy, sticky skin of a toxic frog. DO NOT LICK!
item_1393_Description: This handful of dirt is weird and smelly and full of fungus.
item_1394_Description: Most are nondescript, but a few have streaks of quartz.
item_1395_Description: Powdered bone prepared via a secret necromantic ritual. This is used by necromancers to summon undead skeletons.
item_1397_Description: The hacked-off claw of an aktaari. This is a grim trophy that is upsetting for aktaari to see.
item_1398_Description: The bile gland of an aktaari. This horrific trophy Is upsetting for aktaari to see.
item_14001_Description: A simple wooden key with a faint green magical glow.
item_14002_Description: A key cut from magical ice. It won't melt until it is used in whatever strange door it's designed for.
item_14003_Description:
A brief note written in a lovely flowing script. It says,

"Do you remember when your helm was shattered? Probably not. You gave it to me to repair, but it was beyond my skill. I took it to Haetrix, who used inappropriately-strong magic, and now its reality is damaged. Anyway, if your helm disappears in a puff of logic, don't fret. I've locked it in a box for you near the Sun Vale mouth. Gut a troll to light the lamp light, rip the wings off a deviant fae for the second, and rip out the stomach of your least favorite ogre for the third.

- Trallix"
item_14004_Description: A key cut from magical ice.
item_14005_Description: A key formed of dirt, held in place via magic.
item_14006_Description: It's a piece of lacquered wood shaped like a long worm with a fanged mouth. Pretty impressive, really.
item_14007_Description: It's a small parchment with a magical inscription. You can use this to get past a huge icy barrier beneath the ruined tower in Kur.
item_14008_Description: A small metal key you found in a cave in Kur Mountains.
item_14009_Description: A metal key from Gravamux, a ghoul.
item_1400_Description: The dismembered claw of a demonic beast. Dangerous to the touch.
item_14010_Description: A crystal used to unlock certain Crone Hegemony armory chests. This one is partially digested, but should still work.
item_14011_Description: An ancient stone key, worn by yetis as a good luck token.
item_14012_Description:
A note in a jaggy, hurried handwriting. It says,

"Test is still underway. I have passed the Combat and Teleportation trials, but I can't figure out how to get into the locked chest with the altars around it. I used Divination, but the only answer I got was 'SEWN'.

I still have my secret weapon: a Potion of Brilliance. Will drink it if necessary, but leaving it here for now."
item_14013_Description:
A metal key with a note attached. In flowery script it says,

"My dear Lawyr, I knew you could do it! Meet me on the mainland and I'll help you recover your destiny. - Yurra"
item_14015_Description: It's a parchment with strange symbols on it. Perhaps someone could explain its meaning to you.
item_14016_Description: A drop of blood that's been affected by the fae realm. Winterprize crystals are known to be sensitive to this sort of blood. Carrying it in large quantities is unwise, however, as it can corrupt the bearer. (You cannot carry more than 6 at once.)
item_14017_Description: Lets you leap high and control your fall. You also take no damage from falls, no matter how high. On the down side, you'll be too disoriented to effectively fight, and your attacks will deal very little damage.
item_14018_Description: Lets you fly for 5 minutes (press the Move Up key to begin). Also grants immunity to falling damage. Only usable outdoors; entering a dungeon will cause the effect to immediately end.
item_14019_Description: Lets you sprint very, very fast. (Hold down shift key to switch between sprinting and walking.) You also take no damage from falls. Lasts 2 minutes.
item_1401_Description: One of the spikes from the back of a demonic Osslar.
item_14020_Description: A bulky wooden cup full of greenish energy. You can sip this in your dying moments in order to come back to life. Only druids can consume this energy.
item_14021_Description: A bone key that fits in any of the special event loot chests in Serbule. (Look behind the inn.)
item_14022_Description: A bone key that fits in the special chest in Serbule for level 15-20 loot. (Look behind the inn.)
item_14023_Description: A bone key that fits in the special chest in Serbule for level 30 loot. (Look behind the inn.)
item_14024_Description: A bone key that fits in the special chest in Serbule for level 40 loot. (Look behind the inn.)
item_14025_Description: A bone key that fits in the special chest in Serbule for level 50 loot. (Look behind the inn.)
item_14026_Description: A bone key that fits in the special chest in Serbule for level 60 loot. (Look behind the inn.)
item_14027_Description: A transferrable day-pass to visit the parts of Rahu that are usually off-limits to tourists. While this permit lasts, the citizens of Rahu will not attack you and shopkeepers will generally be willing to deal with you. The pass becomes invalidated if you attack any Rahu citizen, and the plateau where the Onkara train is still off-limits.
item_14028_Description: A thin blanket embroidered with a depiction of a beaming sun. This style of blanket is often given out by followers of Arisetsu, the god of warmth and hope.
item_14029_Description: A rotten urak egg. Completely valueless.
item_1402_Description: The proboscis-like organ of a grickling. Revolting.
item_14030_Description: A Gazluk officer's key to one of the armory storage chests.
item_14031_Description: The key to a goblin hoard chest, typically one of several keys needed to unlock it.
item_14032_Description: The key to a goblin hoard chest, typically one of several keys needed to unlock it.
item_14033_Description: The key to a goblin hoard chest, typically one of several keys needed to unlock it.
item_14034_Description:
A handwritten note. It says,

"If you're reading this then I didn't make it out alive. Please help my friends, they're still trapped in this horrible cave. Look for the ever-burn campfire. We're camped around it because it keeps the spiders away somewhat.

We came for spider eggs, and the spiders seemed weak enough. But as we climbed higher into the caves to reach the egg-chambers, we found MUCH harder ones. Soum died when a spider exploded out of him... we all freaked out and ran... now we can't escape because of injuries. Help, please! And if you're here for spider eggs, turn back now. - Cole Fensmith"
item_14035_Description: A bone key that fits in the special chest in Serbule for level 70 loot. (Look behind the inn.)
item_14036_Description: This series of books by the goblin sage Muligu details each god that might be able to help you with a knowledge-related question.
item_14037_Description: This series of books by the goblin sage Muligu details each god that might be able to help you with a knowledge-related question.
item_14038_Description: This series of books by the goblin sage Muligu details each god that might be able to help you with a knowledge-related question.
item_14039_Description: This series of books by the goblin sage Muligu details each god that might be able to help you with a knowledge-related question.
item_1403_Description: A glowing eye from a grickling.
item_14040_Description: The work of a master stoneshaper, this key is a miniature work of art. It may unlock something somewhere, but even if not, it's an interesting curio.
item_14043_Description: This ticket entitles the bearer to a free game of Monsters and Mantids at any table in the Red Wing Casino.
item_14044_Description: This ticket entitles the bearer to a free game of Lootmaster Match-3 at the Red Wing Casino.
item_14045_Description: This ticket entitles the bearer to a free game of Cashfall Match-3 at the Red Wing Casino.
item_14046_Description: A small runestone that's worth a small fortune at the Red Wing Casino. See Qatik to trade this for 5,000 Councils.
item_14047_Description: A small runestone that's worth a small fortune at the Red Wing Casino. See Qatik to trade this for 7,000 Councils.
item_14048_Description: A small runestone that's worth a small fortune at the Red Wing Casino. See Qatik to trade this for 10,000 Councils.
