item_18185_Description: This piece of warm, pulsing crystal has been coated in some sort of powder, making it safe to touch.
item_18186_Description: A warm crystal that seems to constantly grow and shrink.
item_18187_Description: It's technically a crystal, but it'd be impossible to say precisely what it's made of. It's more of a... liquid crystal? It can be squeezed and twisted into odd shapes. Maybe it'd make a fun children's toy.
item_18201_Description: A pretty gem with dark green bands, it's well known for aiding magical communication. This particular gem has been infused with a rune that attunes it to cattle when used in crafting.
item_18202_Description: A pretty gem with dark green bands, it's well known for aiding magical communication. This particular gem has been infused with a rune that attunes it to the needs of deer when used in crafting.
item_18203_Description: A pretty gem with dark green bands, it's well known for aiding magical communication. This particular gem has been infused with a rune that attunes it to pigs when used in crafting.
item_18204_Description: A pretty gem with dark green bands, it's well known for aiding magical communication. This particular gem has been infused with a rune that attunes it to spiders when used in crafting.
item_18205_Description: A dark red stone, known for its curative properties. This particular stone has been attuned to the First Aid skill if used in crafting.
item_18206_Description: A small crystal made up of black and white bands. Used in magic, it guards against physical dangers. This particular onyx has been attuned to Armor Patching when used in crafting.
item_18207_Description: A pretty gem with dark green bands, it's well known for aiding magical communication. This particular gem has been infused with a rune that attunes it to bats when used in crafting.
item_18208_Description: A pretty gem with dark green bands, it's well known for aiding magical communication. This particular gem has been infused with a rune that attunes it to rabbits when used in crafting.
item_18209_Description: A small yellow topaz. An incredibly versatile magical stone. This particular piece has been infused with a rune that attunes it to Fairy Magic, giving crafted items a chance to include one or more Fairy Magic powers (with the rest being Mentalism powers).
item_18210_Description: A soft blue rock. It's useful in curatives and curse removal potions. This particular gem has been infused with a rune that attunes it to foxes when used in crafting.
item_18301_Description: This small gem is brownish orange. It has been tuned as an amplifier for certain tailoring recipes.
item_18302_Description: A small crystal made up of black and white bands. This particular gem has been tuned to act as an amplifier for certain leatherworking recipes.
item_18303_Description: A small black stone. It has been tuned as an amplifier for certain toolcrafting recipes.
item_18304_Description: A blue-green stone. It has been tuned as an amplifier for certain armorsmithing recipes.
item_18305_Description: A beautiful green crystal. It has been tuned as an amplifier for certain weapon-crafting recipes.
item_18306_Description: A small orange gem with many magical uses. It has been tuned as an amplifier for certain glass equipment.
item_18311_Description: This huge gem is brownish orange. It has been tuned as an amplifier for certain tailoring recipes.
item_18312_Description: A massive crystal made up of black and white bands. This particular gem has been tuned to act as an amplifier for certain leatherworking recipes.
item_18313_Description: A big black stone. It has been tuned as an amplifier for certain toolcrafting recipes.
item_18314_Description: A big blue-green stone. It has been tuned as an amplifier for certain armorsmithing recipes.
item_18315_Description: A large green crystal. It has been tuned to empower certain weapon-crafting recipes.
item_18316_Description: A large orange gem with many magical uses. It has been tuned to empower certain glass equipment recipes.
item_18321_Description:
This large piece of fluorite is blue with white bands. Used in magic, it helps you communicate with animals. It also focuses the mind inwards.

Associated Primary Skill: Animal Handling
Associated Secondary Skill: Meditation
item_18322_Description:
It's a huge peridot, mint green in color. Used in magic, it wards off distracting thoughts and helps align your accuracy.

Associated Primary Skill: Archery
Associated Secondary Skill: Calligraphy
item_18323_Description:
A chunky crystal made up of black and white bands. Used in magic, it guards against physical dangers.

Associated Primary Skill: Shield
Associated Secondary Skills: Armor Patching, Tanning, Leatherworking
item_18324_Description:
This large gem is brownish orange. Used in magic, it provides extra courage or energy to someone under great stress.

Associated Primary Skill: Warden
Associated Secondary Skill: Tailoring
item_18325_Description:
A large orange gem with many magical uses. Humans appreciate it for its ability to reduce anxiety.

Associated Primary Skill: Battle Chemistry
item_18326_Description:
A huge gem with dark green bands, it's well known for aiding magical communication.

Associated Primary Skills: Cow, Pig, Deer, Spider, Giant Bat, etc.
item_18327_Description:
A huge piece of white quartz. High-quality pieces like this one can amplify elemental magic.

Associated Primary Skill: Fire Magic
item_18328_Description:
A big dark red stone, known for its curative properties.

Associated Primary Skill: Druid

Associated Secondary Skill: First Aid
item_18329_Description:
A huge gem, light blue-green in color. It's a traditional protective stone for fishermen.

Associated Primary Skill: Fishing
Associated Secondary Skill: Teleportation
item_18330_Description:
A small black stone. Knives that incorporate obsidian can be enchanted more thoroughly than regular blades.

Associated Primary Skill: Knives
Associated Secondary Skill: Toolcrafting
item_18331_Description:
A white stone with dark green veins, the moss agate is also known as the Gardener's Talisman.

Associated Primary Skill: Hammer

Associated Secondary Skills: Gardening, Mycology
item_18332_Description:
A massive yellow topaz. An incredibly versatile magical stone.

Associated Primary Skill: Mentalism
item_18333_Description:
A purple stone, used in magic to facilitate travel.

Associated Primary Skill: Teleportation
item_18334_Description:
A big dark blue stone, with limited but potent magic uses.

Associated Primary Skill: Staff
item_18335_Description:
A honkin' big red gem. Used in magic, it balances the senses and sharpens the mind to imminent danger.

Associated Primary Skill: Sword
item_18336_Description:
This big banded gem is used in magic to promote grace and give power to the body.

Associated Primary Skill: Unarmed
item_18337_Description:
This megagem is dark green with red spots. In magic it is used to command nature.

Associated Primary Skill: Weather Witching
item_18338_Description:
Milky white, this big crystal is useful in magic that relates to 'new beginnings.'

Associated Primary Skill: Lycanthropy
item_18339_Description:
A big blue rock. It's useful in curatives and curse removal potions.

Associated Primary Skill: Priest
Associated Secondary Skill: Alchemy
item_18340_Description:
A massive green gem. Used in magic, it increases comprehension of scientific concepts.

Associated Primary Skill: Ice Magic
Associated Secondary Skill: Geology
item_18341_Description:
A large scintillating stone. Used in magic, it can mesmerize and soothe.

Associated Primary Skill: Psychology
item_18342_Description:
A big yellow-green stone. Used in magic, it can pierce the barrier between life and death.

Associated Primary Skill: Necromancy
item_18343_Description: A large scintillating gemstone. Used in magic, it can protect against death.
item_18344_Description:
A huge blue gem evocative of dark stormy oceans or winter skies. In magic it enhances musical skill.

Associated Primary Skill: Bard
item_18351_Description: A fragment of magical crystal. It's too small to be used in crafting, but perhaps it could be fused with other shards somehow.
item_18352_Description: A malformed magical quartz. It's too small (and unpredictable!) to be used in crafting.
item_18353_Description: A miniscule version of a Hate Pearl. This one is too small to be used in crafting, but perhaps it could be combined into a larger one via magic.
item_18354_Description: A mineral made mostly of ectoplasm. It shouldn't be used in crafting.
item_18355_Description: A demonic crystal with an unusual structure. It doesn't work well for crafting.
item_18356_Description: A piece of quartz that is partially anchored in another dimension. Being semi-intangible, it is a poor choice for crafting.
item_18357_Description: A fragment of magical crystal. It's too small to be used in crafting, but it's pleasantly warm in your palm.
item_18401_Description: A big blue stone, almost teal in color. In magic, it is associated with beaches and waves.
item_18402_Description: A beautiful big blue stone. In magic it is associated with flight and the sky.
item_18403_Description:
This honkin' big green crystal is a type of beryl. Used in magic, it is associated with perfection, richness, and deities.

In its current form, using it in crafting has unpredictable results. When maximized via magic, it's used for crafting certain exotic weapons.
item_18404_Description: This is an orange hunk of beryl. Used in magic, it is associated with tailoring. It is the favored color of goblins.
item_18405_Description:
This big yellow crystal is extremely reflective from certain angles. In magic, it is associated with growth.

When used in Augment Extraction, Sunstone can extract powers that have no specific skill association.
item_18406_Description: This big golden rock has deep striations. In magic, it is associated with animals, hunting, and livestock.
item_18407_Description: This greenish lump seems to have many other colors embedded in it. While some -- especially orcs -- find it beautiful, others find it disconcerting or even ugly. In magic it is associated with curses and the evil eye.
item_18408_Description:
A clear hunk of beryl. It is not particularly powerful in magic, but it can be used as a sort of energy dampener.

When used in Augment Extraction, Goshenite can extract powers that have no specific skill association.
item_18409_Description: This big white gem with purple streaks is exceptionally strong and makes very resilient jewelry. In magic, it is likewise associated with toughness.
item_18410_Description: Deep crimson in color, this big gem is used in magic to ensure good fortune.
item_18412_Description: A pretty big gem with dark green bands, it's well known for aiding magical communication. This particular gem has been infused with a rune that attunes it to cattle when used in crafting.
item_18413_Description: A pretty big gem with dark green bands, it's well known for aiding magical communication. This particular gem has been infused with a rune that attunes it to the needs of deer when used in crafting.
item_18414_Description: A pretty big gem with dark green bands, it's well known for aiding magical communication. This particular gem has been infused with a rune that attunes it to pigs when used in crafting.
item_18415_Description: A pretty big gem with dark green bands, it's well known for aiding magical communication. This particular gem has been infused with a rune that attunes it to spiders when used in crafting.
item_18416_Description: A big dark red stone, known for its curative properties. This particular stone has been attuned to the First Aid skill if used in crafting.
item_18417_Description: A big crystal made up of black and white bands. Used in magic, it guards against physical dangers. This particular onyx has been attuned to Armor Patching when used in crafting.
item_18418_Description: A pretty big gem with dark green bands, it's well known for aiding magical communication. This particular gem has been infused with a rune that attunes it to bats when used in crafting.
item_18419_Description: A pretty big gem with dark green bands, it's well known for aiding magical communication. This particular gem has been infused with a rune that attunes it to rabbits when used in crafting.
item_18420_Description: A big yellow topaz. An incredibly versatile magical stone. This particular piece has been infused with a rune that attunes it to Fairy Magic, giving crafted items a chance to include one or more Fairy Magic powers (with the rest being Mentalism powers).
item_18421_Description: A big soft blue rock. It's useful in curatives and curse removal potions. This particular gem has been infused with a rune that attunes it to foxes when used in crafting.
item_18423_Description: A mineral made mostly of ectoplasm. It shouldn't be used in crafting.
item_18424_Description: A malformed magical quartz. It's too unpredictable to be used in crafting.
item_18425_Description: A demonic crystal with an unusual structure. It doesn't work well for crafting.
item_18426_Description: A big piece of quartz that is partially anchored in another dimension. Being semi-intangible, it is a poor choice for crafting.
item_18427_Description: A big chunk of magical crystal. It's pleasantly warm in your palm.
item_19001_Description: Rita asked you to deliver this soap to Helena, who lives in the ruins of Castle Eltibule.
item_19002_Description: This bean has little prongs growing out of it that kind of look like horns.
item_19003_Description: A rooty vegetable heart. Would probably be great in a salad.
item_19005_Description: A small sack of foul-smelling white dust. Echur instructed you not to eat it, but that shouldn't be a problem.
item_19006_Description: A handful of odorous twinkling flowers. Cute, but a little too gimmicky.
item_19007_Description: A pack full of extremely small woman's clothing, plus blankets and some costume jewelry.
item_19008_Description: A grisly paw hacked off of The Mauler.
item_19010_Description: Mu asked you to deliver this to a Myconian named Poe.
item_19012_Description: This bundle of sporelings should be delivered to a myconian named Yoy.
item_19013_Description: It's a partially-acid-melted shield without any straps to keep it on.
item_19014_Description: It's a note to Landri the Cold, an elf in Kur Mountains. It reads, "You have not met me, and I cannot reveal myself, but I am a fellow theoretician, and I beg your help. I believe I can tap a portal to other planes of existence, and the plane of ice is very accessible here. I intend to use the Galkun technique to open the wormhole, and power it via ordinary ice lichen." (The note goes on for some length, in increasing technical detail, before ending with a perfunctory request for verification of the plan.)
item_19015_Description: A lead cup with a hefty weight and some cheap jewels on it. As you hold it, it grows unnaturally warm to the touch.
item_19016_Description: A chunk of a weird giant mushroom. It reeks of death, and you're pretty sure it has no practical use.
item_19017_Description: A small poorly-made tiara, sized for a large rat or smallish dog. It seems utterly non-magical.
item_19018_Description: It's an official letter from Praetor Ballard, demanding that a Human named Charles Thompson leave Serbule's surroundings forever... or face death.
item_19020_Description: A sack of millet from a strange plant.
item_19021_Description: A rakshasa's tail ring. It's not magical. It is inscribed, "to Sinda, the explorer supreme."
item_19022_Description: A sage's personal diary. It doesn't contain any useful lore or anything, it's just ramblings about his personal life.
item_19023_Description: A lovely navel ring with a tourmaline in it, apparently designed to calm intense anger.
item_19024_Description: The skull of a dead necromancer.
item_19025_Description: A large gear that came out of a very large golem. It's bent and has no obvious purpose.
item_19026_Description: A small piece of bone inscribed with a dwarven rune for eternal sleep. It doesn't look very useful to you.
item_19027_Description: The skull of a ghoul that invaded from a realm of evil.
item_19028_Description: A prism that has been inscribed with weird symbols. You don't think it would be useful for regular prism-related activities.
item_19029_Description: It's an old note. "Relamir: if you read this, get out of here! The frog men came again... and they killed the guests and staff. There's nobody left but me, and I'm leaving. Fuck this island, fuck this whole inn idea, fuck money, fuck the ocean, fuck everything. I'll pay you the money I owe you within a decade, I promise. - Roolaustus"
item_19030_Description:
It's very light, and very cold, and very fragile.

(Note: if this item is held by someone not on the appropriate quest, it will quickly evaporate.)
item_19031_Description:
A ring that can be used to 'sign' messaging stones as Lord Eltibule.

(Note: if this item is held by someone not on the appropriate quest, it will crumble to dust.)
item_19032_Description: It's labeled 'bear repellent.' It smells spicy. You opt not to taste it.
item_19033_Description: A stinky flower. It's not going to be useful in bouquets, that's for sure.
item_19034_Description: A letter written by Sirine. You're supposed to give it to Yetta in Eltibule.
item_19035_Description:
"Payment: 3 RTC
To: Tal-Saka (outcast rakshasa)
Reason: assistance in interpreting notes about Transmutation."

It's been formally approved by a Lt. Flakis.
item_19037_Description: It's a large, gaudy belt buckle with a hand-painted scene of a man chopping lumber.
item_19038_Description: A bottle with five small areas to hold dirt.
item_19039_Description: It's an old worn shovel with a small handle. It doesn't seem very special to you.
item_19040_Description: A brooch with an emerald inlaid in the middle. It's too dinged up to be valuable, but it's still pretty.
item_19041_Description: A delicate glass star painted red and gold.
item_19042_Description: A tuft of fur from a manticore 'mane'.
item_19043_Description: A hacked-off piece of a rakshasa's ear.
item_19044_Description: A chapter from an adventure novel.
item_19045_Description: A chapter from an adventure novel.
item_19046_Description: A chapter from an adventure novel.
item_19047_Description: A chapter from an adventure novel.
item_19048_Description: A chapter from an adventure novel.
item_19049_Description: A chapter from an adventure novel.
item_19050_Description: Bits of ground up hippo hair. Very itchy.
item_19051_Description: It doesn't seem to do anything, but it's definitely magical. Huh.
item_19052_Description: These were probably decent tassels at some point -- for what, it's impossible to say -- but now they're covered in blood and pus and other fluids.
item_19053_Description: A translucent handkerchief from a ghost. It should have turned into ectoplasm when separated from the ghost, but somehow it hasn't. Eerie.
item_19055_Description: A drop of blood from a gnasher.
item_19056_Description: The mandibles from a particularly nasty insect.
item_19057_Description: The teeth of a very mean bear.
item_19058_Description: A pebble found on a yeti.
item_19059_Description: It says, "Karnag, your family loves you. Please don't do this to yourself! There must be another way. You will miss your son's first words..." the rest is scribbled out except a bit at the bottom written in a different style, "Karnag Has Graduated Wizard School! Signed my mother."
item_19060_Description: It's covered in gibberish -- there are words, but they don't seem to make sentences.
item_19063_Description: The perfumed note says, "W: My love for you grows each night I spend alone. I long to touch you. Your beard, your lips, your... tee hee... meet me tonight behind the kennels. I cannot survive another night without your touch! And don't worry -- the messenger is safe. - E"
item_19064_Description: It's a tooth from a huge Tor-Urak.
item_19065_Description: A drop of magical darkness, trapped in a vial.
item_19066_Description: A keg of Troll's Lip, one of the cheapest and vilest beers in all the Council Lands.
item_19067_Description: The heart of a desert-living drakeworm.
item_19068_Description: A delicate flower that seems simultaneously warm and cold.
item_19069_Description: An ordinary Council that's had weird little marks etched into it.
item_19070_Description: A magical bottle that analyzes water. Use it while standing in the center of a pool of water.
item_19071_Description: Full of anatomical drawings of rat-people, and musings in arcane Goblinese.
item_19072_Description: Looks like meat. Gross meat, but ... just meat.
item_19073_Description: An organ cut out of a large scorpion.
item_19074_Description: A gross dead scorpion. It's very heavy.
item_19075_Description: It says, "I hereby acknowledge that Vangreef's debt to me is paid." It has an elven signature symbol and the letters "SS". Then at the bottom is another note by the author: "Don't waste that spell, Vangreef. Even if you could afford a second casting, I can't recast it until the moon waxes anew. Be smart and fast or your whole gang WILL die down there. And nobody will be able to rescue you."
item_19076_Description: The shell of a dead scorpion. It's very smelly.
item_19077_Description: A small vial that reeks of urine and other unpleasant chemicals. This is an unadulterated "pure" dose of the drug, straight from the alchemists' workbench.
item_19078_Description: A sticky blob of "silk" from a sand worm. It's not what you'd call silky.
item_19079_Description: It seems like an ordinary giant spider leg.
item_19080_Description: A plate-like protrusion from a carnivine.
item_19081_Description: A thorny protrusion from a spriggan weed or plant.
item_19082_Description: A freeze-dried beetle of some sort.
item_19084_Description: The horn of a fae rhino.
item_19085_Description: A hot piece of magical crystal.
item_19086_Description: A cold piece of magical crystal.
item_19087_Description: A warm piece of magical crystal.
item_19088_Description: An old book written by an elf about how to use dwarven engineering in elven designs.
item_19088_Name: Dwarven Circuit Principles and Applications for Elven Dweomers, 5th Edition
item_19089_Description: A care package including some clover, salt licks, sealed documents, and a strange device you don't recognize.
item_19090_Description: A care package including mushroom substrate, a bag of beads, and several paper notes.
item_19091_Description: A care package including sacks of Councils, odd-colored dyes, and a note.
item_19092_Description: A care package consisting entirely of mushrooms.
item_19093_Description: The beak of a very large turkey.
item_19094_Description: A butterfly that's been captured alive. It doesn't look happy... but it's alive!
item_19095_Description: A net designed to catch and contain Rose-mourning butterflies. (Keep this in your inventory when harvesting the butterflies to capture them alive.)
item_19096_Description: These extra-large flower seeds are coated in sticky honey, which is in turn coated in pollen.
item_19098_Description: A shark fin, freshly hacked off.
item_19099_Description: A lump from a very large ooze.
item_19100_Description: The heart of a massive snail.
item_19101_Description: The lung of a newly-sacrificied mega scray. (NOTE: not yet implemented; does nothing!)
item_19102_Description: A special pemphredo eye adjusted to receive messages from a nearby source, instead of a distant one.
item_19103_Description: These pages of parchment are covered in symbols that make no sense to you.
item_19104_Description: A ghostly document. It hasn't quite reverted to ectoplasm yet. Unfortunately it's completely illegible.
item_19105_Description: It looks like a key, but it's made of semi-solid ectolasm. It couldn't possibly unlock anything; it's too mushy!
item_19106_Description: A key that once belonged to a rakshasa named Kimeta.
item_19107_Description: An extremely large, extremely docile frog. It's alive for now, but you should deliver it soon.
item_19108_Description: A chunk of rock from a cave deep under Eltibule.
item_19109_Description: It's a mechanical arm with a strange wand tip at one end.
item_19110_Description: It's a large hollow crystal. Or rather, a crystalline jar that looks like a crystal?
item_19111_Description: It's warm to the touch. Dwarven safety runes indicate that it's explosive, poisonous, and ... there's one other danger symbol that you don't recognize.
item_19112_Description: It's a hollow crystal with some things inside that rattle around.
item_19113_Description: A heavy key that looks unused. It's still polished to a shine.
item_19114_Description: A bundle of rough phlogiston that has carefully sealed up and labeled "Semi-Sturdy Phlogiston".
item_19115_Description: These are ledgers, orders, and addresses. Pretty waterlogged.
item_19116_Description: Perhaps there's a slimy lock somewhere that this opens? It doesn't look like it will last too long... [Note: this key does not yet unlock anything.]
item_19118_Description: It's solid glass with a fish embedded in it.
item_19119_Description: It's a sealed letter addressed to "High Stormmaster Merriana."
item_19120_Description: It's clearly magical: it feels cold to the touch. But nothing seems to happen otherwise.
item_19121_Description: This once-magical crystal ball seems depleted and cracked.
item_19122_Description: It's a hollow crystal with some things inside. Sarina the fairy fixed it for you.
item_19123_Description: A bottle of squid ink that's gone bad. It's leaking from the top.
item_19125_Description: Even the slugs' eggs are poisonous!
item_19126_Description: A fae fish that's been genetically altered to survive here and slowly kill the ocean. It's cold to the touch, and quite inedible.
item_19127_Description: The cork came out of this bottle of blade poison and the liquid inside froze. It doesn't look useful as a weapon anymore, although it could still make fish sick.
item_19128_Description: Looks like regular seaweed. Smells even worse, though. Apparently it's toxic.
item_19129_Description: Apparently this starfish is especially problematic to local fish.
item_19130_Description: Hmm. Could this be part of some weird magical globe?
item_19131_Description: Hmm. Could this be part of some weird magical globe?
item_19132_Description: This lens looks like it fits into a stargazing contraption.
item_19133_Description: This key is made of cork! It seems quite fragile.
item_19134_Description: These are strings for a magical stringed instrument -- a particularly BIG instrument.
item_19135_Description: Hey, this isn't a real skull! It's wood that's been painted to resemble stone. Or perhaps bone.
item_19136_Description: This key fell out of a fake skull. The cuts in the key are clearly decorative; if this opens anything at all, it wouldn't be a normal chest. Probably magic.
item_19137_Description: This key was found on the corpse of a hag.
item_19138_Description: A small key. It must fit something...
item_19139_Description: There are three parts to this weird key.
item_19140_Description: There are three parts to this weird key.
item_19141_Description: There are three parts to this weird key.
item_19142_Description: This looks to be a piece of mummy wrapping.
item_19143_Description: It's a metal spike with various probes and buttons. It makes odd sounds when the buttons are pressed.
item_19144_Description: It's the lens Truxartis asked you to find. It should repair the "blood well".
item_19145_Description: The brain of a swarmer demon.
item_19146_Description: Blood, or at least some sort of blood-like ichor, extracted from the wings of a demonic flapskull.
item_19147_Description: The brain of a demon soldier.
item_19148_Description: A rather heavy sack. It's hard to tell what's inside.
item_19149_Description: This notebook contains arcane sigils in a strange code.
item_19150_Description: It's covered in mold. Perhaps the cork failed?
item_19151_Description: It looks like cheese, smells like ammonia, and stings like a jellyfish.
item_19152_Description: This notebook contains arcane sigils in a strange code.
item_19153_Description: This notebook contains arcane sigils in a strange code. The sage Elmetaph has scribbled out some of the sigils and written in new ones.
item_19154_Description: A sludgey liquid. The label suggests it's used in alchemy.
item_19155_Description: The label indicates that it's some sort of alchemical byproduct of "soul darkening", whatever that is.
item_19160_Description: Seems like a normal skeletal arm.
item_19161_Description: Blood from a crazed vampire thrall, said to be especially tasty. (But tastes like blood to you.)
item_19162_Description: Used by vampires to "clean" blood so it can be used in alcoholic drinks. This one's antique.
item_19163_Description: It says it's a recipe for a mixed drink, but there are no ingredients... Just weird pictograms.
item_19164_Description: An ancient vase of unknown origin. Bring this to someone who understands Flower Arrangements and they may be able to identify it for you.
item_19165_Description: An ancient painting of something natural. Bring this to someone who appreciates nature and they may be able to identify it for you.
item_19166_Description: An ancient crest of unknown origin. Bring this to someone knowledgeable about the past and they may be able to identify it for you.
item_19167_Description: Several pages of notes written entirely in Orcish. There are a few drawings and diagrams depicting a rotational device.
item_19168_Description: A sheet of paper with some kind of mapping between different characters of the Orcish alphabet.
item_19169_Description: A very old book. It looks to be a hand-scribed copy of a book called the Mnemonix Index for the Superficially Undead.
item_19170_Description: The pineal gland of an ancient droach.
item_19171_Description: An oversized ring with bumps that seem to uniquely identify a specific demon.
item_19172_Description: An oversized ring with bumps that seem to uniquely identify a specific demon.
item_19173_Description: An oversized ring with bumps that seem to uniquely identify a specific demon.
item_19174_Description: A series of notes and questions, written entirely in Orcish.
item_19175_Description: A series of notes and questions about a new type of mechanized ice fishing technique. Addressed to someone named "Swamp Dwarf".
item_19176_Description: A torn-off claw of a Demonic Swarmer. It twitches from time to time.
item_19177_Description: A crudely removed horn from the head of a Demon Soldier. It is hot to the touch.
item_19178_Description: A plucked-out eyeball from a Demon Scout. Even in death, it gives you an eerie feeling of being watched.
item_19179_Description: A hacked-off wing from a Lesser Behemoth. No matter how roughed up it gets, it still quickly regenerates.
item_19180_Description: A bony arm spike that's been removed from a demonic Behemoth. It's much heavier than you'd expect for its size.
item_19181_Description: A plucked-out eyeball from a Demon Overseer. If you look directly at it, you can hear it psychically issuing you orders to return it to its owner's head.
item_19182_Description: Fluid that's been siphoned out of a Demon Justiciar's spine. It crackles with electricity.
item_19183_Description: The brain of an ordinary animal that has been corrupted by demonic energy.
item_19184_Description: The brain of a deceased demonic Behemoth, of the Lesser variety.
item_19185_Description: The brain of a deceased demonic Behemoth.
item_19186_Description: The brain of a demonic Overseer. It is hot to the touch, and still pulsating.
item_19187_Description: The brain of a demonic Justiciar. It shocks you every time you handle it.
item_19188_Description: Someone put curry in this conch.
item_19189_Description: A tiny puff of animated air. Somehow it stays coherent.
item_19190_Description: A tiny puff of humid air. Somehow it stays coherent.
item_19191_Description: A tiny puff of fetid air. Somehow it stays coherent.
item_19192_Description: It's dead now, but you swore it was moving just a second ago.
item_19193_Description: A small journal filled with notes on your investigation of Korbok the Goblin in Vidaria.
item_19196_Description: A signet ring, designed to stamp an insignia into printed documents. It is vaguely enchanted. The signet is a single bat wing.

item_19410_Description: It stinks of outdoorsmen and fish.
item_19411_Description: It contains a bunch of little hand-made badges. They don't look valuable.
item_19412_Description: It's an inexpertly made medal with orcish words on it.
item_19413_Description: A handful of odorous twinkling flowers. They smell like body odor that's being poorly masked with citrus.
item_19414_Description: A pile of bones wrapped in twine. Smells smokey.
item_19415_Description: A small amount of sludgy substance.
item_19416_Description: When you touch it, it seems to scream into your brain.
item_19417_Description: A box with an unusual mechanical lock.
item_19418_Description: An amulet. The symbol is a strangely-adorned anvil.
item_19419_Description: It's an authentic rakshasa-style claw weapon.
item_19420_Description: It says, "Dough rose too much; salvage eclair sales; baking time 12".
item_19421_Description: It says, "Getting too old for this. Come home and inherit the bakery. You were always destined for this. And... I forgive you. Completely."
item_19422_Description: It's a scribbled series of images and ratios. You can kind of understand it: it's a recipe of some sort.
item_19423_Description: A particularly vividly-colored painclover. It seems ... About as toxic as the usual kind.
item_19424_Description: A box. It can't be opened, but you hear pitiable pleading coming from inside.
item_19425_Description: A box. It can't be opened, but you hear screaming coming from inside.
item_19426_Description: A box. It can't be opened, but you hear whimpering and crying coming from inside.
item_19427_Description: This salami reeks of spices and... hm, is that a whiff of undeath?

item_21552_Description: Apple brandy, with a distinctive apple taste and a strong kick. A longtime favorite of rural humans.
item_21555_Description: A coarse variety of whisky that's commonly drunk in the dwarven military.
item_21556_Description: Fermented cactus juice with a complex taste. This batch is pretty vile, but barely drinkable.
item_21557_Description: This is some of the worst gin ever. But it is technically not fatal to drink.
item_21558_Description: A favorite of conscripted trolls. The name 'bourbon' is a misnomer since no corn is used. You don't want to know what is used.
item_21559_Description: A bottle of extraordinarily potent rum. It's supposedly made by enslaved fairies who are forced to generate magical sugar all day. This sugar when fermented raises the alcohol percentage well above 100%, much to the chagrin of everyone trying to comprehend what that could possibly mean.
item_21561_Description: Someone has mixed good egg nog with cheap vodka. (This mixed drink is treated as a beer/wine.)
item_21562_Description: A mantis invention: watered-down gin, fruit-flavored rum, and 'secret flavorings' which are probably just mint. A casino favorite! ... because it's usually free. (This mixed drink is treated as a beer/wine.)
item_21601_Description: A simple, watery, slightly insipid beer.
item_21602_Description: A surprisingly complex beer, but... complexity doesn't always taste good.
item_21603_Description: A medium-body beer with a malty flavor.
item_21604_Description: A hoppy beer with hints of spice.
item_21605_Description: A clean, dry beer that goes down easy.
item_21606_Description: A sweet beer with hints of chocolate.
item_21607_Description: A clean beer with hints of oak and licorice. The preferred drink of humans in the Crone Hegemony.
item_21608_Description: A strong dark beer that packs a punch. The preferred socialization drink of dwarves.
item_21609_Description: A golden beer with a slightly bitter taste.
item_21610_Description: A pleasant beer that strongly takes on the characteristics of its flavoring ingredients.

item_21701_Description: This vodka needs to be stored in a cave for an hour before it can be made drinkable. (You do not need to remain in the cave or online the whole time.)
item_21702_Description: This brandy needs to be stored in a cave for an hour before it can be made drinkable. (You do not need to remain in the cave or online the whole time.)
item_21703_Description: This vodka needs to be stored in a cave for an hour before it can be made drinkable. (You do not need to remain in the cave or online the whole time.)
item_21704_Description: This rum needs to be stored in a cave for an hour before it can be made drinkable. (You do not need to remain in the cave or online the whole time.)
item_21705_Description: This whisky needs to be stored in a cave for two hours before it can be made drinkable. (You do not need to remain in the cave or online the whole time.)
item_21706_Description: This tequila needs to be stored in a cave for two hours before it can be made drinkable. (You do not need to remain in the cave or online the whole time.)
item_21707_Description: This gin needs to be stored in a cave for three hours before it can be made drinkable. (You do not need to remain in the cave or online the whole time.)
item_21708_Description: This bourbon needs to be stored in a cave for three hours before it can be made drinkable. (You do not need to remain in the cave or online the whole time.)
item_21709_Description: This cider needs to be stored in a cave for three hours before it can be made drinkable. (You do not need to remain in the cave or online the whole time.)
item_21751_Description: This vodka has been aged and is ready to be filtered and flavored.
item_21752_Description: This brandy has been aged and is ready to be filtered and flavored.
item_21753_Description: This vodka has been aged and is ready to be filtered and flavored.

item_21759_Description: This cider has been aged and is ready to be filtered and flavored.
item_21801_Description: A very rough alcoholic beverage. Some say you grow to like the taste, but others say that's just your tastebuds dying from the pain.
item_21802_Description: Apple brandy, with a distinctive apple taste and a strong kick. A longtime favorite of rural humans.
item_21803_Description: A sweet, relatively palatable variety of vodka. Although it's a traditional dwarven drink, it long ago fell out of favor, and these days dwarves only use it for cooking.
item_21804_Description: Sweet and strong, with the distinctive odor of molasses. In far-off lands, ranalon produce and drink rum in tremendous quantities, but it's less common -- and less delicious -- in Alharth.
item_21805_Description: A simple variety of whisky made from barley. It's drinkable and has a tolerable flavor.
item_21806_Description: Fermented cactus juice with a complex taste. This variety has been aged to make it more drinkable. (It's "more" drinkable, but still not "very" drinkable -- it's an acquired taste.) The stereotypical drink of rakshasa desert bandits.
item_21807_Description: Pleasant, slightly citrusy, but with a distinctive bitterness too. It's said to be the favorite hard liquor of elves, but since elves mostly drink wine, that's not much praise.
item_21808_Description: Made from fermented corn mash, this is very mellow and drinkable. This is the favored drink of Councilwoman Sereel "The Steel" Avenbule. She holds a bourbon competition each year in Vidaria.
item_21809_Description: This variety of cider is made from sour apples and has a complex array of taste sensations. Pity you won't remember the taste after blacking out from it...
item_21851_Description: Although there might be delicious varieties of strawberry wine out there somewhere, this is not it: the strawberry flavor is undermined by a grassy taste.
item_21852_Description: A drinkable wine, but with a slightly musky taste and odor. This is definitely not the kind of wine that elves drink in Verta! But it's not horrible.
item_21853_Description: With notes of citrus and sweetness, this is a decent table wine.
item_21854_Description: Given that it was made from carrots, this wine is... surprisingly good!
item_21855_Description: A buttery, banana-flavored wine that's quite a mouthful.
item_21857_Description: A very dry wine that's quite drinkable.
item_21858_Description: A dry wine. It has a slightly piney smell, but the aroma is not pleasant.
item_21890_Description: A refreshing summer wine. When prepared by a master brewer, this is the perfect outdoor party wine.
item_22001_Description: A chunk of ore that could be used to make silver equipment and jewelry. It can also be used in formulas that augment jewelry.
item_22002_Description: A chunk of fool's gold. It has many alchemical uses, and is great for making costume jewelry. It is often used in formulas that augment jewelry.
item_22003_Description: A chunk of ore that could be used to make gold equipment and jewelry. It is also used in formulas that augment jewelry.
item_22004_Description: A chunk of dense (yet brittle) iridium. It's used in the creation of acid-proof armor and strange robotic constructs. It can also be used to augment jewelry.
item_22005_Description: A tiny bit of unrefined ore. You'd need a bunch of these to make anything useful from them.
item_22006_Description: A chunk of weird ore. It's a potent conductor of certain magics and is used in specialized armor and weapons.
item_22007_Description: A chunk of brittle ore. It is extremely hard to smith, but alchemists have found some uses for it.
item_22008_Description: A chunk of soft metal. While it's not particularly strong, it's very easy for smiths to work with, so it has uses in specialized armors and weapons. Fletchers use it to create dense arrows.
item_22009_Description: A soft strange metal, quite magical, and very poisonous. It has many uses.
item_22010_Description: A chunk of pointy ore. Its most common use is by blacksmiths, who can add fireproofing to armor with it.
item_22011_Description: A chunk of weird ore that can conduct powerful magics, but requires extreme skill to craft with.
item_22012_Description: A chunk of metal with magic-amplification properties. It is used as a form of currency among some goblins due to hyper-inflation of goblin paper money.
item_22013_Description: A small lump of metal said to form in areas of high latent magic. It can be combined with other ores for advanced smithing, and alchemists have their own strange uses for it.
item_22014_Description: Some strips of strange metal with strange properties. It is used by armorsmiths.
item_22015_Description: A small lump of a soft, whitish mineral. You can easily crumble it to a very fine powder that has no smell or taste.
item_22051_Description: A type of stone often found near demonspawn.
item_22052_Description: It's a leaf -- a non-demonic leaf -- encased in an otherworldly variety of amber.
item_22053_Description: A curious side-effect of demonic magic. The protrusions on this rock vaguely resemble teeth.
item_22054_Description: It's just a rock. The name was coined by Plaudius the Wise who posited that the Forthragarian homeworld might be paved with such stones. This has since recently been proven incorrect, but the name is unlikely to change.
item_22055_Description: A tiny chunk of magma that has been magically prevented from cooling. Very weird.
item_22056_Description: A weird mineral found near the rubywall crystals; the origin is completely unknown, and the name seems to be a misnomer.
item_22501_Description: A small metal band used in making casks.
item_22502_Description: A metal band used in making casks.
item_22503_Description: A large metal band used in making casks.
item_22504_Description: Metal pins used mainly in armorsmithing.
item_22505_Description: A metal clamp used by toolmakers and weaponsmiths.
item_22506_Description: A small thin piece of metal covered in a grid of holes. It is used by flower arrangers.
item_22507_Description: A piece of metal covered in a grid of holes. It is used by flower arrangers.
item_22508_Description: A piece of metal that's especially strong.
item_22509_Description: Metal pins used mainly in armorsmithing.
item_22510_Description: Metal pins used mainly in armorsmithing.
item_22511_Description: A small but thick piece of metal covered in a grid of holes. It is used by flower arrangers.
item_22512_Description: Metal pins used mainly in armorsmithing.

item_23202_Description: This is a work-order from a goblin chieftan to a goblin mason guild detailing a home expansion. It's terribly interesting.
item_23203_Description: This is an old battle plan for some intra-goblin skirmish. It might be worth something to somebody... but probably not much in any case.
item_23204_Description: This is sloppily transliterated goblin poetry. It scans poorly, the similes make no sense after translation, and the topic of the poem is toenails. But it is poetry, technically.
item_23301_Description: A simple scroll written in both Orcish and Human. The Human title is "Demands of the Deceased".
item_23302_Description: A simple scroll with both Orcish and Human words on it.
item_23303_Description: A simple scroll written in Orcish, with images of sword techniques.
item_23304_Description: A simple book in Orcish. It has lots of pictures of weapons.
item_23305_Description: A simple book in Orcish. It seems to contain many prayers or chants, plus woodcut images of orcish armies killing people.
item_23306_Description: A simple book in Orcish. It looks like it might be a fiction novel -- very rare among orcs!
item_23308_Description: A book written in Orcish. It has many pictures of spears, polearms, and swords.
item_23310_Description: A scroll in Orcish. It seems to be about orcish wizards.
item_23311_Description: A book written in Orcish. It's clearly a guide to first aid.
item_23312_Description: A book written in Orcish. The woodcuts appear to depict the other major races such as humans, elves, and dwarves.
item_23313_Description: A scroll in Orcish. There is one picture depicting a human wizard.
item_23315_Description: A book written in Orcish. It seems to show techniques for the knife.
item_23316_Description: A book written in Orcish. From the diagrams, it seems to be about magic.
item_23317_Description: A book written in Orcish. In the title, you recognize the orcish word for Gotkul, a famous orc general that commands a massive army in the Crone Hegemony.
item_23318_Description: A book written in Orcish. It seems to be about war machines.
item_23319_Description: A book written in Orcish. Judging by the pictures, it seems to be about Tast, the god of war and armies.
item_23321_Description: A book written in Orcish. It's very long and there are very few pictures.
item_23322_Description: A book with Orcish phrases translated into several languages.
item_23323_Description: A book written in Orcish. The occasional diagrams and woodcut illustrations suggest that it's about orcish mages that perform weird rituals.

Attaching this ribbon to your reins will make you go faster during races. Slightly. Very, very slightly.

This item is attached to Reins or a Saddle via a recipe. Using this item will teach you the recipe if necessary.

item_2530_Description: A ponderous volume wherein an anonymous rakshasa mage explores the idea that the 'old' rakshasa form is somehow still locked within each 'new' rakshasa body. The author posits that certain potions might do the trick, and then explores numerous alchemical hypotheses, none of which pan out too well, but one complex recipe catches your eye.

item_3501_Description: Contains formulae for insanely dangerous run-speed acceleration potions.
item_3501_Name: Alchemy: Ignite The Fire In Your Legs!
item_3502_Description: Explains a technique for hyper-accelerating your organs well beyond their natural limits.
item_3502_Name: Alchemy: Overclock Your Heart and Mind
item_3503_Description: Safe, mild recipes for increasing your Max Power. "Great for beginners!"
item_3503_Name: Alchemy: Safe Brain Boosts For Beginners
item_3504_Description: An introductory text. Most techniques are just tutorials, but the recipe for causing fruit to rot has a few uses. And it's good practice!
item_3504_Name: Alchemy: So You Want To Be An Alchemist
item_3505_Description: Teaches you to make simple acids from exotic metals, or from organic items you may already have in your home. Great for cleaning! Too mild for use in combat.
item_3505_Name: Alchemy: The Hows and Whys of Acid

item_3511_Description: Teaches you several ways to make advanced acids with many uses in crafting. Not quite useful in combat.
item_3511_Name: Alchemy: Potent Acids: Facts and Myths
item_3512_Description: Teaches you a formula for a secret Paleontology ability.
item_3513_Description: Teaches you to brew a tea useful to weather witches.
item_3516_Description: Teaches you various recipes for special inks.
item_3516_Name: Alchemy: Inks for Special Occasions Pt 1
item_3517_Description: Teaches you various recipes for special inks.
item_3517_Name: Alchemy: Inks for Special Occasions Pt 2
item_3518_Description: Teaches you various recipes for special inks.
item_3518_Name: Alchemy: Inks for Special Occasions Pt 3

item_3520_Description: Teaches you how to create a potion that gives you insanely potent jumping power.
item_3520_Name: Alchemy: Why Not Leap Into the Sky?
item_3521_Description: Teaches you how to make Make-Me-Strong Juice, an overly-cutesy name for a potion that boosts max health.
item_3522_Description: Teaches werewolves how to make a special compound from their saliva.
item_3523_Description: Teaches werewolves how to make a special compound from their saliva.
item_3524_Description: Teaches you how to read the phase of the moon.
item_3525_Description: An orcish Hal-Ku story (which are non-fiction accounts of orcs torturing other people to death). Recounts every minute of a six-week torture session, wherein the orcish author slowly killed a human alchemist. Much of the book is padded with grotesque anecdotes, but one useful recipe is hidden in the appendix.
item_3526_Description: Teaches you how to make a hallucinogenic cauldron.
item_3527_Description: Teaches you how to create a potion that increases the time you can stay underwater.
item_3528_Description: Teaches you how to create a potion that accelerates your swimming speed.
item_3529_Description: Teaches you how to create a potion that dramatically boosts your Max Bodyheat.
item_3530_Description: Teaches you a hypothetical recipe designed by a Gazluk sage.
item_3531_Description: Teaches you how to create a potion that grants fire, cold, and electricity resistance.
item_3532_Description: Teaches you how to make a potion that boosts all your attacks +10% against creatures of the Fish anatomy type (which also includes frogs and snails).
item_3533_Description: Teaches you how to make a potion to perceive monsters before they appear.
item_3534_Description: Teaches you how to make a healing potion that can be drunk many times.
item_3535_Description: Teaches you how to make a poison cure.
item_3536_Description: Teaches you how to make a flammable oil that can be applied to weapons.
item_3537_Description: Teaches you how to turn a bunch of gold nuggets into a more useful chunk of gold.
item_3538_Description: Teaches you how to make a potion that helps fairies recoup their energy.
item_3539_Description: Teaches you how to make a potion that helps fairies recoup their energy.
item_3540_Description: Teaches you how to make a pathology toolkit from advanced materials.
item_3541_Description: Teaches you to make a magic ring from otherworldly material.
item_3542_Description: Teaches you how to make an extravagant utility belt for a giant sentient spider.
item_3543_Description: Teaches you how to make an extravagant utility belt for a giant sentient spider.
item_3544_Description: Teaches you how to make an extravagant utility belt for a giant sentient spider.

item_40002_Description: Sturdy and fashionable, if not especially protective.
item_40003_Description: These have been perfectly worn in! Nice. But still very smelly.
item_40005_Description: These boots have been hand-painted, but are still pretty ugly.
item_40006_Description: These were worn by a dainty goblin.
item_40007_Description: These were worn by a tough goblin.
item_40010_Description: When you put these slippers on, your whole body feels lighter.
item_40011_Description: These boots have been dyed red and given a preposterous name.
item_40013_Description: Strong general-purpose footwear, with metal outside for protection, wool inside for insulation.
item_40015_Description: Handmade by a fairy, these make you especially light on your feet.
item_40017_Description: Horseshoes, but fitted for a cow's hooves.
item_40018_Description: Horseshoes, but fitted for a pig's hooves.
item_40019_Description: A pair of simple boots. Echur says that they help one connect with the world around them, whatever that means.
item_4001_Description: A simple autopsy kit for beginning pathologists. (Keep it in your inventory and you'll be given the option to autopsy corpses.)
item_40020_Description: Horseshoes, but fitted for a deer's hooves.
item_40021_Description: These boots are abysmal, leaky, and humiliating to wear.
item_40022_Description: Well, they're hand-made shoes. Not everything that's hand-made is good quality, though, it turns out.
item_40023_Description: Hideous foot wrappings covered in excess leather to provide protection - but not comfort. Expect gigantic blisters.
item_40024_Description: The sort of boots you might imagine if you tried to visualize leather boots in your head. Fairly generic.
item_40025_Description: Boots with a fair amount of reinforcement in the heel, and padding to prevent blisters. Still not elegant, but fairly effective in combat.
item_40026_Description: These boots are pretty nice to wear, and seem like they'll last a long time, too.
item_40027_Description: These are so soft to touch that you almost feel criminal wearing them out into the mud and gore.
item_40028_Description: These boots breathe better than most leather garments, so your feet don't get quite as stinky. Plus, they have double-reinforced laces!
item_40029_Description: This shoe has been heavily over-engineered, with secret cushions and odd snaps to ensure a perfect fit for any foot size.
item_4002_Description: A fancy autopsy kit for advanced pathologists. (+3 to your effective skill when autopsying a corpse.)
item_40030_Description: These are like an extension of your feet, they're so comfortable!
item_40031_Description: Impossibly comfortable for leather. Stylish too!
item_40032_Description: A bastion of basic-shoe quality in a sea of basic-shoe sadness.
item_4003_Description: A fancier autopsy kit for advanced pathologists. (+5 to your effective skill when autopsying a corpse.)
item_40041_Description: Farmers typically don't feel compelled to shod a cow. But some cows are more violent and adventurous than others.
item_40042_Description: Your violent cow will be made all the more violent by these uncomfortable shoes!
item_40043_Description: A crude cow will love these crude shoes.
item_40044_Description: Decent shoes for a cow that's not too shabby.
item_40045_Description: Comfortable shoes that make a violent cow feel free as the wind.
item_40048_Description: These horseshoes - err, cow shoes - are practically supernaturally lovely.
item_40049_Description: Wow, someone put way too much effort into these cow shoes!
item_4004_Description: An autopsy kit made of ice and fairy dust. (Keep it in your inventory and you'll be given the option to autopsy corpses.)
item_40050_Description: If you know anything about cowshoe craftsmanship, you know you're looking at a masterpiece right here.
item_40051_Description: You thought you'd seen a cow-shoe masterpiece before, but this just reset your expectations!
item_40052_Description: A sudden transition is seen here: from elegant but large cowshoes to whisper-thin and unobtrusive ones. The secret is in the fit: it's marvelous.
item_4005_Description: A very complicated device that can help with tricky pathology inquiries. (+10 to your effective skill when autopsying a corpse.)
item_40061_Description: Deer seem to get along fine in nature without having metal hammered into their hooves, but not all deer are content with the life of a passive ungulate.
item_40062_Description: The nails are too long for the deer's small hooves, causing considerable pain while wearing.
item_40063_Description: A crude deer will love these crude shoes.
item_40064_Description: Decent shoes for a deer that's not too shabby.
item_40066_Description: Comfortable shoes that make a violent deer feel free as the wind.
item_40068_Description: These horseshoes - err, deer shoes - are practically supernaturally lovely.
item_40069_Description: Wow, someone put way too much effort into these deer shoes!
item_40070_Description: If you know anything about deershoe craftsmanship, you know you're looking at a masterpiece right here.
item_40071_Description: If a cow needed to be shod for some reason, it is surely not with these wooden, splinter-laced bits of junk.
item_40072_Description: Basically just chunks of wood nailed to a cow's feet. Which makes them sound more comfortable than they really are.
item_40073_Description: Not generally considered a good choice, shodding a cow with bits of wood. These crude shoes are... crude.
item_40074_Description: If all you're looking for is some horseshoe-shaped pieces of wood to nail to your cow's feet, you're in luck.
item_40075_Description: Elegantly whittled and reinforced wooden shoes. They can easily withstand the weight of an angry heiffer.
item_40076_Description: Made by someone with decent working knowledge of wood, these seem fairly comfortable if you have hooves for feet.
item_40077_Description: You'd almost think these were metal, that's how great these bits of wood are!
item_40078_Description: These wooden footguards are a work of art. Avant garde art, where you nail wood to cows' feet.
item_40079_Description: Wow, someone put way too much effort into these wooden cow shoes!
item_40080_Description: A masterpiece of wood-nailed-to-cow-hoof design! Comfortable and stylish. Well, stylish for a cow. They have different sensibilities than people.
item_40081_Description: If a deer needed to be shod for some reason, it is surely not with these wooden, splinter-laced bits of junk.
item_40082_Description: Basically just chunks of wood nailed to a deer's feet. Which makes them sound more comfortable than they really are.
item_40083_Description: Not generally considered a good choice, shodding a deer with bits of wood. These crude shoes are... crude.
item_40084_Description: If all you're looking for is some horseshoe-shaped pieces of wood to nail to your deer's feet, you're in luck.
item_40085_Description: Elegantly whittled and reinforced wooden shoes. They can easily withstand the weight of an angry buck.
item_40086_Description: Made by someone with decent working knowledge of wood, these seem fairly comfortable if you have hooves for feet.
item_40087_Description: You'd almost think these were metal, that's how great these bits of wood are!
item_40088_Description: These wooden footguards are a work of art. Avant garde art, where you nail wood to the feet of an unsuspecting ungulate.
item_40089_Description: Wow, someone put way too much effort into these wooden deer shoes!
item_40090_Description: A masterpiece of wood-nailed-to-deer-hoof design! Comfortable and stylish. Well, stylish for a deer. They have different sensibilities than people.
item_40091_Description: Comfortable, and perfectly fitting in with deer fashion sense.
item_40092_Description: Sacrificing flair for elegance, these are an understated masterpiece.
item_40094_Description: These make it seem easy to make comfortable wooden shoes for cows. (It's not.)
item_40095_Description: Thanks to their near-invisible size, these marvelous cowshoes say almost nothing about the cow that wears them. Friend, foe, demon? These shoes give no clues to the observer.
item_40096_Description: Wow. Now you KNOW you've seen deer-shoe craftsmanship. These tiny things are delicate, yet indestructible.
item_40097_Description: Four out of five talking shoe-wearing deer agree: these are the nicest ones they've ever seen.
item_40101_Description: Special deer-fitted horseshoes made of an otherworldly metal.
item_40102_Description: Thick layers of hide and fur make these boots quite warm.
item_40103_Description: Although they're leather instead of metal, these insulated boots are made for protection, not grace or mobility.
item_40104_Description: It's hard to imagine, but once these boots were the height of fashion.
item_40105_Description: These fancy boots have been digested by a slime creature, leaving mostly just metal behind. Don't worry about them being uncomfortable, though: there's tons of ooze in there to keep your feet cozy.
item_40106_Description: These boots have clearly been the birthing spot for baby spiders in the past. You can clean them off, but can you ever really get the image out of your head?
item_40107_Description: A werewolf wore these boots while transforming a few too many times. The armor quality is totally shot, but it still has some value.
item_40109_Description: Profoundly uncomfortable and rather awkward to move in, but very stylish.
item_40110_Description: Made of a bizarre metal, these uncomfortable boots seem to clench your feet with malice.
item_40111_Description: Common apparel from the Serbule Revolt, 1500 years ago. These boots are so over-engineered that they look brand new. But the smell is pretty bad.
item_40112_Description: The evil creature Ciervos wore these for thousands of years. Must be lucky! Hmm, but he died wearing them. Maybe they're unlucky...
item_40113_Description: Makeshift fae armor crafted from repurposed Winter Court armor. It doesn't have quite the style or powers of Summer Court armor, but it's not bad.
item_40114_Description: Designed by a spiteful necromancer bent on conquering a city, these cloth foot coverings seethe with hatred. And it turns out that pure hatred is pretty warm, providing mild protection against the cold.
item_40115_Description: When thousands of Rakshasa warriors arrived on Alharth, they wore boots like these, designed to accommodate all environments. The secret to their crafting is lost to the Rakshasa living here now.
item_40116_Description: Popular with elven messengers, these elegant cloth footwraps make your feet move much faster! But they don't protect your feet very well, so you'll need significant endurance to tolerate the numerous stubbed toes and sprained ankles.
item_40117_Description: Sturdy and fashionable, if not especially protective.
item_40118_Description: These boots have been hand-painted, but are still pretty ugly.
item_40120_Description: A bit of leaves, a bit of bark, and a lot of magic.
item_40121_Description: Part of the ancient uniform of Dwarven Stalwarts, the elite warriors entrusted with protecting the most important Dwarven secrets. Quality-wise, they're very protective, but very uncomfortable.
item_40122_Description: A pair of old cloth shoes that are much, much tougher than they look.
item_40123_Description: These are shoddy boots that have had sections of their protective armor removed to make them a little more agile. The holes let moisture in, and they quickly stink beyond fathoming.
item_40124_Description: Reasonable boots that have been oiled and softened to let the wearer dash and dart about. Kinda comfortable, but not really.
item_40125_Description: Someone with competence put together these nimble leather boots. Only a small amount of protection is lost in exchange for nimble dodging ability.
item_40126_Description: Quality stuff! These are the sort of evasion boots you'd see in a storefront window, if there was a store specifically for evasion boots, which there is not.
item_40127_Description: Great stuff! These are the sort of evasion boots you'd see in a storefront window, if there was a store specifically for evasion boots, which there is not.
item_40128_Description: Amazing stuff! These are the sort of evasion boots you'd see in a storefront window, if there was a store specifically for evasion boots, which there is not.
item_40129_Description: Astounding stuff! These are the sort of evasion boots you'd see in a storefront window, if there was a store specifically for evasion boots, which there is not.
item_40130_Description: Awesome stuff! These are the sort of evasion boots you'd see in a storefront window, if there was a store specifically for evasion boots, which there is not.
item_40131_Description: Incredible! You can evade like crazy in these.
item_40132_Description: Boots so nimble, you can't even see the wearer's legs when they move. (That's hyperbole.)
item_40150_Description: Made of snail shells and sinew.
item_40151_Description: Made of snail shells and sinew. The sinews were not properly cured and stink beyond comprehension.
item_40152_Description: Made of snail shells and sinew. Only somewhat stinky.
item_40153_Description: Made of snail shells and sinew.
item_40154_Description: Made of snail shells and sinew.

item_40222_Description: Simple bits of metal designed to fit on the hind legs of a wolf.
item_40223_Description: Simple bits of metal designed to fit on the hind legs of a wolf.
item_40224_Description: Resilient metal guards for a wolf's hind legs.

item_40242_Description: Cloth slippers with a bit of resilience. But not much.
item_40243_Description: Cloth slippers with a bit of resilience.
item_40244_Description: Cloth slippers that fit well and don't look too ridiculous.
item_40245_Description: Slippers that combine comfort, safety, and fashion.
item_40246_Description: Slippers that combine comfort, safety, and fashion.
item_40247_Description: Slippers that combine comfort, safety, and fashion.
