item_40251_Description: Slippers that combine comfort, safety, and fashion.
item_40252_Description: Slippers that combine comfort, safety, and fashion.
item_40264_Description: Extra insulated to protect against winds.
item_40265_Description: Cozy and warm, with lots of fur trim.
item_40266_Description: Professional-looking winter boots with heavy insulation.
item_40267_Description: Professional-looking winter boots with heavy insulation.
item_40268_Description: Professional-looking winter boots with heavy insulation.
item_40269_Description: Professional-looking winter boots with heavy insulation.
item_40270_Description: Professional-looking winter boots with heavy insulation.
item_40271_Description: Professional-looking winter boots with heavy insulation.
item_40272_Description: Professional-looking winter boots with heavy insulation.
item_40286_Description: Strong enough to survive a drakeworm's breath, yet supple enough for light dancing in the evening.
item_40287_Description: Strong enough to survive a drakeworm's breath, yet supple enough for light dancing in the evening.
item_40288_Description: Strong enough to survive a drakeworm's breath, yet supple enough for light dancing in the evening.
item_40289_Description: Strong enough to survive a drakeworm's breath, yet supple enough for light dancing in the evening.
item_40290_Description: Strong enough to survive a drakeworm's breath, yet supple enough for light dancing in the evening.
item_40291_Description: Strong enough to survive a drakeworm's breath, yet supple enough for light dancing in the evening.
item_40292_Description: Strong enough to survive a drakeworm's breath, yet supple enough for light dancing in the evening.
item_40301_Description: Worn leather boots with metal meticulously inserted into them. Whoever made these lacked skill in leatherworking, but made up for it with time and attention to detail.
item_40302_Description: This is the latest masterpiece of rakshasa leatherworking, designed to replace the old Ravana armor they can no longer create. In many ways, this surpasses anything from their homeworld, a testament to rakshasa skill... and the toughness of beasts in Alharth.
item_40303_Description: Thick matted hair combined with spider silk. These feel great. No, really. They don't look great, but they feel great.
item_40304_Description: Sturdy and fashionable, if not especially protective.
item_40305_Description: Thick layers of hide and fur make these boots quite warm.
item_40306_Description: The isolated elf colony of Sedgewick Forest prefers armor of this type because it reminds them of their home in Verta. It's been explained to them that nobody in Verta dresses this way anymore, but they don't seem to care.
item_40307_Description: Made of a bizarre metal, these uncomfortable boots seem to clench your feet with malice.
item_40307_Name: Great Boots of the Winter Court
item_40308_Description: Used by the typical troops of the Crone Hegemony.
item_40309_Description: The evil necromancer/mentalist wore these comfy booties whenever he destroyed towns or villages.
item_40310_Description: "To my lover, may she run back to me soon. - Raul"
item_40311_Description: Made of insanely strong scorpion and beetle carapace plates riveted together, these boots will keep your feet safe from everything except blisters. It's a pity carapace plates aren't more comfortable...
item_40312_Description: The customized scorpiclaw boots of the dread Onkara general. They fit a little better than normal.
item_40313_Description: Budget-conscious goblins love these boots, which look as sturdy as regular goblin boots, but a fraction of the cost!
item_40315_Description: The rightly-feared orcish Misery Troopers from the Snowblood Army wear these near-indestructible boots. Well-maintained and polished.
item_40316_Description: Worn by orcish mages after they've been promoted to 'Magic Trooper', which is the highest rank that most can expect to reach, given mages' glass ceiling in orcish society.
item_40317_Description: Named after a legendary werewolf who supposedly designed this style of footwear, these shoes fall off cleanly when a lycanthrope transforms, avoiding embarrassing trip-and-fall accidents in the heat of battle. A symbol of Arisetsu is carefully embroidered into the left shoe.
item_40318_Description: This red-tinted armor is worn by Gazluk City's front-line soldiers, known as "Death Troopers". Their armor enhances their strength and melee prowess.
item_40319_Description: This blue-tinted armor is worn by Gazluk City's support soldiers, known as "Misery Troopers". Their armor is strong while also providing survival protection in harsh weather.
item_40320_Description: This green-tinted armor is worn by Gazluk City's gadgeteers and tacticians, known as "Treachery Troopers". Their armor protects them from battlefield conditions and enhances their tricky combat techniques.
item_40321_Description: This gold-tinted armor is worn by the highest battlefield officers in Gazluk City's army. It is versatile and strong, like its wearer.
item_40322_Description: This cloth armor is worn by the few Gazluk wizards who have become officers. It is designed to aid powerful spellcasters.
item_40323_Description: The terrifying orcish cult known as the Mutterers wear these garments.
item_40324_Description: The terrifying orcish cult known as the Mutterers wear these garments.
item_40325_Description: Originally gifted to ratkin guerillas by the Crone Hegemony, these magic boots have been re-soled and re-leathered several times in the intervening years.
item_40326_Description: The product of a short-lived treaty with the Orcs of Gazluk, these orcish-style boots are comfortable and keep the feet dry in any environment. The boots were stored in a military warehouse for decades, which gives them a musty odor, but they are in great shape.
item_40327_Description: Made of a bizarre metal, these uncomfortable boots seem to clench your feet with malice.
item_40328_Description: Made of a bizarre glowing metal, these uncomfortable boots seem to clench your feet with malice.
item_40329_Description: These old boots have been resoled with a mysterious metal, and the most recent owner has etched eerie symbols into them.
item_40330_Description: Made of giant seashells. It's more comfortable than it looks. (Includes random level 35 enchantments.)
item_40331_Description: Made of giant seashells. It's more comfortable than it looks. (Includes random level 40 enchantments.)
item_40332_Description: Made of giant seashells. It's more comfortable than it looks. (Includes random level 45 enchantments.)
item_40334_Description: Skintight shoes worn by ranalon. Their creation method is a mystery to outsiders.
item_40335_Description: Skintight shoes worn by ranalon. Their creation method is a mystery to outsiders.
item_40336_Description: These fancy boots have been digested by a slime creature, leaving mostly just metal behind. Don't worry about them being uncomfortable, though: there's tons of ooze in there to keep your feet cozy.
item_40337_Description: Worn by fae officers aboard their strange ships. Made of an unrecognizable material.
item_40338_Description: The goblin chief Rubgag used these magic slippers to win dancing competitions (which was definitely cheating, or "Chiefing", as he called it.).
item_40339_Description: This style of plate boot is named after the elven knight Niph. As the story goes, Niph fought off an army of undead alone, somehow running "faster than death itself."
item_40340_Description: Mass-produced in Povus right before the town was destroyed.
item_40341_Description: Sturdy metal boots with a guiding mechanism to orient a shovel while digging.
item_40342_Description: A thick layer of fungal "skin" peeled off of a dead fungus-man.
item_40343_Description: Sized for a genetically-enhanced goblin known as a hobgoblin. Their feet are pretty big, but you can still make these fit.
item_40344_Description: Dwarven in design and quite heavy. Insulated against cold, though.
item_40345_Description: Worn by both elves and dwarves during the Elf-Orc Wars, these boots were made for stomping.
item_40347_Description: The evil creature Ciervos wore these for thousands of years. Must be lucky! Hmm, but he died wearing them. Maybe they're unlucky...
item_40348_Description: These fancy boots have been digested by a slime creature, leaving mostly just metal behind. Don't worry about them being uncomfortable, though: there's tons of ooze in there to keep your feet cozy.
item_40349_Description: Old-fashioned elven armor from the age of Romast, thousands of years ago.
item_40350_Description: An invisible pair of boots, designed to force others to look at your bare feet whether they want to or not. Useful in glamouring.
item_40351_Description: Grotesque boots with an uncomfortably slime-like internal material. Crudely sewn into the inside is the phrase "Boots Not Art."
item_40352_Description: Created in homage to an unknown god or king. They are inscribed with the phrase "Be quick, my servant! Time is fleeting!"
item_40353_Description: This is a pair of "Servant's Walkers" boots that have been customized for extra speed.
item_40354_Description: Likely of human make, these thick metal boots expand or contract to meet any foot size and always fit perfectly.
item_40355_Description: Sages say the Timedancer was a scion of Laeth, the goddess of Time. Or perhaps the Timedancer will be so one day. Time paradoxes are complex, and all that can be reliably said is that the Timedancer is not here right now.
item_40356_Description: Owned by a once-powerful mage who died choking on a lemon seed. They retain some of Soteric's power but also a traumatic death-memory.
item_40357_Description: Etched with the symbols of Dreva and carved of an unknown wood, these animal shoes radiate authority over nature. Only a deer could wear these.
item_40358_Description: Etched with the symbols of Dreva and carved of an unknown wood, these animal shoes radiate authority over nature. Only a cow, pure of heart, could wear these.
item_40359_Description: Etched with the symbols of Dreva and carved of an unknown wood, these animal shoes radiate authority over nature. Only a brave and daring pig could wear these.
item_40404_Description: Soft, astonishingly flexible, and resistant to murder.
item_40405_Description: Soft, astonishingly flexible, and resistant to murder.
item_40406_Description: Soft, astonishingly flexible, and resistant to murder.
item_40407_Description: Soft, astonishingly flexible, and resistant to murder.
item_40408_Description: Soft, astonishingly flexible, and resistant to murder.
item_40409_Description: Soft, astonishingly flexible, and resistant to murder.
item_40410_Description: Soft, astonishingly flexible, and resistant to murder.
item_40411_Description: Soft, astonishingly flexible, and resistant to murder.
item_40412_Description: Soft, astonishingly flexible, and resistant to murder.
item_40420_Description: Popular with elven messengers, these elegant cloth footwraps make your feet move much faster! But they don't protect your feet very well, so you'll need significant endurance to tolerate the numerous stubbed toes and sprained ankles. This pair was enhanced by Raim of the Crafting Caravan.
item_40441_Description: These metal boots are devoid of padding and practicality.
item_40442_Description: These metal boots have a little bit of lining and a lot of poor craftsmanship.
item_40443_Description: They mostly stay on your foot, which is good.
item_40444_Description: Aside from the jangle sound when you walk, these boots are decent.
item_40445_Description: Padded chain footwear made by a competent hand.
item_40446_Description: These chain boots are both protective and comfortable.
item_40447_Description: These chain boots are both protective and comfortable.
item_40448_Description: Chainmail that combines comfort, safety, and even a bit of style.
item_40449_Description: Chainmail that combines comfort, safety, and even a bit of style.
item_40450_Description: Chainmail that combines comfort, safety, and even a bit of style.
item_40451_Description: Chainmail that combines comfort, safety, and even a bit of style.
item_40452_Description: Chainmail that combines comfort, safety, and even a bit of style.
item_40486_Description: With a simple, streamlined style and a sharp, edgy aesthetic.
item_40487_Description: With a simple, streamlined style and a sharp, edgy aesthetic.
item_40488_Description: With a simple, streamlined style and a sharp, edgy aesthetic.
item_40489_Description: With a simple, streamlined style and a sharp, edgy aesthetic.
item_40490_Description: With a simple, streamlined style and a sharp, edgy aesthetic.
item_40491_Description: With a simple, streamlined style and a sharp, edgy aesthetic.
item_40492_Description: With a simple, streamlined style and a sharp, edgy aesthetic.
item_40525_Description: Linen slippers with grippy soles and magic-sustaining properties.
item_40526_Description: Linen slippers with grippy soles and magic-sustaining properties.
item_40527_Description: Linen slippers with grippy soles and magic-sustaining properties.
item_40528_Description: Linen slippers with grippy soles and magic-sustaining properties.
item_40529_Description: Linen slippers with grippy soles and magic-sustaining properties.
item_40530_Description: Linen slippers with grippy soles and magic-sustaining properties.
item_40531_Description: Linen slippers with grippy soles and magic-sustaining properties.
item_40532_Description: Linen slippers with grippy soles and magic-sustaining properties.
item_41001_Description: These have been in the muck for ages...
item_41002_Description: Nice greaves. Vaguely stylish yet vaguely protective.
item_41003_Description: Sure they're gross, but they're already worn in, and the caked-on dirt provides extra resilience.
item_41004_Description: Comfy, invigorating, and magically protective. They smell like lilac and pimentos for some reason.
item_41005_Description: Gold! With little white frill things. Reasonably protective, too.
item_41006_Description: A protective garment that also keeps you light on your feet. The goblin smell, unfortunately, doesn't wash out.
item_41007_Description: Strong pants made of leather. You don't want to think about what creature the leather may have come from.
item_41008_Description: Made from the skin of abominations, these pants seem to constantly fondle your legs as you wear them. It's really quite disturbing, but the armor is tough...
item_41009_Description: The "quality" in the title refers to the defensive merits, not the fashion statement.
item_41011_Description: Crafted by elves who were too homesick to get out of bed, the Pants of the Yearning Sea are sorrow incarnate. But these sure are comfy. Plus, the tears that dripped onto the fabric have made it extra bouncy!
item_41012_Description: Once worn by Makopa the semi-legendary goblin boss.
item_41013_Description: They're supernaturally slimming! Plus they have a hidden dimensional pocket inside that can fit a few extra items.
item_41014_Description: Created by an alchemist/farmer to protect her legs in the fields, these pants are great at keeping nature off of you. Too bad about the odor...
item_41015_Description: Full of wool! Way too much wool. You'd get heatstroke if it was too hot!
item_41016_Description: These once-amazing pants aren't amazing anymore. They're torn and mangled. But there's still some armor on them... could be worse! A hand-sewn tag inside says "To General Lawyr, may you remember me always, your Kello."
item_41020_Description: Apparently Rita gave these to Sir Coth, who has since discarded them.
item_41021_Description: Somebody has worn these undergarments previously without washing them. Hopefully that person was you. But you're not sure. Anyway, they reek, plus they provide no armor.
item_41022_Description: Someone was buried in these, apparently. They're caked with dust, but don't seem the worse for wear.
item_41023_Description: Any beauty these leggings once possessed has long faded. But they still have an inner beauty, right?
item_41024_Description: There's a big hole in these, but a decade of spiderweb build-up kind of seals the hole. Kind of.
item_41025_Description: These cotton undergarments are possibly the least interesting piece of clothing possible.
item_41026_Description: These are just cotton underclothes, but they're really well made!
item_41027_Description: Leather armor with a thin layer of strong metal inside.
item_41028_Description: During the Elf-Dwarf wars, Dwarven mages created leather-evaporation magic, rendering elves' usual armor obsolete. These ceremonial plate leggings were hurried into battle. Although they're impressively protective, they're still pretty uncomfortable.
item_41029_Description: The metal is always freezing cold, but you'll soon become numb to such trivial irritations.
item_41030_Description: It's been about 15 centuries since the Serbule Revolt, but these dwarven leggings don't seem a day over 450. Their preservative magic is quite strong.
item_41031_Description: Made from repurposed Winter Court armor in an imperfect process. This somehow conveys the warmth of summer while still being quite cold to the touch.
item_41032_Description: Designed by a spiteful necromancer bent on conquering a city, these airy pants seethe with hatred. And it turns out that pure hatred is pretty warm, providing mild protection against the cold.
item_41033_Description: When thousands of Rakshasa warriors arrived on Alharth, they wore suits of armor like this, designed to accommodate all environments. The secret to their crafting is lost to the Rakshasa living here now.
item_41034_Description: This kilt is part of the uniform of the dwarven Idealist's Guild. Members produce a substance they call "optimism", which is sold in the form of addictive crystals.
item_41035_Description: Nice greaves. Vaguely stylish yet vaguely protective.
item_41036_Description: Gold! With little white frill things. Reasonably protective, too.
item_41037_Description: The "quality" in the title refers to the defensive merits, not the fashion statement.
item_41038_Description: Kind of revealing. But very comfortable.
item_41039_Description: Dwarven Stalwarts wore leg armor like this a thousand years ago, and then handed down for centuries after the Stalwarts were no more, because these pants are great at absorbing punishment.
item_41040_Description: The metal is always freezing cold, but you'll soon become numb to such trivial irritations.
item_41040_Name: Great Greaves of the Winter Court
item_41041_Description: With horrid stitching and gigantic holes, these smelly pants are just plain sad.
item_41042_Description: Sewn by an amateur, with low-quality materials and a lack of fashion sense.
item_41043_Description: With an eye toward performance over elegance, these leather pants will protect you from monsters, but not from chafing.
item_41044_Description: Thanks to a modicum of padding inside, wearing these pants will only leave you severely chafed, instead of incapacitated.
item_41045_Description: Someone with a basic sense of style has fashioned these leather pants. Unfortunately the protective reinforcement ruins the look, but it could be worse.
item_41047_Description: Astonishingly, these leather pants don't chafe! This is some high-grade magic right here. Or just great tailoring.
item_41048_Description: These beautiful leather pants are so soft, you just want to pet them. Don't let anybody see you doing that.
item_41049_Description: You get the feeling that, even when they were brand new, these pants already had that great worn-in feeling. The padding is strong but not bulky and the leather smell is intoxicating.
item_41050_Description: Slip into these and enjoy having leather on your crotch like never before.
item_41051_Description: Someone was buried in these, apparently. They're caked with dust, but don't seem the worse for wear.
item_41052_Description: A classic dwarven military design. While it has down sides, the upside is incredible durability.
item_41053_Description: A pair of invisible pants, so when you are inevitably kicked out of establishments you can still tell all of your friends that you were technically wear pants. Useful in glamouring.
item_41054_Description: Naturally svelte with every movement, these pants highlight the surprising suppleness of leather.
item_41055_Description: From the material to the craftsmanship to the enchantments, these fashionable pants exude a calm mastery.
item_41063_Description: A sub-par pair of leather leggings has been chopped inexpertly to make it more evasive. The result is humiliating in most respects.
item_41064_Description: These leather pants have had the joints replaced with super-soft leather. The stitching is poor, though, and the pants are drafty. Drafty pants!
item_41065_Description: These leather pants are so soft. They're nice for dodging arrows and the like.
item_41066_Description: Stylish, highly mobile, and almost-but-not-quite comfortable leather pants.
item_41067_Description: Stylish, highly mobile, and comfortable leather pants.
item_41068_Description: Stylish, highly mobile, and comfortable leather pants.
item_41069_Description: Stylish, highly mobile, and comfortable leather pants.
item_41070_Description: Stylish, highly mobile, and comfortable leather pants.
item_41071_Description: Stylish, highly mobile, and comfortable leather pants.
item_41072_Description: Stylish, highly mobile, and comfortable leather pants.
item_4107_Description: This is damned good calligraphy ink.
item_41084_Description: Extra insulated to protect against winds.
item_41085_Description: Cozy and warm, with lots of fur trim.
item_41086_Description: Professional-looking winter pants with heavy insulation.
item_41087_Description: Professional-looking winter pants with heavy insulation.
item_41088_Description: Professional-looking winter pants with heavy insulation.
item_41089_Description: Professional-looking winter pants with heavy insulation.
item_41090_Description: Professional-looking winter pants with heavy insulation.
item_41091_Description: Professional-looking winter pants with heavy insulation.
item_41092_Description: Professional-looking winter pants with heavy insulation.
item_41101_Description: A barbaric act of cruelty to cotton.
item_41102_Description: The quadruple-thick stitching screams "overcompensating" and the pattern is frankly horrendous. But it'd be good for sewer-delving or something.
item_41103_Description: A rustic farmer might consider these pants barely adequate. Might.
item_41104_Description: The premier choice for those who work the land daily. Strong stitching, and none of that annoying "attractiveness" to get in the way.
item_41105_Description: Not bad. You wouldn't even be embarrassed to be seen in these nice pants. Unless you were seen by someone you wanted to impress.
item_41106_Description: Good thread count. Sturdy crotch support. A little stiff, but to be expected for such a resilient pair of pants.
item_41107_Description: Very soft to the touch, but resistant to damage.
item_41108_Description: Very soft to the touch, but resistant to damage.
item_41109_Description: Very soft to the touch, but resistant to damage.
item_41110_Description: Very soft to the touch, but resistant to damage.
item_41111_Description: Very soft to the touch, but resistant to damage.
item_41112_Description: Very soft to the touch, but resistant to damage.
item_4111_Description: A potent acid for removing inscriptions on items.
item_41151_Description: Someone has awkwardly painted these sub-par pants. Well, woodland animals aren't super smart, so maybe that'll do.
item_41152_Description: Decent pants that blend into the surroundings.
item_41153_Description: This is a great camouflage job! The pants, well, they're not bad per se...
item_41154_Description: Don't drop these in the forest or you'll never find them again.
item_41155_Description: These look like really soft plants. Wait, those are soft pants. That's great camo.
item_41156_Description: These look like really soft plants. Wait, those are soft pants. That's great camo.

item_41171_Description: Made of snail shells and sinew. The sinews were not properly cured and stink beyond comprehension.
item_41172_Description: Made of snail shells and sinew. Only somewhat stinky.

item_4121_Description: An empty book. Experts at Calligraphy can write in it at a Writing Table.
item_41221_Description: Simple bits of metal designed to fit on the lower legs and haunches of a wolf.
item_41222_Description: Simple bits of metal designed to fit on the lower legs and haunches of a wolf.
item_41223_Description: Simple bits of metal designed to fit on the lower legs and haunches of a wolf.

item_4123_Description: Used by calligraphers when extremely fine detail must be inked.
item_41246_Description: These pants are made of leather that's incredibly soft, yet strong. Although it has an inner lining to prevent chafing, that lining is made of silk, and silk chafes pretty badly too. But you get used to it.
item_41247_Description: These pants are made of leather that's incredibly soft, yet strong. Although it has an inner lining to prevent chafing, that lining is made of silk, and silk chafes pretty badly too. But you get used to it.
item_41248_Description: These pants are made of leather that's incredibly soft, yet strong. Although it has an inner lining to prevent chafing, that lining is made of silk, and silk chafes pretty badly too. But you get used to it.
item_41249_Description: These pants are made of leather that's incredibly soft, yet strong. Although it has an inner lining to prevent chafing, that lining is made of silk, and silk chafes pretty badly too. But you get used to it.
item_41250_Description: These pants are made of leather that's incredibly soft, yet strong. Although it has an inner lining to prevent chafing, that lining is made of silk, and silk chafes pretty badly too. But you get used to it.
item_41251_Description: These pants are made of leather that's incredibly soft, yet strong. Although it has an inner lining to prevent chafing, that lining is made of silk, and silk chafes pretty badly too. But you get used to it.

item_41264_Description: Made of ultra-strong cloth, this garment makes it easy to dodge attacks.
item_41265_Description: Made of ultra-strong cloth, this garment makes it easy to dodge attacks.
item_41266_Description: Made of ultra-strong cloth, this garment makes it easy to dodge attacks.
item_41267_Description: Made of ultra-strong cloth, this garment makes it easy to dodge attacks.

item_41271_Description: Made of ultra-strong cloth, this garment makes it easy to dodge attacks.
item_41272_Description: Made of ultra-strong cloth, this garment makes it easy to dodge attacks.
item_41281_Description: Some sort of hempen material stuffed with strange leaves. Itchy.
item_41282_Description: Stuffed with silky leaves and bits of straw. Not comfortable, but great protection!
item_41283_Description: You would never realize these pants were crafted by treants... if indeed they were crafted by treants. (Followers of Ri-Shin constantly lie about how many things are crafted by treants. Most likely, it was elves.)
item_41284_Description: A pant-shaped conglomeration of plant material. Sometimes a bit of moss is exposed, but they mostly work great.
item_41285_Description: Silky leaves, soft moss ... These things are almost comfortable!
item_41301_Description: Worn leather pants with metal meticulously inserted into them. Whoever made these lacked skill in leatherworking, but made up for it with time and attention to detail.
item_41302_Description: This is the latest masterpiece of rakshasa leatherworking, designed to replace the old Ravana armor they can no longer create. In many ways, this surpasses anything from their homeworld, a testament to rakshasa skill... and the toughness of beasts in Alharth.
item_41303_Description: Thick matted hair combined with spider silk. These feel great. No, really. They don't look great, but they feel great.
item_41304_Description: Nice greaves. Vaguely stylish yet vaguely protective.
item_41305_Description: The isolated elf colony of Sedgewick Forest prefers armor of this type because it reminds them of their home in Verta. It's been explained to them that nobody in Verta dresses this way anymore, but they don't seem to care. An outdated version of the Symbol of Arisetsu can be found on the front.
item_41306_Description: A protective garment that also keeps you light on your feet. The goblin smell, unfortunately, doesn't wash out.
item_41307_Description: Used by the typical troops of the Crone Hegemony.
item_41308_Description: Not really that comfortable unless you're in a blizzard, but then they are really cozy.
item_41309_Description: It's amazing that these awkwardly-riveted pieces of scorpion and beetle carapace give you any mobility at all. But they do! Just don't sit down too quickly, and you'll be fine.
item_41310_Description: The terrifying Onkara lord wore these pants all the time. Like, nonstop. But they don't smell. Eerie.
item_41311_Description: The stitching on these leather pants is awful. And the quality of the leather sub-par. But goblins forced to buy their own armor often choose these for cost reasons.
item_41312_Description: It's unclear what sort of leather was used in these greaves, but it's not cow leather. Something thicker.
item_41313_Description: Worn by the elite Snowblood orcs that take care of problematic situations in battle. They're hard to maintain, but impressively protective.
item_41314_Description: Worn by orcish mages after they've been promoted to 'Magic Trooper', which is the highest rank they can expect to reach, given mages' glass ceiling in orcish society.
item_41315_Description: The brutish barbarians known as the 'McNasty clan' are known for their fearlessness almost as much as for their malodorous grooming habits. These crude metal pants are certainly not clean. But they are strong.
item_41316_Description: This red-tinted armor is worn by Gazluk City's front-line soldiers, known as "Death Troopers". Their armor enhances their strength and melee prowess.
item_41317_Description: This blue-tinted armor is worn by Gazluk City's support soldiers, known as "Misery Troopers". Their armor is strong while also providing survival protection in harsh weather.
item_41318_Description: This green-tinted armor is worn by Gazluk City's gadgeteers and tacticians, known as "Treachery Troopers". Their armor protects them from battlefield conditions and enhances their tricky combat techniques.
item_41319_Description: This gold-tinted armor is worn by the highest battlefield officers in Gazluk City's army. It is versatile and strong, like its wearer.
item_41320_Description: This cloth armor is worn by the few Gazluk wizards who have become officers. It is designed to aid powerful spellcasters.
item_41321_Description: The terrifying orcish cult known as the Mutterers wear these garments.
item_41322_Description: The terrifying orcish cult known as the Mutterers wear these garments.
item_41323_Description: While not exactly beautiful, it's definitely elegant and comfortable. The early work of an up-and-coming master tailor, perhaps.
item_41324_Description: About a million of these enchanted pants were made by the Crone Hegemony for ratkin revolutionaries who said they were going to overthrow their gob-land masters. Ultimately the revolutionaries freed themselves and their friends, then called it quits and traded their armor away.
item_41325_Description: Designed to be worn by either orcs or humans, these pants feature several cinch-points to make them comfortable for any leg girth. With clever manipulation of these belts you store a few small items in them... although that wouldn't be very comfortable.
item_41326_Description: Extraordinarily light armor that is very cold to the touch.
item_41327_Description: When you don this strange glowing metal suit, you can feel your sense of empathy and community diminish. All the better to kill without remorse!
item_41328_Description: The leather in these pants has been been partially transformed into wood bark, which is profoundly uncomfortable.
item_41329_Description: Made of giant seashells stitched together with seaweed rope. Surprisingly, they don't stink. (Includes random level 35 enchantments.)
item_41330_Description: Made of giant seashells stitched together with seaweed rope. Surprisingly, they don't stink. (Includes random level 40 enchantments.)
item_41331_Description: Made of giant seashells stitched together with seaweed rope. Surprisingly, they don't stink. (Includes random level 45 enchantments.)
item_41333_Description: Skintight tights worn by ranalon. Their creation method is a mystery to outsiders.
item_41334_Description: Skintight tights worn by ranalon. Their creation method is a mystery to outsiders.
item_41335_Description: A system of belts and harnesses worn around the hips and legs. Keeps equipment close at hand!
item_41336_Description: A customized system of harnesses worn around the hips and legs. Armor-enhancing nanodispensers are hidden behind the knee guards.
item_41337_Description: Produced in Povus right up until the day the town was attacked. While stylish, they unfortunately lack pockets.
item_41338_Description: Uncomfortable tights made of scray skin. When a scray dies, ranalon often make pants like these to honor them by using all the parts of the corpse.
item_41339_Description: Peeled from the legs of a dead fungus-man, these form-fitting "leggings" are made of fungus.
item_41340_Description: Sized for a very large goblin, or slightly-large human.
item_41341_Description: During the Elf-Orc war, dwarven priests of Umrad made tens of thousands of these for their elven allies.
item_41342_Description: A customized system of harnesses worn around the hips and legs. Armor-enhancing nanodispensers are hidden behind the knee guards.
item_41343_Description: The leather in these pants has been been partially transformed into wood bark, which is is profoundly uncomfortable.
item_41344_Description: This type of armor can be found in picture-books, but nobody has met an actual Thorian in quite some time.
item_41345_Description: These ancient pants prove that not all powerful mages have good fashion sense.
item_41346_Description: These silk pants are uncomfortably warm at all times.
item_41347_Description: The... err... "backside" of these sturdy leather pants doesn't seem to be part of the original design. Some previous owner has sewn in a thick leather square to ensure full body coverage.
item_41348_Description: This metal armor can shift the wearer's weight automatically to ensure they don't fall over.
item_41349_Description: Pants that aren't quite all there... Weird.
item_41401_Description: Poorly-made chain that fits and protects poorly.
item_41403_Description: Poorly-made chain that has been organized into a semblance of pants.
item_41404_Description: Aside from the jangle sound when you walk, these are decent pants.
item_41405_Description: Padded chain leggings with a nominal attention to detail.
item_41407_Description: Surprisingly light for chainmail, these pants are very comfortable except for the swishing sound they make, and the lack of pockets.
item_41408_Description: Fitted, protective chainmail. If it had pockets, you'd forget it was armor.
item_41409_Description: Strong, well-fitting chainmail. A shame about the lack of pockets.
item_41410_Description: Strong, well-fitting chainmail. A shame about the lack of pockets.
item_41411_Description: Strong, well-fitting chainmail. A shame about the lack of pockets.
item_41412_Description: Strong, well-fitting chainmail. A shame about the lack of pockets.

item_4161_Description: The simplest kind of bait that can be used with angling.

item_4163_Description: The most complex kind of bait that can be used with angling.
item_4171_Description:
A typical orcish-style fishing device. A very long string is stored on a winchable spool. Bizarre.

(Keep this in your inventory in order to use Angling abilities.)
item_4181_Description: A delicate tool for grinding orcish wheat into flour. (Using this item will teach the recipe if needed.)
item_42002_Description: Pretty shoddy workmanship... but hey, it's armor.
item_42003_Description: This is the grossest possible thing. But all the blood smears make it harder for monsters to tell where to stab you.
item_42005_Description: This simple platemail has sufficient protection, but it could do with more padding. Very uncomfortable!
item_42006_Description: Strong enough for a Human, but made for a Goblin. By slaves who toil in very poor working conditions.
item_42007_Description: This is good armor, but rather uncomfortable if you have chest hair... lots of pinching.
item_42009_Description: Strong armor with a strong statement to make: "I like red!"
item_42010_Description: The breastplate features a perfectly preserved Giant Snail shell.
item_42011_Description: Sacrificing armor for killing power, these robes are popular with wizards. A Necromancy Gem is woven into the chest.
item_42012_Description: Imbued with potent magic, the ever-patching shirt is constantly fixing itself.
item_42013_Description: The best armor ever! Oh, except it rusts easily, squeaks a lot, is hot in the summer and cold in the winter, very uncomfortable, and particularly prone to lightning damage. And also it's not the best armor in terms of armor capacity. But ... but it's not bad.
item_42014_Description: It was in the bedding of Old Fangsworth's lair.
item_42015_Description: Lawara the amnesiac was wearing this when she first awoke. It clearly used to be something special, but now it's barely holding together.
item_42017_Description: Another piece of once-ceremonial armor that was retrofitted for combat during the Elf-Dwarf wars. The suit has since become a favorite of elven warriors thanks to its handy odor-repulsion magic. And also, of course, its insane amount of protection.
item_4201_Description: For healing minor injuries. Used by First Aid abilities.
item_42023_Description: Woven with fairy magic, this lovely shirt regenerates its leaves continuously. Unfortunately it is extremely itchy to wear. But noone said safety and comfort had to go hand in hand, so stop complaining.
item_42027_Description: This gross shirt smells of urine. It provides no armor protection, and it's also very itchy.
item_42028_Description: This simple platemail has sufficient protection, but it could do with more padding. Very uncomfortable!
item_42029_Description: Simple in design, but heavy on the armor plating.
item_4202_Description: For healing moderate injuries. Used by First Aid 2 (and other abilities).
item_42030_Description: Rust damage has taken a heavy toll on this breastplate. It's still functional, but you'll probably get rust stains all over yourself.
item_42031_Description: Somehow this breastplate got stuck onto a spider via sticky web! What are the odds? Unfortunately, spider acid has filled it with holes, but what a great story!
item_42032_Description: This breastplate is very old, and a sequence of enchantments has been laid atop it to protect it from the elements. An active symbol of Arisetsu is barely visible under layers of rust and paint.
item_42033_Description: It's cotton! Breathable, pleasant, cotton. Yep. Cotton.
item_42034_Description: Inlaid with beautiful silver filigree, this cozy shirt looks gorgeous. A universal holy symbol is embroidered inside, allowing priests of Arisetsu, Saledar, or Kirad to enforce their wills.
item_42035_Description: This tough leather armor is interwoven with magical threads that supposedly keep psionic attacks at bay.
item_42036_Description: When you don this strange metal suit, you can feel your sense of empathy and community diminish. All the better to kill without remorse!
item_42037_Description: When the dwarves were holed up under Serbule so many centuries ago, they didn't have a lot to do but make really resilient armor. The resiliency magic bleeds over to the wearer.
item_42038_Description: Makeshift Summer armor. It grants empathy to the wearer, though only those who are attuned to such things will notice.
item_42039_Description: Designed by a spiteful necromancer bent on conquering a city, these robes seethe with hatred. And it turns out that pure hatred is pretty warm, providing mild protection against the cold.
item_4203_Description: For healing serious injuries. Used by First Aid 3 (and other abilities).
item_42040_Description: When thousands of Rakshasa warriors arrived on Alharth, they wore suits of armor like this, designed to accommodate all environments. The secret to their crafting is lost to the Rakshasa living here now.
item_42041_Description: The rings of this chain suit are ridiculously thick, resulting in a very protective garment that is pretty ugly -- and very conductive of cold and electricity.
item_42042_Description: Pretty shoddy workmanship... but hey, it's armor.
item_42043_Description: This simple platemail has sufficient protection, but it could do with more padding. Very uncomfortable!
item_42044_Description: Strong armor with a strong statement to make: "I like red!"
item_42045_Description: Itchy and viney, but the magic is impressively resilient.
item_42046_Description: This thick and ungainly thing was the preferred armor of the ancient Dwarven Stalwarts. It's easy to see why: this armor is very... stalwart.
item_42047_Description: A strange mechanism that can augment an arthropod's venomous attack capabilities. (Only wearable by spiders.)
item_42048_Description: Cozy and warm, with lots of fur trim.
item_42049_Description: Worn leather with metal meticulously inserted into it. Whoever made this lacked skill in leatherworking, but made up for it with time and attention to detail.
item_4204_Description: For healing debilitating injuries. Used by First Aid 4 (and other abilities).
item_42050_Description: This is the latest masterpiece of rakshasa leatherworking, designed to replace the old Ravana armor they can no longer create. In many ways, this surpasses anything from their homeworld, a testament to rakshasa skill... and the toughness of beasts in Alharth.
item_42051_Description: Thick matted hair combined with spider silk. It's kind of sticky, making it a little too form-fitting. It's surprisingly durable, though!
item_42052_Description: Pretty shoddy workmanship... but hey, it's armor.
item_42053_Description: The isolated elf colony of Sedgewick Forest prefers armor of this type because it reminds them of their home in Verta. It's been explained to them that nobody in Verta dresses this way anymore, but they don't seem to care. A symbol of Arisetsu is painted onto the front.
item_42054_Description: When you don this strange metal suit, you can feel your sense of empathy and community diminish. All the better to kill without remorse!
item_42054_Name: Great Coat of the Winter Court
item_42055_Description: Used by the typical troops of the Crone Hegemony.
item_42056_Description: It's gotten a bit frayed from use, but it's still thick and warm.
item_42057_Description: Made of giant beetle and scorpion carapace, this stifling, awkward, and heavy coat rewards those who have the fortitude to endure wearing it.
item_42058_Description: The general seems to have customized this Scorpiclaw suit to his own strange needs: there's a hidden compartment inside.
item_42059_Description: The preferred armor of goblin tribes on serious budget crunches.
item_4205_Description: For healing devastating injuries. Used by First Aid 5 (and other abilities).
item_42060_Description: Sewn by an expert tailor in Rahu, this gown is lovely, but apparently not lovely enough, since it was discarded.
item_42061_Description: It's seen years of freezing cold weather, digging in the dirt, and horrific violence. It's surprisingly clean for all that.
item_42062_Description: This armor is feared in any land where orcs are known.
item_42063_Description: Worn by orcish mages after they've been promoted to 'Magic Trooper', which is the highest rank they can expect to reach, given mages' glass ceiling in orcish society.
item_42064_Description: For the mage who needs to clear a sewer in the morning and dance at a ball in the evening.
item_42065_Description: This red-tinted armor is worn by Gazluk City's front-line soldiers, known as "Death Troopers". Their armor enhances their strength and melee prowess.
item_42066_Description: This blue-tinted armor is worn by Gazluk City's support soldiers, known as "Misery Troopers". Their armor is strong while also providing survival protection in harsh weather.
item_42067_Description: This green-tinted armor is worn by Gazluk City's gadgeteers and tacticians, known as "Treachery Troopers". Their armor protects them from battlefield conditions and enhances their tricky combat techniques.
item_42068_Description: This gold-tinted armor is worn by the highest battlefield officers in Gazluk City's army. It is versatile and strong, like its wearer.
item_42069_Description: This cloth armor is worn by the few Gazluk wizards who have become officers. It is designed to aid powerful spellcasters.
item_4206_Description: For healing heinous injuries. Used by First Aid 6 and 7 (and other abilities).
item_42070_Description: The terrifying orcish cult known as the Mutterers wear these garments. This piece is fitted with a Necromancy Gem.
item_42071_Description: The terrifying orcish cult known as the Mutterers wear these garments.
item_42073_Description: These coats were a gift from the Crone Hegemony to Gob-land guerillas who were trying to free enslaved ratkin. Since the Hegemony is not against slavery in general, it's generally assumed they were just trying to stir up disharmony in the Gob-lands... but perhaps there was an ulterior motive. Anyway, they're still in pretty good shape a decade later.
item_42074_Description: Created by the orcs of Gazluk as part of a short-lived coalition with the neighboring Council Lands. The "Pathfinders of Kur" initiative would have been a mixed-race team of orcs and humans that patrolled and mapped the vast mountain range. Unfortunately one of the human diplomats to Kur drunkenly revealed that the Council cared little about Kur itself, but mostly wanted this opportunity to work cooperatively with the orcs and perhaps build comeraderie. The orcs perceived it as "manipulative human trickery", killed the diplomat, and called the alliance off. But the armor had already been delivered. It's pretty good.
item_42075_Description: Extraordinarily light armor that is very cold to the touch.
item_42076_Description: When you don this strange glowing metal suit, you can feel your sense of empathy and community diminish. All the better to kill without remorse!
item_42077_Description: Expertly made out of a barghest, or something similar.
item_42078_Description: These massive seashells have been reinforced with a thin layer of wood, making for uncomfortable but effective armor. (Includes random level 35 enchantments.)
item_42079_Description: These massive seashells have been reinforced with a thin layer of wood, making for uncomfortable but effective armor. (Includes random level 40 enchantments.)
item_4207_Description: For healing devastating injuries. Used by First Aid 5 (and other abilities).
item_42080_Description: These massive seashells have been reinforced with a thin layer of wood, making for uncomfortable but effective armor. (Includes random level 45 enchantments.)
item_42082_Description: Skintight clothing worn by ranalon. It's remarkably stretchy.
item_42083_Description: Skintight clothing worn by ranalon. It's remarkably stretchy.
item_42084_Description: Imbued with potent magic, the ever-patching shirt is constantly fixing itself.
item_42085_Description: Made from a particularly well-bred scray, this fleshy coat has predictable odor problems but is nonetheless quite impressive.
item_42086_Description: A sort of jumpsuit worn by fae officers on their strange ships. Its strange material is very stretchy.
item_42087_Description: An expert armor seller decided this coat was special.
item_42088_Description: Crafted by skilled rakshasa armorsmiths prior to the desolation of Povus.
item_42089_Description: A torso-shaped piece of fungal "flesh" from a fungus-man.
item_4208_Description: For healing heinous injuries. Used by First Aid 6 and 7 (and other abilities).
item_42090_Description: A goblin military coat that's been fitted for a hobgoblin, an extra-large, extra-stupid goblin.
item_42091_Description: Brand new, but of an old dwarven design. The lack of fire-retardant is a problem with this sort of gear, but otherwise it's quite impressive.
item_42092_Description: This coat is a tight squeeze for larger races. It smells faintly of urine, and no amount of cleaning seems to fix it.
item_42093_Description: This exquisite piece was made by priests of Umrad. Rumors once abounded that the god Umrad himself gave them the design, but Umrad has denied this (and found the idea offensive for several reasons).
item_42094_Description: It doesn't SEEM like it's made from the hides of prarie dogs that wre sacrificed to Urtigal in a profane ceremony centuries ago... (But it is.)
item_42095_Description: Made from a particularly well-bred scray, this fleshy coat has predictable odor problems but is nonetheless quite impressive.
item_42096_Description: Thorians were elves that followed Romast, the god of Martial Arts, who taught that being one with nature was key to grace. That's why their armor was made of metal: nothing's more natural than metal.
item_42097_Description: This gross shirt still smells of urine. It continues to provide no armor protection, and is even itchier than before. But it does cause enemies to perceive you as less of a threat.
item_42098_Description: This gross shirt still smells of urine. It continues to provide no armor protection, and is even itchier than before. But it does cause enemies to perceive you as less of a threat.
item_42099_Description: An invisible shirt, perfect for the budding exhibitionist. Useful in glamouring.
item_4209_Description: For healing horrific injuries. Used by First Aid 8 and 9 (and other abilities).
item_42100_Description: This armpits of coat emit an acrid odor whenever the wearer is in combat, purportedly smelling "like a goblin", though goblins would fervently deny this. The odor is noxious but also somewhat protective.
item_42101_Description: Poor-quality leather has been crudely woven into a fairly repulsive shirt. It's essentially made of garbage.
item_42102_Description: A novice-quality piece of work, with embarrassingly wide stitches.
item_42103_Description: Not nice to look at, but its got it where it counts. Which is to say the chest region.
item_42105_Description: This is a pro-quality piece of equipment! The stitching isn't even visible from the outside. Fancy.
item_42106_Description: This looks great! Very professional. And moderately comfortable, too!
item_42107_Description: A soft leather vest with thick reinforced sleeves. Who knew wearing ten pounds of leather could be this not-horrible?
item_42108_Description: This leather armor is so comfortable, it's almost criminal.
item_42109_Description: This is a masterpiece. It's got little hidden straps and buckles to make sure you can fit it perfectly to any form.
item_4210_Description: For healing hideous injuries. Used by First Aid 8 and 9 (and other abilities).
item_42110_Description: Wow! Awesome! You could sleep in this baby, it's so comfortable.
item_42111_Description: Incredible crafstmanship and a fashion-forward view on pockets. (That is: not having any.)
item_42112_Description: A marvelous shirt made almost wholly of leather that's soft yet rugged, protective yet breathable. Modern, yet made for the years to come.
item_42144_Description: Extra insulated to protect against winds.
item_42145_Description: Cozy and warm, with lots of fur trim.
item_42146_Description: A professional-looking winter coat with heavy insulation.
item_42147_Description: When winter comes and you need a great coat, now you have one. During summer, though, this is a "nice" coat. At best.
item_42148_Description: When winter comes and you need an amazing coat, now you have one. During summer, though, this is a "quality" coat. At best.
item_42149_Description: When winter comes and you need an astounding coat, now you have one. During summer, though, this is an "amazing" coat. At best.
item_42150_Description: When winter comes and you need an astounding coat, now you have one. During summer, though, this is an "amazing" coat. At best.
item_42151_Description: When winter comes and you need an astounding coat, now you have one. During summer, though, this is an "amazing" coat. At best.

item_42163_Description: This crumpled leather shirt can't decide whether it wants to be nimble or protective, instead failing at both.
item_42164_Description: This is incredibly light leather chest armor. You can really move around in this baby. Pity about the stitching. And the color. And the design.
item_42165_Description: You feel nice wearing a leather shirt like this. Nice enough to be able to dodge arrows.
item_42166_Description: A pro made this leather armor, and aside from some unusual choices in tailoring, there's not much to complain about.
item_42167_Description: Leather this strong shouldn't be this flexible. But it is.

item_42202_Description: A few years ago, the humans of Statehelm decided shirts like this were ironically in fashion. That time has come and gone, however, and now it's just hideous again.
item_42203_Description: Hm... there must be something nice to say about this shirt. Ah! The pockets are sewn on straight. Nice!
item_42204_Description: Good for lounging around the house. Where nobody will see you.
item_42205_Description: Not exactly formalwear, but not humiliating either.
item_42206_Description: Pretty high quality. Good thread count, decent coloration, not too tight. Good stuff.
item_42207_Description: Simultaneously elegant and protective. If you own only one shirt, ... then you probably can't afford this one.
item_42208_Description: Simultaneously elegant and protective. If you own only one shirt, ... then you probably can't afford this one.

item_42254_Description: People won't realize how silly your shirt is, because they won't even notice you're there! Much.
item_42255_Description: Is it a camouflaged nice shirt, or a nice camouflaged shirt? A little of both, probably.
item_42256_Description: It's hard to see, which is good for a camouflaged shirt.
item_42257_Description: This is the sort of shirt a masterful guerilla elf might wear. Not that you'd ever see them wearing it, of course, but that's the point.

item_42701_Description: Made by someone with potent magics and very poor knitting skills, this coat is also unfortunately woven of dog hair and is rather uncomfortable. An inscription inside says "K2togYO"
item_42702_Description: Drab and very old, this robe was once cloth but is now held together by load-bearing cobwebs and magic. Elaborate necromantic symbols have been inscribed inside; the dye of the symbols is long-faded by they can still be detected by the resonant angst one feels when touching the inside of the garment. A slightly newer inscription inside says "Protect your sanity lest you succumb to the hatred."
item_42703_Description: While many bards lamented Tennenbrandt University's shift away from music and toward combat magic, Lostt reveled in a newfound spotlight, for his music was known across many realms as being offensively bad.
item_43001_Description: These are so slimy, wearing them is like sticking dozens of leeches to your hands.
item_43002_Description: Wow, off-red utility gloves. Nice. I guess.
item_43003_Description: The padding is worn through in all the right places, making it easy to fight in these disgusting gloves.
item_43007_Description: Who doesn't love steel plate armor on your hands? Well... besides wizards?
item_43008_Description: Protects your hands so well that you can even punch harder than before.
item_43009_Description: These gloves have a quality, and that quality is adequateness.
item_43012_Description: Great for punching! Also, somehow makes your kicks hurt more, too... weird.
item_43013_Description: Popular among the guards of the Council, these gloves are lined with lead to protect against mind control. Of course, the lead would be more effective if it was on your head instead of your hands, but nobody really wants a bunch of lead on their head.
item_43014_Description: Thick smelly gloves that have been through a few fights
item_43015_Description: Light, delicate gloves that seem far more fragile than they really are.
item_43016_Description: Dainty gloves made of a stretchy material. They could fit any sized hands, but were made for a child.
item_43017_Description: Insulated gloves for the most demanding of environments.
item_43018_Description: Well, there's no name inscribed on them or anything. But you could give them a name if you wanted! The symbol of some god is etched on the inside.
item_4301_Description: For repairing minor armor damage. Used by Armor Patching abilities.
item_43021_Description: Instead of wearing these, why not just strap some dead animals to your hands? That would be more stylish, more convenient, and less smelly.
item_43022_Description: Although clearly intended to be gloves, these are little more than wads of leather in vaguely-hand-shaped form. Expect immense amounts of palm sweat!
item_43023_Description: The craftsmanship of these gloves is adequate, but the material is poor, resulting in a functional pair of gloves that aren't much fun to wear.
item_43024_Description: These still get insanely hot and sweaty when worn, but at least they're kind of fashionable. Kind of.
item_43025_Description: Made of reasonable materials, these gloves are nice. Just nice.
item_43026_Description: These gloves were made by someone with a keen sense of anatomy, allowing a full range of motion.
item_43027_Description: Pleasant to wear and reasonably protective.
item_43028_Description: These soft elegant gloves have custom gripping surfaces to make sure you never slip up in combat.
item_43029_Description: These gloves are so light, yet so protective! Astonishing craftsmanship.
item_4302_Description: For repairing moderate armor damage. Used by Armor Patching abilities.
item_43030_Description: They're as comfortable as not wearing any gloves, and as protective as wearing plate metal on your hands.
item_43031_Description: Designed for orcish scouts and mobile assault troops, these gloves are patterned after the famous orcish plate gauntlets, but just made out of many layers of leather. Nobody really loves the result, but it's still better than steel gauntlets if you're trying to aim a bow.
item_43032_Description: These freezing-cold gloves are hard to don, but once worn, they reveal themselves to be remarkably dextrous.
item_43033_Description: Oversized gloves were the style hundreds of years ago, which is why these dwarven gauntlets can fit your hands.
item_43034_Description: Oddly warm to the touch, even in cold weather, these makeshift Summer Court gloves protect from frostbite.
item_43035_Description: These super-thick chainmail gloves are used by orcish arena fighters. They're uncomfortable and ugly, but very thick.
item_43036_Description: Wow, off-red utility gloves. Nice. I guess.
item_43038_Description: These gloves have a quality, and that quality is adequateness.
item_43039_Description: Magic gauntlets that were in a giant spider's hoard for decades, taking some of the spider's aspect.
item_4303_Description: For repairing serious armor damage. Used by Armor Patching abilities.
item_43040_Description: The Dwarven Stalwarts were hammer-lovers to a fault, and these thick gauntlets were engineered with hammers in mind.
item_43041_Description: The isolated elf colony of Sedgewick Forest prefers armor of this type because it reminds them of their home in Verta. It's been explained to them that nobody in Verta dresses this way anymore, but they don't seem to care.
item_43042_Description: These freezing-cold gloves are hard to don, but once worn, they reveal themselves to be remarkably dextrous.
item_43042_Name: Great Gauntlets of the Winter Court
item_43043_Description: Insulated gloves for the most demanding of environments.
item_43044_Description: Used by the typical troops of the Crone Hegemony.
item_43045_Description: Thick smelly gloves that have been through a few fights
item_4304_Description: For repairing debilitating armor damage. Used by Armor Patching abilities.
item_43056_Description: When goblin tribes suffer from high turnover and budget shortfalls, they often have to make do with armor like this.
item_43057_Description: Caked in frozen blood, these gloves always seem to be damp and smelly no matter what you do to clean them.
item_43058_Description: Horrendously strong, but not especially deft.
item_43059_Description: Worn by orcish mages after they've been promoted to 'Magic Trooper', which is the highest rank they can expect to reach, given mages' glass ceiling in orcish society.
item_4305_Description: For repairing heinous armor damage. Used by Armor Patching abilities.
item_43060_Description: Designed for ice mages in hostile environments, these gloves try to be the perfect blend of protection and versatility.
item_43061_Description: This red-tinted armor is worn by Gazluk City's front-line soldiers, known as "Death Troopers". Their armor enhances their strength and melee prowess.
item_43062_Description: This blue-tinted armor is worn by Gazluk City's support soldiers, known as "Misery Troopers". Their armor is strong while also providing survival protection in harsh weather.
item_43063_Description: This green-tinted armor is worn by Gazluk City's gadgeteers and tacticians, known as "Treachery Troopers". Their armor protects them from battlefield conditions and enhances their tricky combat techniques.
item_43064_Description: This gold-tinted armor is worn by the highest battlefield officers in Gazluk City's army. It is versatile and strong, like its wearer.
item_43065_Description: This cloth armor is worn by the few Gazluk wizards who have become officers. It is designed to aid powerful spellcasters.
item_43066_Description: The terrifying orcish cult known as the Mutterers wear these garments.
item_43067_Description: The terrifying orcish cult known as the Mutterers wear these garments.
item_43068_Description: These were designed for the ratkin by the Crone Hegemony. The crones underestimated how long ratkin fingernails grow, and these gloves are now pretty holey. Still good for punching though.
item_43069_Description: While decades old, these gloves have never been worn in combat. They were crafted for "all-weather exploration", but to the orcs that made them, that mostly meant "cold-weather exploration".
item_4306_Description: For repairing incredible armor damage. Used by Armor Patching abilities.
item_43070_Description: These freezing-cold gloves are hard to don, but once worn, they reveal themselves to be remarkably dextrous.
item_43071_Description: These glowing metal gloves are hard to don, but once worn, they reveal themselves to be remarkably dextrous.
item_43072_Description: Inlaid with intricate patterns that are incredibly precise. The palms have tiny quick-access pockets, too!
item_43073_Description: These gloves are made of woven fishgut and then adorned with strong shells. Odd, but wearable. Includes a built-in utility knife for opening oysters. (Includes random level 35 enchantments.)
item_43074_Description: These gloves are made of woven fishgut and then adorned with strong shells. Odd, but wearable. Includes a built-in utility knife for opening oysters. (Includes random level 40 enchantments.)
item_43075_Description: These gloves are made of woven fishgut and then adorned with strong shells. Odd, but wearable. Includes a built-in utility knife for opening oysters. (Includes random level 45 enchantments.)
item_43077_Description: Tight dried-kelp gloves worn by ranalon. They're a little itchy.
item_43078_Description: Tight dried-kelp gloves worn by ranalon. They're a little itchy.
item_43079_Description: Protects your hands so well that you can even punch harder than before.
item_4307_Description: For repairing devastating armor damage. Used by Armor Patching abilities.
item_43080_Description: Part of the uniform worn on fae ships. The stretchy gloves seem especially designed for precision work.
item_43081_Description: These old Magic Trooper gloves have been patched many times. But each repair was done with such care that it actually made the gloves stronger overall.
item_43082_Description: Chain gloves made by recently-deceased rakshasa armorsmiths.
item_43083_Description: The fleshy part of a fungus-man's hand, worn as a glove.
item_43084_Description: Extra-tough gloves for hobgoblins, specially grown battle-goblins with keen combat senses and little common sense.
item_43085_Description: Definitely dwarven in design, but relatively dainty for dwarven gear.
item_43086_Description: Magical gloves used by nobles who don't want to accidentally misspell words: these gloves prevent such simple errors. They improve accuracy in other ways, too. Unfortunately, this pair is stained and gross.
item_43087_Description: Gauntlet-style gloves, but maneuverable enough for casting work.
item_43088_Description: Reinforced leather gloves for heavy-duty work.
item_43089_Description: Dainty gloves made of a stretchy material. They could fit any sized hands, but were made for a child.
item_4308_Description: For repairing heinous armor damage. Used by Armor Patching abilities.
item_43090_Description: While there are still-extant offshoots of the Thorians, such as the Thentree elves and the Seekers of Marakir, only the actual Thorians made gloves like these! The weighting is pretty uncomfortable.
item_43091_Description: A pair of invisible gloves, for protecting your hands while still showing them off. Useful in glamouring.
item_43092_Description: They're as comfortable as not wearing any gloves, and as protective as wearing plate metal on your hands.
item_43093_Description: They're as comfortable as not wearing any gloves, and as protective as wearing plate metal on your hands.
item_43101_Description: Warm and cozy without getting in the way of violence.
item_43102_Description: An attempt at perfect harmony: metal outside, wool inside. Unfortunately, the gloves get really sweaty and are prone to fungal infestation. Ew.
item_43103_Description: The goblin-trained bear Graz was outfitted with these after he killed one too many overzealous goblin mages. (Only animals can wear these.)
item_43104_Description: The legacy is one of rusting quietly in a cave for hundreds of years. A faded symbol of Arisetsu makes the gloves slightly warm to the touch.
item_43105_Description: Decades of spiderwebs have made the joints hard to open and close. It's hard to make magical gestures while wearing them, but on the bright side, they're silky smooth inside.
item_43106_Description: Lost in a cave and worn by generations of insane werewolves, these well-made gauntlets have become infused with lycanthropic power.
item_43108_Description: These were once used in ceremonies, not combat, but they turned out to work pretty well there too, as long as you're not trying to do anything too terribly dextrous.
item_43109_Description: Designed by a spiteful necromancer bent on conquering a city, these cloth gloves seethe with hatred. And it turns out that pure hatred is pretty warm, providing mild protection against the cold.
item_43110_Description: When thousands of Rakshasa warriors arrived on Alharth, they wore suits of armor like this, designed to accommodate all environments. The secret to their crafting is lost to the Rakshasa living here now.
item_43111_Description: Thick smelly gloves that the goblin Jumjab augmented. A Necromancy gem has been embedded into the right palm.
item_43112_Description: Worn leather with metal meticulously inserted into it. Whoever made this lacked skill in leatherworking, but made up for it with time and attention to detail.
item_43113_Description: This is the latest masterpiece of rakshasa leatherworking, designed to replace the old Ravana armor they can no longer create. In many ways, this surpasses anything from their homeworld, a testament to rakshasa skill... and the toughness of beasts in Alharth.
item_43115_Description: Thick matted hair combined with spider silk. These feel great. No, really. They don't look great, but they feel great. An ancient holy symbol has been inscribed inside.
item_43116_Description: Wow, off-red utility gloves. Nice. I guess.
item_43117_Description: Warmth is the key goal here. They're a little clumsy, but not enough to complain about.
item_43118_Description: Made of immaculately-carved pieces of giant insect carapace, these are a work of mechanical art. Heavy and prone to painful pinching, they are tolerable only to hardened warriors.
item_43119_Description: These scorpiclaw gauntlets were personally adjusted by the revered general. They are denser and yet more agile than one would expect.
item_43120_Description: Worn to high-society functions by the elite of Rahu, these lovely gloves are also fairly comfortable.
item_43121_Description: Made of snail shells and sinew. The sinews were not properly cured and stink beyond comprehension.

item_44003_Description: A thick layer of mold lines the inside, helping to seal the helmet to your head.
item_44008_Description: The finest in slave-made head protection.
item_44011_Description: Named by a bard who was having a bad day, this jaunty hat is actually pretty despair-free. A symbol of Arisetsu was added as a tassle by a later owner.
item_44012_Description: Knights love this sort of thing. Hope they don't go out in a thunderstorm though...
item_44014_Description: A fairly basic helmet with some unnecessary stylistic flourishes.
item_44015_Description: Truth in advertising: this is not a pretty helmet. It has other charms, though, such as a deep-seated aroma of spoiled cheese.
item_44016_Description: This hat was stuck to a spider for years. There's so much webbing that you can't get all of it off, and it'll keep falling in your face while you try to murder things.
item_44017_Description: A cotton hat that's good in the cold.
item_44018_Description: A horrific cotton cap. High quality in every way, but just... ugly.
item_44019_Description: For orcs that rely on speed and stealth to survive, this leather helmet is the standard. It's not really the best possible cap, but standards usually aren't.
item_4401_Description: A simple skinning knife for beginning skinners. (Keep it in your inventory and you'll be given the option to skin the corpses of some enemies.)
item_44020_Description: Ceremonial armor refitted for battlefield use, this helm looks sharp -- and can make the wearer look sharp, too, provided they know how to wear it with style.
item_44021_Description: While vaguely protective, this lopsided helmet is so unbalancing that you're almost better off just going into combat without a helm at all.
item_44022_Description: Designed to protect from concussions and minor scrapes, this helmet has the serious drawbacks of reduced visibility, unpleasant odor, and terrible craftsmanship.
item_44023_Description: While the craftsmanship is adequate, the materials are not, and this helmet looks more like a dead beaver than a helm.
item_44024_Description: A well-made helmet that will keep you alive in combat. But you'll sweat profusely in it, and you don't want to know what it's going to do to your hair.
item_44025_Description: A comfy helmet that is almost (but not quite) attractive, too!
item_44026_Description: Good materials and great craftsmanship combine in a helmet that breathes easy and protects from nearly any injury.
item_44027_Description: A helmet that's stylish and also functional! Great.
item_44028_Description: This helmet goes beyond mere adequacy. It says "adequate isn't enough!" It takes it one step farther, all the way to more-than-adequate.
item_44029_Description: A hat you wouldn't be embarrassed being seen in, and so light and protective! Anti-odor components keep it smelling fresh all day, too.
item_4402_Description: A basic skinning knife for novice skinners. (+2 to your effective skill when skinning.)
item_44030_Description: A great helmet with great safety features, like extra padding to keep sweat out of your eyes and customized ear-holes to make sure you never miss a thing.

item_4403_Description: A quality skinning knife for experienced skinners. (+4 to your effective skill when skinning.)
