item_4405_Description: A perfect skinning knife for master skinners. (+8 to your effective skill when skinning.)
item_4406_Description: A perfect skinning knife for master skinners. (+10 to your effective skill when skinning.)
item_4407_Description: A perfect skinning knife for master skinners. (+12 to your effective skill when skinning.)
item_44201_Description: This bizarre helmet affects the mind of the wearer ever so slightly. Just a touch.
item_44202_Description: "Coif of Calamities" is inscribed inside, in tiny lettering. It doesn't seem very calamitous, though. Maybe it was some sort of ancient marketing campaign.
item_44203_Description: The damaged remains of an intellect-enhancement helmet.
item_44204_Description: A resilient piece of history! Perfect for the dwarf fetishist in your life. Or you could wear it in battle, if that's what you're into.
item_44205_Description: This was once a Winter Court helm that was repurposed. Instead of twisting the mind, it protects against such things.
item_44206_Description: Designed by a spiteful necromancer bent on conquering a city, this hood seethes with hatred. And it turns out that pure hatred is pretty warm, providing mild protection against the cold.
item_44207_Description: Fae blacksmiths have infused the metal of this helm with the essence of eternity, meaning that this helm has never not existed. Don't think about it too much.
item_44208_Description: When thousands of Rakshasa warriors arrived on Alharth, they wore suits of armor like this, designed to accommodate all environments. The secret to their crafting is lost to the Rakshasa living here now.
item_44209_Description: This ugly leather thing is supposed to be worn on the head, apparently.
item_44214_Description: The face of a big snail has been made into a makeshift helmet.
item_44215_Description: It's a tough helmet that was once used by the elite Dwarven Stalwarts. But that was many years ago, and more recent wearers have not been big on armor maintenance. It's still a great helm, but you imagine it was once even more impressive.
item_44216_Description: Worn leather with metal meticulously inserted into it. Whoever made this lacked skill in leatherworking, but made up for it with time and attention to detail.
item_44217_Description: This is the latest masterpiece of rakshasa leatherworking, designed to replace the old Ravana armor they can no longer create. In many ways, this surpasses anything from their homeworld, a testament to rakshasa skill... and the toughness of beasts in Alharth.
item_44218_Description: Thick matted hair combined with spider silk. The silk gets all in your hair and eyes and is rather uncomfortable, but it's really warm.
item_44220_Description: The isolated elf colony of Sedgewick Forest prefers armor of this type because it reminds them of their home in Verta. It's been explained to them that nobody in Verta dresses this way anymore, but they don't seem to care.
item_44221_Description: Made of snail shells and sinew. The sinews were not properly cured and stink beyond comprehension.
item_44222_Description: Made of snail shells and sinew. Only somewhat stinky.
item_44223_Description: Made of snail shells and sinew.
item_44224_Description: Made of snail shells and sinew.
item_44225_Description: Made of snail shells, sinews, and animal claws.
item_44226_Description: Made of snail shells and sinew.
item_44227_Description: Made of snail shells and sinew.
item_44228_Description: Made of snail shells and sinew.
item_44229_Description: Made of snail shells and sinew.
item_44241_Description: Hammered together from simple materials, for a simple battle cow.
item_44242_Description: A better-made helmet for a better-made bovine.
item_44243_Description: Hammered and polished and ready for battle.
item_44244_Description: Hammered and polished and ready for battle.
item_44245_Description: Hammered and polished and ready for battle.
item_44246_Description: Hammered and polished and ready for battle.
item_44247_Description: Hammered and polished and ready for battle.
item_44248_Description: Hammered and polished and ready for battle.
item_44249_Description: Hammered and polished and ready for battle.
item_44261_Description: It's metal. It fits on a werewolf. These are the only positive attributes.
item_44262_Description: It's metal. It fits on a werewolf. These are the only positive attributes.
item_44263_Description: It's metal. It fits on a werewolf. These are the only positive attributes.
item_44264_Description: A reasonable-looking metal helm for a werewolf.
item_44282_Description: A cowl of the quality that a peasant farmer might wear.
item_44283_Description: A tough head covering. Ugly, but practical.
item_44284_Description: A head covering that begins to combine fashion sense with survival sense.
item_44286_Description: A cowl that makes the wearer seem more mysterious and aloof.
item_44287_Description: A cowl that makes the wearer seem more mysterious and aloof.
item_44288_Description: A cowl that makes the wearer seem more mysterious and aloof.
item_44289_Description: A cowl that makes the wearer seem more mysterious and aloof.
item_44290_Description: A cowl that makes the wearer seem more mysterious and aloof.
item_44291_Description: A cowl that makes the wearer seem more mysterious and aloof.
item_44292_Description: A cowl that makes the wearer seem more mysterious and aloof.
item_44301_Description: This bizarre helmet affects the mind of the wearer ever so slightly. Just a touch.
item_44301_Name: Great Helm of the Winter Court
item_44302_Description: A blob of organic sentient tissue that you can kind of fit onto your head!
item_44303_Description: Used by the typical troops of the Crone Hegemony.
item_44304_Description: A garnet that floats around your head and emits light. This was obviously made by an apprentice still learning the craft. The enchantment is clumsy and the gem wobbles slightly in its orbit.
item_44305_Description: Warm, warm, warm. A little smelly. But warm!
item_44306_Description: A freakish mask crafted by an unliving malevolence.
item_44307_Description: A garnet that floats around your head and emits light. Not especially well-made, but it's still vaguely impressive.
item_44308_Description: This magic garnet can float around your head and emit light. It also emanates hard-won wisdom, and can whisper warnings and advice when you're about to do something stupid.
item_44309_Description: A terrifying visage made of hardened scorpion sinews backed by reflective beetle carapaces.
item_44310_Description: The Onkara leader claimed to wear this mask even when resting, often saying that a true warrior could never be unprepared.
item_44311_Description: A subpar goblin helm, dented and worn.
item_44312_Description: Perfect for the pig that wishes it were something else. A symbol of Arisetsu has been hidden inside.
item_44313_Description: This clever costume is perfect for cows that need to go undercover. A symbol of Arisetsu has been hidden inside.
item_44314_Description: Deer can use this handy disguise to appear less conspicuous. A symbol of Arisetsu has been hidden inside.
item_44315_Description: A large shell has been lined with sharkskin and netting. It takes some practice to fight with this on!  (Includes random level 35 enchantments.)
item_44316_Description: A large shell has been lined with sharkskin and netting. It takes some practice to fight with this on!  (Includes random level 40 enchantments.)
item_44317_Description: A large shell has been lined with sharkskin and netting. It takes some practice to fight with this on!  (Includes random level 45 enchantments.)
item_44318_Description: This tiny gourd floats around your head and watches what you do.
item_44319_Description: This tiny gourd floats around your head and emits evil light.
item_4431_Description: A versatile knife for skinning or butchering. (Keep it in your inventory and you'll be given the option to skin and/or butcher the corpses of some enemies.)
item_44320_Description: Where did all these bugs come from?
item_44321_Description: A helmet sized for a large sow, with a simple but extremely durable design. It's hard to fathom why this exists, but it does.
item_44322_Description: This dwarven-made pig helm was no doubt worn in an illicit pig-fighting competition. The powerful combat runes are carefully hidden on the inside of the band.
item_44323_Description: The horn of a deceased unicorn. Alicorns have many medicinal uses, but in the right hooves, they have formidable combat uses as well. This particular alicorn looks like it would fit on a deer's head quite nicely, bonding to the skull and enhancing their abilities.
item_44324_Description: This oversized hat was created by fairy magic for an unknown purpose. It's too large to fit on a human's head, but it would fit a cow's head perfectly.
item_44325_Description: The horn of a unicorn, enhanced with some sort of dark metal. It looks like it would fit on a deer's head, bonding to the skull and enhancing their abilities.
item_44327_Description: Sewn by Eveline Rastin for a friend.
item_44328_Description: Once used by ratkin guerillas, these cowls were later repurposed for cheese-storage, then un-purposed for that and re-repurposed as armor. As a result they smell a lot like cheese, which the ratkin consider a feature.
item_44329_Description: Foul-smelling and poorly-maintained, yet quite dangerous. Like ratkin homes.
item_4432_Description: A versatile knife for skinning, butchering, oyster-opening, or skull extraction. (Keep it in your inventory and you'll be given the option to skin, butcher, shuck, and/or extract the skull of some enemies.)
item_44330_Description: After his brother burned to death, Vagreef spent years mastering Fire Magic to create suits of fire-resistance armor. He planned to manufacture these suits for his entire gang of bandits, but he only managed to make one prototype suit before he was murdered. This was his helmet.
item_44331_Description: This helmet was crafted by orcs, but was intended to be worn by both orcs and humans. The quality is excellent, but the most interesting feature is definitely the custom ear guard: the creators apparently believed that human ears are especially vulnerable, so they provided special removable padding to give them extra security. No-one knows why the orcs felt this was necessary, and the decision is somewhat troubling.
item_44332_Description: Apparently intended to be a gift to Ursula's mother, this cowl is very poorly made and offers no real protection. It has been enchanted with an apprentice-level spell that would normally not be very effective, but the enchantment has been repeated thousands of times over many years, and this has somehow cohered into a decent level of magic.
item_44333_Description: Although it would appear to leave the wearer completely blind, the magical helmet allows perfect visibility.
item_44334_Description: This bizarre glowing helmet affects the mind of the wearer ever so slightly. Just a touch.
item_44335_Description: This hat was digested by something unnatural, and now it exists only in the abstract.
item_44336_Description: Made of fae mollusc shell, a notoriously difficult material to obtain.
item_44337_Description: An ancient alicorn that's been fitted into a harness for a deer to wear. It's adorned with weird citrus-fruit iconography, which contrasts strangely with the extraordinarily sharp horn.
item_44338_Description: Flexible kelp material worn by ranalon. It smells exactly like you'd expect.
item_44339_Description: Flexible kelp material worn by ranalon. It smells exactly like you'd expect.
item_4433_Description: A bulky knife designed to pry oysters open. (Keep in your inventory to allow oyster-opening. Also provides a 7% chance for a bonus pearl.)
item_44340_Description: Oversized headgear worn by a supernaturally large skeleton. Various runes and a necromancy gem seem to empower it.
item_44341_Description: A device that floats around the wearer's head and conveys critical information during battle.
item_44342_Description: The perfect hat for goblin chiefs: it has protection from loud noise, a secret compartment to stash your bribe money, and extra weight to make your headbutts really impressive.
item_44343_Description: Carefully cut from the fungal "flesh" of a dead fungus-man.
item_44344_Description: Magically tough. With variable-sizing latches because hobgoblin head sizes differ pretty dramatically from one batch to the next.
item_44345_Description: While doubtlessly dwarven in design, this helmet seems light. There is extra insulation against cold.
item_44346_Description: A freakish mask crafted by an unliving malevolence.
item_44347_Description: The face of a big snail has been made into a makeshift helmet.
item_44348_Description: The Thorians disappeared after their god, Romast, died, thousands of years ago. It's extraordinary to find a working piece of Thorian headgear like this!
item_44349_Description: A small sentient gem that takes notes on your use of fire magic.
item_4434_Description: A simple skinning knife made of magical ice. (Keep it in your inventory and you'll be given the option to skin the corpses of some enemies.)
item_44350_Description: A small sentient gem that takes notes on your use of fire magic and automatically adjusts your spells to be more effective and protective.
item_4435_Description: A multi-tool embossed with the face of a smiling cartoon elf. The skinning and butchering tools seems decent, but the other tools would break if you tried to use them. (Keep in your inventory to allow skinning and butchering of corpses. +3 to your effective skinning/butchering skill.)
item_44406_Description: It's a high-quality hat, although it seems unlikely that an average cow would appreciate the style.
item_44407_Description: It's a high-quality hat, although it seems unlikely that an average cow would appreciate the style.
item_44408_Description: It's a high-quality hat, although it seems unlikely that an average cow would appreciate the style.

item_44412_Description: It's a high-quality hat, although it seems unlikely that an average cow would appreciate the style.
item_44426_Description: It's a high-quality hat, sized to fit snugly around a pig's ears.
item_44427_Description: It's a high-quality hat, sized to fit snugly around a pig's ears.
item_44428_Description: It's a high-quality hat, sized to fit snugly around a pig's ears.

item_44446_Description: It's a high-quality hat, sized to fit a deer for some reason.
item_44447_Description: It's a high-quality hat, sized to fit a deer for some reason.

item_44452_Description: It's a high-quality hat, sized to fit a deer for some reason.
item_44464_Description: Extra insulated to protect against winds.
item_44465_Description: Cozy and warm, with lots of fur trim.
item_44480_Description: It's a metal headband designed for a small animal. Mages uses these to control unruly familiars after Awakening them, and also to augment the animals' burgeoning magic powers. This particular control ring allows hares to use ice magic -- as long as the hare has already learned the rituals, of course.
item_44481_Description: It's a metal headband designed for a small animal. Mages uses these to control unruly familiars after Awakening them, and also to augment the animals' burgeoning magic powers. This particular control ring allows hares to use ice magic -- as long as the hare has already learned the rituals, of course.

item_44501_Description: Surprisingly clean. Orcs wash their hair more often than most would expect. While orcs don't especially enjoy bathing, they enjoy orc-lice far less.
item_44502_Description: An insulated helmet that is as frightening as it is protective.
item_44503_Description: Worn by orcish mages after they've been promoted to 'Magic Trooper', which is the highest rank they can expect to reach, given mages' glass ceiling in orcish society.
item_44504_Description: This tattered cloak would seem like an ordinary rag except for a thick Necromancy Gem carefully sewn inside.
item_44505_Description: This red-tinted armor is worn by Gazluk City's front-line soldiers, known as "Death Troopers". Their armor enhances their strength and melee prowess.
item_44506_Description: This blue-tinted armor is worn by Gazluk City's support soldiers, known as "Misery Troopers". Their armor is strong while also providing survival protection in harsh weather.
item_44507_Description: This green-tinted armor is worn by Gazluk City's gadgeteers and tacticians, known as "Treachery Troopers". Their armor protects them from battlefield conditions and enhances their tricky combat techniques.
item_44508_Description: This gold-tinted armor is worn by the highest battlefield officers in Gazluk City's army. It is versatile and strong, like its wearer.
item_44509_Description: This cloth armor is worn by the few Gazluk wizards who have become officers. It is designed to aid powerful spellcasters.
item_44510_Description: The terrifying orcish cult known as the Mutterers wear these garments.
item_44511_Description: The terrifying orcish cult known as the Mutterers wear these garments.
item_44513_Description: Traditional hat worn by humans during the winter celebration of Ri-Shin, the god of plants and trees. This version is moderately uncomfortable.
item_44514_Description: Traditional hat worn by humans during the winter celebration of Ri-Shin, the god of plants and trees. This version is made of decent materials, but doesn't fit too well.
item_44515_Description: Traditional hat worn by humans during the winter celebration of Ri-Shin, the god of plants and trees. This version is typical of the ones you'd see in Statehelm.
item_44516_Description: Traditional hat worn by humans during the winter celebration of Ri-Shin, the god of plants and trees. This version is pretty nice.
item_44517_Description: Traditional hat worn by humans during the winter celebration of Ri-Shin, the god of plants and trees. This version has a bunch of extra padding.
item_44518_Description: Traditional hat worn by humans during the winter celebration of Ri-Shin, the god of plants and trees. This version is enchanted for extra warmth.
item_44519_Description: Traditional hat worn by humans during the winter celebration of Ri-Shin, the god of plants and trees. This version is enchanted for extra warmth.
item_4451_Description: A large knife for cutting meat and organs out of corpses. (Keep it in your inventory and you'll have the option to butcher the corpses of some enemies.)
item_44520_Description: Traditional hat worn by humans during the winter celebration of Ri-Shin, the god of plants and trees. This version is enchanted for extra warmth.
item_44522_Description: This variant of the Ri-Shin hat is used by humans to differentiate teams for winter games. Vol-Mu, or Volmue, is a long-deceased god of winter. In the human telling of the myth, Ri-Shin defeated Vol-Mu to allow spring to return to the land.
item_44523_Description: This variant of the Ri-Shin hat is used by humans to differentiate teams for winter games. Vol-Mu, or Volmue, is a long-deceased god of winter. In the human telling of the myth, Ri-Shin defeated Vol-Mu to allow spring to return to the land.
item_44524_Description: This variant of the Ri-Shin hat is used by humans to differentiate teams for winter games. Vol-Mu, or Volmue, is a long-deceased god of winter. In the human telling of the myth, Ri-Shin defeated Vol-Mu to allow spring to return to the land.
item_44525_Description: This variant of the Ri-Shin hat is used by humans to differentiate teams for winter games. Vol-Mu, or Volmue, is a long-deceased god of winter. In the human telling of the myth, Ri-Shin defeated Vol-Mu to allow spring to return to the land.
item_44526_Description: This variant of the Ri-Shin hat is used by humans to differentiate teams for winter games. Vol-Mu, or Volmue, is a long-deceased god of winter. In the human telling of the myth, Ri-Shin defeated Vol-Mu to allow spring to return to the land.
item_44527_Description: This variant of the Ri-Shin hat is used by humans to differentiate teams for winter games. Vol-Mu, or Volmue, is a long-deceased god of winter. In the human telling of the myth, Ri-Shin defeated Vol-Mu to allow spring to return to the land.
item_4452_Description: A large knife for cutting meat and organs out of corpses (+5% chance for an organ). (Keep it in your inventory and you'll have the option to butcher the corpses of some enemies.)
item_44531_Description: A classic polished crown with small embedded gems. (Level 40 variety.)
item_44532_Description: A classic polished crown with small embedded gems. (Level 50 variety.)
item_44533_Description: A classic polished crown with small embedded gems. (Level 60 variety.)
item_44534_Description: A classic polished crown with small embedded gems. (Level 70 variety.)
item_44535_Description: A mass of enchanted webbing from a gigantic wind spider. It can be worn as a makeshift hat by the insane.
item_44536_Description: A classic polished crown with small embedded gems. (Level 80 variety.)
item_44537_Description: A wool hat made by a sentient sheep. It's very comfy!
item_44538_Description: These coifs used to be mass-produced for sale to Statehelm, but the attack on Povus killed most of the town's smiths.
item_44539_Description: A classic polished crown with small embedded gems. (Level 90 variety.)
item_4453_Description: A large knife for cutting meat and organs out of corpses (+5 to your effective skill when Butchering). (Keep it in your inventory and you'll have the option to butcher the corpses of some enemies.)
item_44549_Description: A slightly sentient fungal mat that fits under a psychic mantis's top hat. It can also be worn without a hat if you're uncultured.
item_4454_Description: Yogzi created this convenient butcher knife that can also extract skulls. (+3 to your effective skill when Butchering or Extracting Skulls). (Keep it in your inventory and you'll be given the option to butcher and/or extract the skull of some enemies.)
item_44550_Description: A helm made in the signature style of Tremmond Blackhammer, a once-famous dwarven armorsmith.
item_44551_Description: These attach to a cow's head, giving them glowing red horns and a hateful disposition. You must be a cow to wear this.
item_44552_Description: These lenses fit in the eyes of deer, giving them glowing red eyes. You must be a deer to wear this.
item_44553_Description: Made by dwarven forge-priests during the Elf-Orc wars.
item_44554_Description: A thick dwarven helm. Etched inside is a quote: "Where shall it be? Point me and I shall go. - Gondeem"
item_44555_Description: The unmistakable smell of fungus hangs around this damp cowl.
item_44556_Description: This style of hat is named after a cow who, in stories and tall tales, was said to be the mightiest cow ever. Truffle supposedly went on adventures and defeated demons, ghosts, and werewolves -- all while wearing this hat.
item_44557_Description: An invisible hat, or so the wearer would have you believe. Useful in glamouring.
item_4455_Description: A large knife for cutting meat and organs out of corpses (+7% chance for an organ). (Keep it in your inventory and you'll have the option to butcher the corpses of some enemies.)
item_4456_Description: A large knife for cutting meat and organs out of corpses. It's made of magical ice. (Keep it in your inventory and you'll have the option to butcher the corpses of some enemies.)
item_44601_Description: Luxuriously soft, this headgear moves with your body, preventing blind spots.
item_44602_Description: Luxuriously soft, this headgear moves with your body, preventing blind spots.

item_4472_Description: A large caliper designed for measuring the distance between two points on a skull. Used by Phrenologists.
item_4473_Description: A large caliper designed for measuring the distance between two points on a skull. Used by Phrenologists.
item_44741_Description: Traditional hat worn by priests of Ri-Shin. Treants and other intelligent plants recognize this hat, and most will refrain from attacking the wearer.
item_44742_Description: Traditional hat worn by priests of Ri-Shin. Treants and other intelligent plants recognize this hat, and most will refrain from attacking the wearer.

item_44761_Description: A sheet of interwoven chains that you can arguably place upon your head.
item_44762_Description: A shroud of metal with a face-hole.
item_44763_Description: An imperfectly weighted, terribly uncomfortable head covering.
item_44764_Description: Approximating the traditional look of a chainmail coif.
item_44765_Description: Appropriate padding and reinforcement make this coif a pleasure to wear into battle.
item_44766_Description: This coif features precision chainwork and a clever pattern that keeps the wearer's vision-lines clear.
item_44767_Description: A bit weighty due to the sheer amount of protection, but you barely notice thanks to the weighting.
item_44769_Description: The high attention to detail elevates this headgear beyond mere armor.
item_44770_Description: The high attention to detail elevates this headgear beyond mere armor.
item_44771_Description: The high attention to detail elevates this headgear beyond mere armor.
item_44772_Description: The high attention to detail elevates this headgear beyond mere armor.
item_44786_Description: Heavy but not unwieldy, this style of helmet provides extra protection against massive opponents.
item_44787_Description: Heavy but not unwieldy, this style of helmet provides extra protection against massive opponents.

item_44806_Description: More ornate than most orcish armor, this helm is worn with pride by countless elite orcs.
item_44807_Description: More ornate than most orcish armor, this helm is worn with pride by countless elite orcs.

item_44851_Description: A well-recognized hat in Spider culture. This denotes that the spider wearing it is quite friendly, and not to be feared.
item_44852_Description: A well-recognized hat in Spider culture. This denotes that the spider wearing it is quite friendly, and not to be feared.
item_44853_Description: A well-recognized hat in Spider culture. This denotes that the spider wearing it is quite friendly, and not to be feared.
item_44854_Description: A well-recognized hat in spider culture that denotes friendliness and approachability. The hat has been enhanced with powerful magic that shrinks the spider to seem less scary.
item_44855_Description: A hat worn proudly by spiders who have turned their backs on violent ways, and opted instead to embrace pacifism.
item_44856_Description: A hat worn proudly by spiders who have turned their backs on violent ways, and opted instead to embrace pacifism.
item_44857_Description: When you see a spider wearing this hat, there is no mistaking the fact that they are a party animal, through and through.
item_44858_Description: When you see a spider wearing this hat, there is no mistaking the fact that they are a party animal, through and through.
item_44859_Description: When you see a spider wearing this hat, there is no mistaking the fact that they are a party animal, through and through.
item_44860_Description: Spiders wear this hat for one reason and one reason only: to show off their impeccable sense of fashion.
item_44861_Description: Spiders wear this hat for one reason and one reason only: to show off their impeccable sense of fashion.
item_44891_Description: An esoteric glass bauble that floats around the head. It supposedly boosts psychic power.
item_44892_Description: An esoteric glass bauble that floats around the head. It supposedly boosts psychic power.
