item_45001_Description: Well, you wouldn't want to eat off this shield, that's for sure. But why would you eat off a shield? Just strap it on your arm, weirdo.
item_45003_Description: This sucker's seen some good times and some bad times. But mostly bad times, from the shield's point of view.
item_45004_Description: If this shield is so lucky, why did Marna give it away? Still, it seems pretty sturdy.
item_45005_Description: It's wood with some thin metal plating over it. Pretty sturdy, and also lightweight.
item_45006_Description: Etchings in the Goblin language roughly translate to "I Bash Really Well". It sounds better in the native tongue.
item_45007_Description: Etchings in the Goblin language roughly translate to "I Don't Care About Your Feelings". It sounds better in the native tongue.
item_45010_Description: "You'll look protected yet stylish with the latest quality shield from Helena's Quality Killsuits."
item_45011_Description: Made from a perfectly preserved Giant Snail shell.
item_45012_Description: This was once owned by a powerful shaman who protected cute woodland animals. A tiny bit of his soul resides in this shield. If you don't use it, you're throwing away his life's work!
item_45013_Description: This antique shield once had an insignia, but it's been dented and scratched beyond recognition.
item_45014_Description: "You'll look protected yet stylish with the latest quality shield from Helena's Quality Killsuits."
item_45015_Description: A thick wooden shield, etched with magical runes.
item_45016_Description: Found stuck to a giant spider, this shield has been scarred and pitted with acid. It looks pretty badass, but it's not really very protective.
item_45017_Description: Obtained from a human named Tyler, this shield seems to have been well-maintained.
item_45018_Description: It's somewhat melted around the edges, but still pretty sturdy.
item_45019_Description: A mass-produced shield for non-elite orcs.
item_4501_Description: Perfect for making maps, surveys, wills, poetry, you name it.
item_45020_Description: Although nothing special by elven standards, other races would kill for the secrets of the protective runes etched into this shield.
item_45021_Description: Robust, like dwarves. Flammable, like dwarves.
item_45022_Description: Obtained from a human named Nelson Ballard. It's not in the best condition, but it has been powerfully enchanted.
item_45024_Description: It's wood with some thin metal plating over it. Pretty sturdy, and also lightweight.
item_45025_Description: This shield was stuck to a big droach for decades. It apparently absorbed some of the droach's protective power.
item_45026_Description: The elite Stalwarts used this sort of shield, embossed with protective sigils and a symbol of Arisetsu. It's hard to find fault with the design, except that it's super heavy.
item_45027_Description: This is a fake: Ravana soldiers did not wear shields when they arrived on Alharth. This shield is a masterpiece made in the style of their armor.
item_45028_Description: Not many yetis use shields, but they aren't terrible. This one is made of super-thick wood that's been reinforced with pounds of spider silk.
item_4502_Description: Perfect for making maps, surveys, wills, poetry, you name it.
item_45030_Description: The elves of Sedgewick Forest use shields like this for some reason.
item_45031_Description: This special testing shield grants the wielder immense health.
item_45032_Description: The stock shield used by countless grunts in the Crone Hegemony.
item_45033_Description: An attractive longshield. The symbols on the back say "Lead Without Fear" in elvish.
item_45034_Description: Etchings in the Goblin language roughly translate to "I Have My Rights And I Don't Care About Yours". It sounds better in the native tongue.
item_45035_Description: A heavy shield with a strange fae magic.
item_45036_Description: A stock shield used by countless orc armies.
item_45038_Description: Etched with weird runes of distraction, this shield is especially useful at attracting opponents.
item_4503_Description: Perfect for making maps, surveys, wills, poetry, you name it.
item_45040_Description: Designed by blacksmith-in-hiding Torgan, this absurdly heavy shield can only be used by those with the most intense courage.
item_45041_Description: These impressive shields were a gift to the ratkin, designed to be light yet sturdy. The ratkin also use them as plates, hats, and for myriad other things.
item_45042_Description: Someone dreamed of this sturdy shield, then lost control of it.
item_45043_Description: Sacrificing defense for maneuverability, this shield is for experts who want to deal more damage with their shield.
item_45044_Description: Made of the exotic ocean metal Orichalcum, this shield is light, strong, and durable. (Includes random level 35 enchantments.)
item_45045_Description: Made of the exotic ocean metal Orichalcum, this shield is light, strong, and durable. (Includes random level 40 enchantments.)
item_45046_Description: Made of the exotic ocean metal Orichalcum, this shield is light, strong, and durable. (Includes random level 45 enchantments.)
item_45047_Description: It's made of ice! It will eventually melt, but it usually lasts a while.
item_45048_Description: It's made of ice! It will eventually melt, but it usually lasts a while.
item_45049_Description: Fashioned to look like ice, but significantly more protective.
item_4504_Description: Perfect for making maps, surveys, wills, poetry, you name it.
item_45050_Description: Made in Povus for sale to Statehelm, which recently found itself in need of demonic shielding.
item_45051_Description: Weighted to feel comfortable for a hobgoblin, which makes it too heavy for most other races.
item_45053_Description: A dark shield bearing the inscription "In the absence of light, darkness prevails."
item_45054_Description: This antique shield once had an insignia, but it's been dented and scratched beyond recognition.
item_45055_Description: Although nothing special by elven standards, other races would kill for the secrets of the protective runes etched into this shield.
item_45056_Description: Enhanced by prayers to Harukita, this shield radiates malice. A necromancy gem is in the center.
item_45057_Description: Named after a famous shield that was reluctantly given by Ormorek,the  god of bitterness, to his chosen champion during the Rebuke.
item_45058_Description: While it has an unfortunate racist past (invented by humans who were terrified that neighboring elves were going to mind-assault them), the shield still sees use due to its broad protective powers.
item_4505_Description: Perfect for making maps, surveys, wills, poetry, you name it.
item_4506_Description: Perfect for making maps, surveys, wills, poetry, you name it.
item_4507_Description: Perfect for making maps, surveys, wills, poetry, you name it.
item_4508_Description: Perfect for making maps, surveys, wills, poetry, you name it.
item_4551_Description: A small glass jar used to hold dye indefinitely. It is not reusable.
item_45521_Description: A poisoner's blade that's seen better days.
item_4552_Description: A clamp and a delicate hammer for turning gems into powder.
item_45533_Description: An ugly and not-especially-sharp weapon made of cold iron. Goblins use them when hunting fairies for capture.
item_45541_Description: This beautiful knife is used in the elvish custom of Surreto Silosi, where young heartbroken elves impale themselves under the oversight of a healer. This rite of passage signals their entrance into the second of three stages of elven maturity. The knife is usually kept as a memento.
item_45553_Description: This is a favorite of goblin healers, who use it as a surgical tool more often than as a weapon. A universal holy symbol attunes it to priests of Saledar, Arisetsu, or Kirad.
item_45554_Description: This style of dwarven weapon has been in use for centuries. It makes an excellent stabbing weapon but lacks versatility.
item_45561_Description: A goblin soldier's backup weapon. The hilt guard provides protection at the expense of killing power.
item_45562_Description: A magically sharp dagger with complex weighting that makes it difficult for neophytes to master. A necromancy gem has been fastened to the handle by its previous owner.
item_45571_Description: A military dagger used as a close-quarters weapon by some armies in the Crone Hegemony.
item_45573_Description: Enchanted with cold magic, this weapon is uncomfortable to hold at first, but after a while your hand goes numb and it stops being so bothersome... as long as you don't need to use your hand in combat for anything else.
item_45581_Description: The signature blade of the human assassin guild called the Shadow Keepers, this weapon design is now found across the continent. Whenever nefarious types scheme, a venomizer is likely at hand.
item_45582_Description: These excellent daggers are standard-issue for the Stalwarts, but few dwarves actually use them. They are routinely lost in gambling matches and then reported as stolen, leading to the dwarven idiom "goblins stole your dagger", which is essentially synonymous with "you have a serious gambling problem."
item_45591_Description: An ancient weapon from the War of Froliac, used by elves who were very, very angry. It is etched with an image of the elven begonia, a symbol of trust broken and secrets defiled. Unfortunately, other races don't understand the symbolism and seem to think it's kind of sissy.
item_45592_Description: Supposedly coated in blood from over 100 different sacrificed creatures, this knife emanates hatred and can lead to unwanted thoughts of murder. A necromancy gem is embedded in the hilt.
item_45601_Description: Made from a giant scorpion tail, this weapon is difficult to use deftly, but poisoners love it.
item_45602_Description: Supposedly used by the Rakshasa forces that first arrived on Alharth, this weird weapon is definitely not typical.
item_45621_Description: The freakish orcish spellcasters known as Mutterers use these ornate blades to enhance their spells.
item_45622_Description: A small but very sharp blade inlaid with ivory. The craftsmanship is very high.
item_45623_Description: The favored close-quarters weapon of orcish archers.
item_45624_Description: Fashioned after the long knife used by the Executrix of Command, the terrifying head of the Council's personal defenses. This is no match for the real Executrix's knife, but it is still an incredible weapon.
item_45625_Description: The preferred blade of elvish courtesans in non-elvish lands, this sneaky knife can be concealed almost anywhere. The hilt has symbols of Arisetsu and Errana.
item_45626_Description: An orcish chef used this to chop vegetables... and any drunk orcs that got out of line. Sharpened via an unknown technique, it cleaves through anything with ease.
item_45627_Description: Designed by blacksmith-in-hiding Torgan, this cruel knife rewards the foolish warrior who dares bring a knife to a melee.
item_45628_Description: A thin and non-descript blade that's been designed to maximize the impact of applied poisons.
item_45629_Description: A weapon whose appearance belies the skill of the ratkin crafter. Making a blade this sharp out of junk metal isn't easy.
item_45630_Description: A common backup weapon for many types of fairies.
item_45631_Description: A standard Ranalon utility knife that's also a dangerous weapon. (Acts as an Oyster Knife.)
item_45632_Description: A standard Ranalon utility knife that's also a dangerous weapon. (Acts as an Oyster Knife.) (With random level 35 enchantments.)
item_45633_Description: A standard Ranalon utility knife that's also a dangerous weapon. (Acts as an Oyster Knife.) (With random level 40 enchantments.)
item_45634_Description: A standard Ranalon utility knife that's also a dangerous weapon. (Acts as an Oyster Knife.) (With random level 45 enchantments.)
item_45635_Description: This is a favorite of goblin healers, who use it as a surgical tool more often than as a weapon. A universal holy symbol attunes it to priests of Saledar, Arisetsu, or Kirad.
item_45637_Description: When held, this weapon seems to freeze the very air around it. A weapon of elite Winter Court troops.
item_45638_Description: The hilt of this magical blade has been coated in tiny gemstones. They almost completely obscure the necromantic symbols on the pommel.
item_45639_Description: Actually made by dwarven priests in New Aufghel and rebranded by Povus smiths, who did not specialize in weaponry.
item_45640_Description: Wielded by the mad assassin Nopec who wished to bring about Endless Nights in the name of Fenna, goddess of Dusk and Innocence. Fenna was repulsed by this idea, but that didn't stop Nopec's spree of murder.
item_45640_Name: Nopec's Dreaded Dagger of Endless Nights
item_45641_Description: This deadly knife is made from the fang of a Winter Dragon. (Thus making it contraband in Council cities, where dragon parts are forbidden.) It has been augmented to allow the casting of ice magic.
item_45642_Description: The blade of one who has lost graces in elvish court and sunken to depraved lows.
item_45643_Description: An exotic filleting tool, only for fishing experts.
item_45701_Description: A long knife held in the off-hand.
item_45702_Description: A long knife held in the off-hand.
item_45703_Description: A long knife held in the off-hand. It was once very sharp, but now not so much.
item_45704_Description: A simple but robust off-hand blade.
item_45705_Description: A simple but robust off-hand blade.
item_45706_Description: A long knife held in the off-hand.
item_45707_Description: A simple off-hand knife that's been crafted to exacting standards.
item_45708_Description: A simple off-hand knife that's been crafted to exacting standards.
item_45709_Description: An old dwarven tool. While unexceptional by dwarven standards, it's quite impressive to many other races, who use it as an off-hand weapon.
item_45710_Description: A strangely-weighted off-hand knife that's good for bashing as well as stabbing.
item_45712_Description: An off-hand blade favored by some elven fencers, who use it in conjunction with a longsword.
item_45713_Description: A gimmicky off-hand knife with a spring mechanism to increase thrusting damage. The mechanism gets gunked up a lot, but on the plus side, the knife is VERY sharp.
item_45714_Description: An off-hand knife used as a personal-defense weapon by some mages. It has a tiny warding glyph that diminishes damage.
item_45715_Description: An off-hand weapon with nasty barbs to cause as much traumatic damage as possible.
item_45716_Description: An ancient dirk that has been altered by dark magic. But don't worry, it's still sharp.
item_45717_Description: This dirk has never not existed and will never stop existing. It was removed from the timestream by Romast, the god of Martial Arts, who is ironically long-dead.
item_45718_Description: A sneaky ratkin weapon designed for poison use.
item_45719_Description: A common backup weapon for Spring Fairies that's beginning to find popularity among Winter as well.
item_45720_Description: An old dwarven tool. While unexceptional by dwarven standards, it's quite impressive to many other races, who use it as an off-hand weapon.
item_45721_Description: Wielded by the more sinister variety of Winter Court fae assassins, this shiv freezes everything it touches.
item_45722_Description: Styled after the blade of the notorious vampire Ruxandra, who publicly forsook blood-drinking, but was nevertheless hunted and defanged by a mob of angry humans. She took her revenge, using this dirk to feed on the blood of those she deemed unworthy of life.
item_46001_Description: Well, it's still got a pointy end and a handle. It should work fine.
item_46002_Description: It's a sword! Looks pretty sharp.
item_46003_Description: The hilt has been worn smooth with use, leaving indentations that make it easier for neophytes to know how to hold it properly.
item_46005_Description: This is a basic sword that Joeh custom-modded over the years. It has a unique hilt and "speed stripes" painted on the blade. Joeh had an entire joke routine planned for when someone asked him what the stripes were for, but nobody ever did.
item_46006_Description: This sword is sharp. Really sharp. As sharp as you can probably get without imbuing it with magic.
item_46007_Description: Forged by the most skilled goblin weaponsmiths in the tri-county area.
item_46008_Description: Crafted by dwarven blacksmith (and notorious druid-hater) Goh Rastuk. It was going to be imbued with anti-nature magic, but Goh didn't get around to it. It's still a nice sword, though.
item_46010_Description: Stained with blood, ash, and lightning scars, this dark, evil sword is cold to the touch. Which is kind of creepy. But it's surprisingly well-maintained, and wonderfully sharp! A necromancy gem is embedded in the hilt.
item_46011_Description: An old style of blade, favored by some werewolves but not many others.
item_46012_Description: Silver is a poor metal for making weapons, and this pocked and marred blade shows it. But its effect against lycanthropes is quite pronounced.
item_46013_Description: An iron sword with a thick coat of silver on top.
item_46014_Description: Each orc is given a xiphos upon their attainment of adulthood. Made of cold iron, it has a broad blade that slightly favors strength over finesse.
item_46015_Description: It's a sword! Looks pretty sharp.
item_46016_Description: This sword is sharp. Really sharp. As sharp as you can probably get without imbuing it with magic.
item_46017_Description: This sword was stuck to a big spider. It's not really in good shape, but the holes in the blade make a neat whistling sound when you swing it.
item_46018_Description: The  rune etchings in this blade were made by Elven masters. It is rare to find these so far from Elven homelands.
item_46019_Description: A very old style of Dwarven sword design.
item_4601_Description: A rock for sharpening the blades of tools.
item_46020_Description: A blade that has been in the Sedgewick family line for generations.
item_46022_Description: Goblin-made, with a focus on sharpness over all else.
item_46023_Description: Rust has taken its toll on this blade, but it's better than nothing.
item_46024_Description: It has an inscription: "Ensorcelled by Official Council Battlemages. Not for use by Rakshasa upon penalty of triple-death."
item_46025_Description: This is a really old weapon design! They don't make 'em like this anymore, for better or for worse.
item_46026_Description: This short sword is fairly terrible for anything except gutting livestock. But it does that really well!
item_46027_Description: The hilt has been worn smooth with use, leaving indentations that make it easier for neophytes to know how to hold it properly.
item_46028_Description: This goblin-made weapon uses a manticore tail as a powerful injector. It's not a very good sword, but it's a great poisoning tool.
item_46029_Description: Stained with blood, ash, and lightning scars, this dark, evil sword is cold to the touch. Which is kind of creepy. But it's surprisingly well-maintained, and wonderfully sharp! A necromancy gem is embedded in the hilt.
item_4602_Description: A rock for sharpening the blades of advanced tools or small blades.
item_46030_Description: An Elven Longsword that has been customized and modified by an expert. Dozens of little lines have been scratched into the blade, suggesting perhaps that the weapon has been used to kill a great many of something.
item_46031_Description: A rapier-like weapon that also holds a strong cutting edge, this blade is the invention of Lord ErDrick, who was known for his expertise in defensive swordsmanship.
item_46032_Description: Made for orcish armies by dwarven smiths under an ancient treaty.
item_46033_Description: An evil weapon forged in a dark fire. A necromancy gem rests in the hilt like a gaudy bauble.
item_46034_Description: This odd weapon was made specifically for chopping attacks. The weighting makes it difficult to use for piercing.
item_46036_Description: Used by Gazluk orcs to inspire courage and/or fear.
item_46037_Description: Designed by blacksmith-in-hiding Torgan, this heavy blade yearns to spill blood.
item_46038_Description: A weapon design from a far-away land.
item_46039_Description: A sturdy military weapon made for ratkin. Thanks to impressive magic, it seems undamaged by years of extreme misuse.
item_46040_Description: Etched with the symbol of a long-lost elven family.
item_46041_Description: As soon as you wield this blade, you can sense its eagerness: it wants to be your partner in murder! Of course, good partnerships require sacrifice from both partners...
item_46042_Description: An Orichalcum blade with a seashell motif. (With random level 35 enchantments.)
item_46043_Description: An Orichalcum blade with a seashell motif. (With random level 40 enchantments.)
item_46044_Description: An Orichalcum blade with a seashell motif. (With random level 45 enchantments.)
item_46045_Description: A strange weapon made by trolls in a distant realm.
item_46046_Description: An extraordinarily light weapon. The entire blade weighs less than the gaudy gem in the hilt.
item_46047_Description: This quality weapon has been adorned with tiny jewels and acid-etched with subtle, elegant patterns.
item_46048_Description: It carries the seal of Jerahd, the only swordsmith in Povus (now deceased). It feels oddly unsettling in the hand.
item_46049_Description: Wielded by the warrior-priests of Saledar, the goblin god of Societal Cooperation.
item_46050_Description: A new sword made in a very old dwarven style. It has a glyph that must be the Eltibule crest.
item_46051_Description: The life's work of dwarven blacksmith Jott Battlestar, who claimed it contains a shred of the essence of a star.
item_46052_Description: While arguably not as effective as a super-sharp normal sword, Prognek's flaming design is fun for pyromaniacs.
item_46053_Description: Though now best known as a captain of the splinter group known as the Alharth Rebels, Tabor first gained fame in the Sand War, wielding this blade in the name of the Council.
item_46054_Description: This broadsword was enchanted with the sole goal of taking off heads.
item_46055_Description: A strange weapon made by trolls in a distant realm.
item_46056_Description: A blade that has been in the Sedgewick family line for generations.
item_46057_Description: The  rune etchings in this blade were made by Elven masters. It is rare to find these so far from Elven homelands.
item_46058_Description: It's unclear what this sword is made of, or who might have smithed it. The edge is so sharp that it can cut flesh from three inches away.
item_46059_Description: While inarguably well-made, it seems somewhat ceremonial: the blade isn't even sharp! But it is very, very, VERY hot, which might count for something. There is an inscription: "The Bearer Is Worthy"
item_46060_Description: A Veilmoor clan promotional weapon. The blade psychically screams on contact with enemies.
item_46101_Description: A professional-grade broadsword made for slicing and injuring.
item_46102_Description: A professional-grade broadsword made for slicing and injuring.
item_46103_Description: A professional-grade broadsword made for slicing and injuring.
item_46104_Description: An exquisite broadsword made for slicing and injuring.
item_46105_Description: An exquisite broadsword made for slicing and injuring.
item_46106_Description: An exquisite broadsword made for slicing and injuring.
item_46107_Description: An exquisite broadsword made for slicing and injuring.
item_4611_Description: A grindstone for sharpening swords and other large weapons.
item_4612_Description: Tools for refining the balance and movement of weapons. Includes restringing kit.
item_4613_Description: When grip precision is crucial, this paper has the perfect level of abrasiveness.
item_4614_Description: From burs to brushes, screws to setting tools, it's all in here.
item_46503_Description: The fretboard is reinforced with steel to keep it from breaking at an inopportune moment.
item_46504_Description: The fretboard is reinforced with steel to keep it from breaking at an inopportune moment.
item_46505_Description: This once-high-quality Trancelute was left out in the elements. No amount of tuning will make it very effective.
item_46506_Description: This bardic instrument has a lovely, uplifting sound that makes it fun to play.
item_46507_Description: Mass-produced by competent artisans, there is nothing special -- or especially wrong -- about this lute.
item_46508_Description: Mass-produced by competent artisans, there is nothing special -- or especially wrong -- about this lute.
item_46509_Description: Made for goblins by craftsman slaves. It seems fairly old, and has a nice sound quality.
item_46510_Description: Augmented with magical sigils that enhance the lute's hypnotic properties. (The magic isn't strong, but it's noticeable.) One of the sigils happens to be a symbol of Arisetsu, enabling priest magic.
item_46511_Description: This ancient doomlute was severely damaged and then repaired by an expert. It still holds a bit of its magic.
item_46512_Description: This is slightly louder than a normal lute, allowing more effective rallying.
item_46513_Description: Made by an expert craftsman in Rahu. The body is imported hardwood, the strings are high-quality grimalkin gut, and the unusual enchantments give it a deep luxurious sound quality. Not for beginners!
item_46514_Description: The strange symbols etched into this lute seem to represent constellations, but not ones you're immediately familiar with.
item_46515_Description: Resonance sigils give this bardic weapon a potent boom.
item_46516_Description: An ancient dwarven instrument from a long-lost age.
item_46517_Description: A gift from Rappanel, the elven bard. It has something of his signature sound.
item_46518_Description: Made of wood from a world that you will never see (and if you did, all the trees are extinct anyway). It's a little small, but serviceable.
item_46519_Description: A rebranded lute that was actually made in Rahu.
item_4651_Description: A customized hammer used by toolsmiths and weaponsmiths.
item_46520_Description: A grandiose sound comes from this rather hefty lute.
item_46521_Description: A practice tool used by bards during their studies.
item_4652_Description: A customized hammer used by armorsmiths.
item_4653_Description: A customized hammer used by smiths.
item_4654_Description: A customized hammer used by smiths.
item_4655_Description: A customized hammer used by smiths.
item_4656_Description: A customized hammer used by smiths.
item_4657_Description: A customized hammer used by chefs.
item_4658_Description: A customized hammer used by armor and jewelry crafters.
item_4659_Description: A customized hammer used by master smiths.
item_46701_Description: A simple instrument made of animal horn.
item_46702_Description: A simple instrument made of animal horn.
item_46703_Description: A musical instrument made of ram's horn.
item_46704_Description: Made of a large animal horn and enchanted with magics that enhance its volume.
item_46705_Description: Carved from a ram's horn by an artisan in Statehelm.
item_46706_Description: Carved from a ram's horn by an artisan in Statehelm.
item_46707_Description: A wooden instrument that was invented by lumberjacks in Sedgewick Forest to ward off treant saplings.
item_46708_Description: Made of stone, this traditional dwarven instrument requires much skill to use.
item_46709_Description: A wooden instrument with terrible and toxic enchantments. Even touching it to your lips causes slight pain.
item_46710_Description: Made from a sea-snail shell, this instrument has an ominous tone.
item_46711_Description: Made of a secret elven wood (not oak, despite the name), this horn has a warm but somber quality.
item_46712_Description: A simple horn instrument that's been enchanted to make listeners more nimble.
item_46713_Description: An instrument made of two ram horns, one embedded inside the other. The sound is terrible, so it is exclusively used as a weapon.
item_46714_Description: This horn is made of shell and enchanted with protective runes.
item_46715_Description: This sort of instrument is said to be used by elves when they go to war. The last elven war is a distant memory, however, so it's unclear who's making these horns.
item_46716_Description: Made from the horn of the extinct Destiny Ram, this instrument is illegal in six countries, including Verta. But it sounds great!
item_46719_Description: A white-label horn sold as part of Povus's product line. It's unclear who actually created it.
item_4671_Description: A tiny batch of adhesive. It's used by crafters such as carpenters and fletchers.
item_46720_Description: Grimly fashioned from a mythical rhino's horn. It has a sound like... a dying rhino.
item_46722_Description: An inscription on this beautiful instrument claims that it was used by Ultimator to wake up a drunken company of troops at the Battle of Buttocks' End. If true, this battle has been lost to the ages. The instrument primarily excels at low fart-like noises.
item_46723_Description: An inscription claims "Fame and Fortune are Fleeting..."
item_4672_Description: A tiny batch of adhesive. It's used by crafters such as carpenters and fletchers.
item_46734_Description: A shell that can make noise.
item_46735_Description: A beautiful shell with a mellow sound.
item_46736_Description: A lovely shell that's perfect for bardic performances.
item_46737_Description: An exceptional instrument that's too complex for amateurs. This shell's intricate fingering is best left to bards.
item_46738_Description: An exceptional instrument that's too complex for amateurs. This shell's intricate fingering is best left to bards.
item_46739_Description: An exceptional instrument that's too complex for amateurs. This shell's intricate fingering is best left to bards.
item_4673_Description: A tiny batch of adhesive. It's used by crafters such as carpenters and fletchers.
item_46740_Description: An exceptional instrument that's too complex for amateurs. This shell's intricate fingering is best left to bards.
item_46741_Description: An exceptional instrument that's too complex for amateurs. This shell's intricate fingering is best left to bards.
item_46742_Description: An exceptional instrument that's too complex for amateurs. This shell's intricate fingering is best left to bards.
item_4674_Description: A tiny batch of adhesive. It's used by crafters such as carpenters and fletchers.
item_4681_Description: A simple knife designed for beginning knife-throwers. (Used with the Knife combat skill.)
item_4682_Description: A knife designed for throwing. (Used with the Knife combat skill.)
item_4683_Description: A well-balanced knife designed for throwing. (Used with the Knife combat skill.)
item_4684_Description: A carefully-made knife designed for expert knife-throwers. (Used with the Knife combat skill.)
item_4685_Description: A masterfully-made throwing knife. (Used with the Knife combat skill.)
item_4686_Description: An exceptionally well-made throwing knife. (Used with the Knife combat skill.)
item_4687_Description: An exceptionally well-made throwing knife. (Used with the Knife combat skill.)
item_4688_Description: An exceptionally well-made throwing knife. (Used with the Knife combat skill.)
item_47001_Description: This staff has seen so much. Thankfully it can't talk.
item_47003_Description: It's charred and black from tossing hundreds of fireballs over the years, but it's still really comfortable to use.
item_47004_Description: This was enchanted by a competent apprentice, but it's nothing special.
item_47005_Description: An inscription in Elven reads "To my lovely Velkort, thanks for a great evening."
item_47006_Description: This was made by an advanced apprentice, perhaps. While not a masterpiece, it avoids all the classic blunders of staff-making.
item_47007_Description: Designed for inquisitive fire mages who like to explore new ways to burn things.
item_47008_Description: The original Fatestick was created in a bygone age by Jessandra the Cruel, who used it to save an orphanage from frost bees. Afterwards she declared that the staff had somehow tricked her into being kind and discarded it.
item_47009_Description: So named because it makes a sort of swishing noise. Nobody said weapons crafters were good at naming things.
item_47010_Description: Well-suited for staff-fighting, but a reasonable fire staff as well. Versatility!
item_47011_Description: Hogan the staff-fighter's masterpiece... Well, a reasonable facsimile.
item_47012_Description: The lich Khyrulek crafted this wand during her centuries as a living corpse. It works better for the undead, but it's still pretty good for living people. (Not suitable for use as a fighting staff. This only works for fire magic.)
item_47013_Description: Handmade by a fairy, this staff is embarrassingly dainty.
item_47015_Description: Very sturdy, but with a hollow core that gives it a strange heft when swung.
item_47016_Description: Made of burned bones, this staff is great for necromancers or fire mages. Less great for staff fighting, because burned bones aren't exactly quality building material. It's topped with a necromancy gem.
item_47017_Description: A plain stick topped with a necromancy gem. Also works for Fire Magic and Staff Fighting.
item_47018_Description: Elves create these staves when they're feeling old. The feeling usually passes after a few decades, though, and then the elves give them away.
item_47019_Description: Honed to perfection by a staff expert who slowly went mad from Lycanthropy. It's a powerful weapon, though it is marred by long ugly claw marks.
item_47020_Description: Infused with arcane protective rituals. While technically usable by fire Fire Mages and Staff Fighters, this weapon is designed to be wielded by those who fancy themselves healers.
item_47021_Description: Infused with arcane protective rituals. While technically usable by fire Fire Mages and Staff Fighters, this weapon is designed to be wielded by those who fancy themselves healers.
item_47022_Description: Given to neophyte druids to help them hone their powers.
item_47023_Description: This was the favored match-stick of a minor flame spirit.
item_47024_Description: This was enchanted by a competent apprentice, but it's nothing special.
item_47025_Description: This was made by an advanced apprentice, perhaps. While not a masterpiece, it avoids all the classic blunders of staff-making.
item_47026_Description: Orloaka the goblin sage augmented this staff by filling its hollow core with tears collected from dying unicorns. Unicorns that he burned alive! (They were actually rhinos, not unicorns, though, so the enchantment isn't all that great.)
item_47027_Description: Used by dwarves who must venture onto the battle field to aid fallen comrades.
item_47028_Description: This wooden staff is icy cold to the touch.
item_47029_Description: This seems to be made of enchanted ice. Kind of neat.
item_47030_Description: It doesn't have any sand magic or anything. It's just been in the sand a long time.
item_47031_Description: A staff with modest enchantments and modest grip and modest... well, let's just say the whole thing is 'modest'. In a 'not very good' kind of way.
item_47032_Description: A traditional druidic training staff has been augmented with dozens of arcane sigils and then dipped in ... something waxy. Probably something organic. Best not to contemplate.
item_47033_Description: It's charred and black from tossing hundreds of fireballs over the years, but it's still really comfortable to use.
item_47034_Description: It's charred and black from tossing hundreds of fireballs over the years, but it's still really comfortable to use.
item_47035_Description: Inscribed with ancient rakshasa runes, this staff taps magic from another world, making it quite hot. A recent owner also attuned it to Arisetsu, further heating it to the point that wielding the staff is a bit painful.
item_47036_Description: A customized staff with heavy weights on each end.
item_47037_Description: Originally popularized by the terrifying harpy mages known as the Winged Witches.
item_47038_Description: A weapon for experienced staff-fighters who wish to get a little more oomph out of their lunges.
item_47039_Description: This potent battle staff was used by the thaumaturges of Gazluk to train their pet hippogriff.
item_47040_Description:
Used by the elite Gazluk orcish tacticians.

Note: this halberd is considered a Staff, but it cannot be used to cast spells.
item_47041_Description: Designed by blacksmith-in-hiding Torgan, this staff is weighted very oddly, rewarding users for truly committing to each attack.
item_47042_Description:
The traditional weapon of Ranalon Guardians. This model is pretty basic, but still very effective.
Note: this weapon is considered a Staff.
item_47043_Description: A common ranalon weapon that doubles as a multi-purpose tool. Ranastaves are sturdy enough to dig with and resilient enough to survive earth-magic being cast through them.
item_47044_Description: Used by ratkin summoners to raise the dead and toss the occasional fireball.
item_47045_Description: Made of a pretentious fairy wood, this staff is incredibly strong, yet gives in just the right ways.
item_47046_Description: This traditional merfolk weapon has useful water enchantments, but unfortunately those same enchantments make it useless for fire magic. (With random level 35 enchantments.)
item_47047_Description: This traditional merfolk weapon has useful water enchantments, but unfortunately those same enchantments make it useless for fire magic. (With random level 40 enchantments.)
item_47048_Description: This traditional merfolk weapon has useful water enchantments, but unfortunately those same enchantments make it useless for fire magic. (With random level 45 enchantments.)
item_47050_Description: A common ranalon weapon that doubles as a multi-purpose tool. Ranastaves are sturdy enough to dig with and resilient enough to survive earth-magic being cast through them.
item_47051_Description:
The traditional weapon of Ranalon Guardians. This model has a great heft and has been augmented with some common ranalon magics.
Note: this weapon is considered a Staff.
item_47052_Description:
The custom-tailored weapon of a mighty Ranalon Guardian captain.
Note: this weapon is considered a Staff.
item_47053_Description: Made of a felled treant, this staff almost seems to squirm with resentment.
item_47054_Description: This appears to be a custom-detailed ranastaff, not something originally made in Povus. In any case, the quality is high.
item_47055_Description: Used by goblin paleontologists in pursuit of knowledge.
item_47056_Description: While it resembles a wooden staff, it's actually made of super-compressed, highly conductive mushrooms.
item_47057_Description: Customized for the Unfun Guy by visiting weaponsmiths, this staff hits hard. And often.
item_47058_Description: An ornate staff that celebrates your renowned generosity.
item_47059_Description: A simple wooden wand with runes etched into it.
item_47060_Description: A potent weapon used by Leafmasters, Stormmasters, and other guardians of elven society.
item_47061_Description: It looks like a stick. But it's a very magical stick.
item_47062_Description: Adorned with dead leaves that rustle to their own magical wind.
item_47063_Description: It emits a discomfiting aura, as if it isn't quite as real as it ought to be.
item_47064_Description: A deadly wand in the right hands.
item_47065_Description: For many decades this was the de facto standard for masters of the magical arts, only falling from favor because the tree it is made from has gone extinct.
item_47066_Description: A curiously-whittled piece of wood. It hums with electricity.
item_47067_Description: A curiously-whittled piece of wood. It hums with electricity.
item_47068_Description:
The traditional weapon of Ranalon Guardians. This model is pretty nice.
Note: this weapon is considered a Staff.
item_47069_Description: What is that stuff running down the side of the Blood Staff, you ask? Blood. It's blood.
item_47070_Description: A custom wand made by an old druid.
item_47071_Description: A cruel weapon for cruel necromancers who like to burn things.
item_47101_Description: Come on, the bark isn't even fully removed from this stick! But it's pretty thick. (It will work for Staff Fighting but not Fire Magic abilities.)
item_47102_Description: Well, the bark has mostly been removed. Mostly. You'll only get a few splinters trying to use this. (It will work for Staff Fighting but not Fire Magic abilities.)
item_47103_Description: Look, someone figured out how to sand down the edges of a stick. Too bad the creator had no idea how to balance its weight. (It will work for Staff Fighting but not Fire Magic abilities.)
item_47104_Description: This staff is what you'd expect from a combat staff. Absolutely nothing special, though. This will work for Staff Fighting but lacks the runes necessary to work for Fire Magic.
item_47105_Description: Someone took a lot of time balancing and testing this staff to make sure it had the perfect weight distribution. This will work well for Staff Fighting, but lacks the runes necessary to work for Fire Magic.
item_47106_Description: Reinforced and quite sturdy. The weight distribution is slightly off because of the added metal, but it's hardly noticeable. This staff will work well for Staff Fighting, but lacks the runes necessary to work for Fire Magic.
item_47107_Description: The creator of this staff obviously had a lot of experience in making these. Largely metal-clad, but just the right amount of give from the wood. (This staff will work well for Staff Fighting, but lacks the runes necessary to work for Fire Magic.)
item_47108_Description: This staff has a surprising amount of detail work. Grips at just the right places, metal tips, and a reduction of weight without adversely affecting durability. (It will work for Staff Fighting but not Fire Magic abilities.)
item_47109_Description: This has all the relevant features that a staff fighter could want: durable, flexible, perfectly weighted, and makes a cool whistling sound when swung. (This will work for Staff Fighting but not Fire Magic abilities.)
item_47110_Description: No words. Just swing this staff around a few times and you'll realize that its beauty surpasses the spoken language. (This will work for Staff Fighting but not Fire Magic abilities.)
item_47111_Description: No words. Just swing this staff around a few times and you'll realize that its beauty surpasses the spoken language. (This will work for Staff Fighting but not Fire Magic abilities.)
item_47112_Description: No words. Just swing this staff around a few times and you'll realize that its beauty surpasses the spoken language. (This will work for Staff Fighting but not Fire Magic abilities.)
item_47121_Description: Come on, the bark isn't even fully removed from this stick! But it's pretty thick. Very basic Fire Magic runes have been crudely etched into the bark.
item_47122_Description: Well, the bark has mostly been removed. Mostly. You'll only get a few splinters trying to use this. The crude Fire Magic runes bear the mark of an amateur, but should work.
item_47123_Description: Look, someone figured out how to sand down the edges of a stick. Too bad the creator had no idea how to balance its weight. The Fire Magic runes look a little less haphazard, though.
item_47124_Description: This staff is what you'd expect from a combat staff. Absolutely nothing special, though. The standard Fire Magic runes have been etched into the wood.
item_47125_Description: Someone took a lot of time balancing and testing this staff to make sure it had the perfect weight distribution. Then Fire Magic runes were carefully sculpted into the wood.
item_47126_Description: Reinforced and quite sturdy. The weight distribution is slightly off because of the added metal, but it's hardly noticeable. The Fire Magic runes were burned through the metal, creating a neat tactile sensation.
item_47127_Description: The creator of this staff obviously had a lot of experience in making these. Largely metal-clad, but just the right amount of give from the wood. The Fire Magic Runes were burned in, and glow with comforting warmth when used.
item_47128_Description: This staff has a surprising amount of detail work. Grips at just the right places, metal tips, and a reduction of weight without adversely affecting durability. The Fire Magic runes are elegant but understated.
item_47129_Description: This has all the relevant features that a staff fighter or fire mage could want: durable, flexible, perfectly weighted, fire-spewing, and makes a cool whistling sound when swung.
item_47130_Description: No words. Just swing this staff around a few times and you'll realize that its beauty surpasses the spoken language. The Fire Magic runes are a masterpiece of calligraphy, too.
item_47131_Description: No words. Just swing this staff around a few times and you'll realize that its beauty surpasses the spoken language. The Fire Magic runes are a masterpiece of calligraphy, too.
item_47132_Description: No words. Just swing this staff around a few times and you'll realize that its beauty surpasses the spoken language. The Fire Magic runes are a masterpiece of calligraphy, too.
item_47141_Description: A symbol of Arisetsu has been crudely carved into this crude staff.
item_47142_Description: A symbol of Arisetsu has been etched into this inelegant staff. Functional but unimpressive.
item_47143_Description: A symbol of Arisetsu adorns this otherwise-uninteresting staff.
item_47144_Description: A professional-looking staff with fire magic and priestly runes etched into it.
item_47145_Description: All three common symbols of Arisetsu are inscribed in Elvish into this nice staff.
item_47146_Description: The Six Names of Arisetsu are etched in Elvish into this lovely wooden staff.
item_47147_Description: An excellent and versatile staff. All eight Suns of Arisetsu are etched into this staff, hidden amongst the arcane runes.
item_47148_Description: Perfect weighting, professional sigiling, and understated holy symbols combine in this exquisite and versatile staff.
item_47149_Description: This masterpiece includes the Six Names of Arisetsu, the Eight Suns of Arisetsu, the Four Priestly Runes, and dozens of other icons.
item_47150_Description: No words. Just swing this staff around a few times and you'll realize that its beauty surpasses the spoken language. The Fire Magic and Priest runes are a masterpiece of calligraphy, too.
item_47151_Description: No words. Just swing this staff around a few times and you'll realize that its beauty surpasses the spoken language. The Fire Magic and Priest runes are a masterpiece of calligraphy, too.
item_47152_Description: No words. Just swing this staff around a few times and you'll realize that its beauty surpasses the spoken language. The Fire Magic and Priest runes are a masterpiece of calligraphy, too.
item_47166_Description: A lovely weapon, marred perhaps by its unsightly acid-flinging mechanism.
item_47167_Description: A strong staff with a chemistry system on the end.
item_47168_Description: Covered in magical runes that augment battle chemistry.
item_47169_Description: Coated in too many etched symbols to count, it's easy to overlook the secret button that sprays chemicals from the end.
item_47170_Description: Gaudy but somehow still gorgeous, this staff has hidden controls for all sorts of mechanisms to spurt acid.
item_47171_Description: Gaudy but somehow still gorgeous, this staff has hidden controls for all sorts of mechanisms to spurt acid.
item_47172_Description: Gaudy but somehow still gorgeous, this staff has hidden controls for all sorts of mechanisms to spurt acid.
item_4725_Description: A handful of magical ice from the fairy dimension.
item_4726_Description: A handful of magical essence from the fairy dimension. It is used in many fairy creation techniques and can also be consumed directly to restore health and wing luster. (Note: fairy dust is extremely toxic to mortals and should never be consumed by non-fae!)
item_47401_Description: Crafted by expert Winter Court ice mages for use in the eternal struggle of the seasons. (Although it's made of incredibly strong ice, it's not suitable for use as a melee weapon.)
item_47402_Description: Crafted out of magical ice that doesn't melt. (Not suitable for use as a melee weapon.)
item_47403_Description: Crafted out of magical ice that doesn't melt. (Not suitable for use as a melee weapon.)
item_47404_Description: Crafted by expert Winter Court ice mages for use in the eternal struggle of the seasons. (Although it's made of incredibly strong ice, it's not suitable for use as a melee weapon.)
item_4751_Description: A metal tube with a closeable valve. Used on alcohol barrels to make them easier to use. It is not reusable.
item_4752_Description: A flask strong enough to contain even magical alcohol without evaporation.
item_4771_Description: A flimsy set of tools for picking locks. They'll break when used.
item_4781_Description:
Powdered skull dust for use in consecrating items or places. Harukita, the god of Hate, and Kajich, the god of Fear, both appreciate this sort of thing.

(You can turn this special bone-meal into ordinary bone-meal by right-clicking the item.)
item_4782_Description:
Powdered skull dust for use in consecrating items or places. Harukita, the god of Hate, and Kajich, the god of Fear, both appreciate this sort of thing.

(You can turn this special bone-meal into ordinary bone-meal by right-clicking the item.)
item_4783_Description:
Powdered skull dust for use in consecrating items or places. Harukita, the god of Hate, and Kajich, the god of Fear, both appreciate this sort of thing.

(You can turn this special bone-meal into ordinary bone-meal by right-clicking the item.)
item_48004_Description: Shaped like a tiny pair of longswords.
item_48005_Description: A tiny metal fist on a chain.
item_48006_Description: A tiny metal foot on a chain.
item_48007_Description: A figurine of a tiny metal fireball on a chain.
item_48008_Description: A figurine of two tiny metal fireballs on a chain.
item_48010_Description: A figurine of a heart with many smaller little hearts attached to it. Eerie.
item_48011_Description: A figurine of two hearts combined. Weird.
item_48012_Description: A figurine of two hearts with little hearts coming off of them, as if they were in love. Creepy.
item_48013_Description: A figurine of a tiny shield.
item_48014_Description: A figurine of a tiny shield.
item_48015_Description: A figurine of a tiny shield.
item_48016_Description: A figurine of a tiny shield.
item_48017_Description: A figurine of a tiny shield.
item_48018_Description: A figurine of two wolves killing a deer together. So tiny and yet so detailed!
item_48019_Description: This is just some weird elven runes on a chain.
item_4801_Description: A metal mesh, useful for cages, filters, and similar systems.
item_48020_Description: This is just some weird elven runes on a chain.
item_48021_Description: This is just some weird elven runes on a chain.
item_48022_Description: A tiny metal fist squeezing a man, who seems unperturbed. That's craftsmanship!
item_48023_Description: A tiny metal fist squeezing a man, who seems unperturbed. That's craftsmanship!
item_48024_Description: A tiny metal fist squeezing a man, who seems unperturbed. That's craftsmanship!
item_48025_Description: A tiny metal fist squeezing a man, who seems unperturbed. That's craftsmanship!
item_48026_Description: A tiny metal fist squeezing a man, who seems unperturbed. That's craftsmanship!
item_48027_Description: A figurine of a tiny man with a sword in his skull, who seems to be unperturbed.
item_48028_Description: A figurine of a tiny man with a sword in his skull, who seems to be unperturbed.
item_48029_Description: A figurine of a tiny man with a sword in his skull, who seems to be unperturbed.
item_4802_Description: A metal mesh, useful for cages, filters, and similar systems.
item_48030_Description: A figurine of a tiny man with a sword in his skull, who seems to be unperturbed.
item_48031_Description: A figurine of a tiny man with a sword in his skull, who seems to be unperturbed.
item_48032_Description: A figurine of a man with an arrow in his back, who seems to be grinning.
item_48033_Description: A figurine of a man with an arrow in his back, who seems to be grinning.
item_48034_Description: A figurine of a man with an arrow in his back, who seems to be grinning.
item_48035_Description: A figurine of a man with an arrow in his back, who seems to be grinning.
item_48036_Description: A figurine of a man with an arrow in his back, who seems to be grinning.
item_48037_Description: A figurine of a woman with hearts glued to every surface of her body. Very disturbing.
item_48038_Description: An elegant opal on a thin gold chain. Tasteful.
item_48039_Description: Hand-made from a leftover snail shell, and infused with simple magic by Sie's fairy friends.
item_4803_Description: A metal frame for attaching a torch to a wall.
item_48040_Description: A figurine of a smiling man with ice-entombed feet.
item_48041_Description: A medallion with an abstract design. Perhaps it is mantis claws raised in victory. Or in pain.
item_48042_Description: A figurine of a grinning skull. But, of course, all skulls grin. A necromancy gem is in the eye socket.
item_48043_Description: Made of blackened metal and etched with demonic runes.
item_48044_Description: A figurine of a tiny shield.
item_48045_Description: A figurine of a man holding a heart in his hands. The tiny heart pulses with its own rhythm.
item_48046_Description: Blackened as if from fire, this metal glyph emanates controlled rage. A soot-covered Necromancy Gem is still affixed on the back.
item_48048_Description: A string covered in weird little beads and shells.
item_48049_Description: Used by goblin necromancers. A necromancy gem has been embedded in a case made of bone shards.
item_48050_Description: Used by the goblin necromancer Jumjab. It contains two different necromancy gems, in case one gets shattered in combat.
item_48051_Description: A weird elven rune on a chain. Unlike most elven rune amulets, this one was mass-produced in Verta for export to other lands. Not exactly a triumph of art, but its protective magic is undeniable.
item_48052_Description: An expensive necklace with a well-cut magic tourmaline. This type of necklace is often used by people who fear they are in danger of having a psychotic break, although it can only hold off the inevitable for a little while.
item_48053_Description: A figurine of a muscular human with a huge pack on his back.
item_48054_Description: This is just some weird elven runes on a chain.
item_48056_Description: A figurine of a tiny shield.
item_48057_Description: A mirror on a chain with a subtle illusion. Enemies briefly see you as being far more beautiful than you are, then suddenly perceive you as being far uglier than reality. It's pretty unnerving.
item_48058_Description: A tiny enchanted saw on a chain. But it's not just an amulet: upon command, the tiny saw can cut lumber at high speed! (This item acts as a handsaw.)
item_48059_Description: Foxes are beloved wizard familiars because they channel tremendous Power. This little metal image of a fox does too.
item_48060_Description: A fad item by the master of trend magic, Rea Shuloth. Rea's spell compelled everyone to love these necklaces, but even magic can't control fashion for long, and now these are seen as incredibly gauche.
item_48061_Description: This bit of magical rhino horn can be worn as a makeshift necklace.
item_48062_Description: A very old locket with a portrait of a human woman painted inside. The artistic quality is quite high. It is engraved, "Don't cry, but don't forget. Love always, Caroline."
item_48063_Description: A small bag of something tied with a cord. You can't tell what's in the bag because it's frozen completely solid, and it seems impervious to any form of heat.
item_48065_Description: A figurine of a tiny brain.
item_48066_Description: Designed to let you drink longer without unfortunate gastrointestinal issues.
item_48067_Description: Created by the famed elven jeweler Sharra, this necklace supposedly reflects the wearer's treatment of the poor. It doesn't seem to do anything like that, but nobody has been brave enough to ask Sharra what's up.
item_48068_Description: A magical prize given to the first-place horse in the decennial Jamurra Derby.
item_48069_Description: This thin chain features an onyx figurine of an orc on a horse. Orcish rituals have attuned it to several gods, including Tast, Arisetsu, and Akhisa.
item_48070_Description: A jeweled collar given to a hippogriff by its orcish caretaker.
item_48071_Description: A tasteful silver-and-pyrite chain with an opal pendant.
item_48072_Description: An elegant, but simple, medallion on a chain.
item_48073_Description: A tacky necklace that calls upon Harukita, the God of Hate, to grant the wearer power. This power is typically used in pretty hateful ways. This necklace also acts as a necromancy gem.

item_48104_Description: A squid amulet worn by those that venerate Enoyos. This one is a failed experiment by Chirrra. The enchantment has been altered in strange and seemingly-random ways.

item_48132_Description: A necklace depicting the warm sun, one of the symbols of Arisetsu. This variety was mass-produced for use by Council priests, and was probably lost during the War of Rahu.
item_48133_Description: During Winter's reign in the fae realm, these necklaces are extremely common.
item_48134_Description: This bit of magical rhino horn can be worn as a makeshift necklace.
item_48135_Description: Practically drowning in magical tourmalines, this necklace wards off bad thoughts in the very literal sense.
item_48136_Description: A small collar studded with sardonyx gems. Popular among certain sentient animals.
item_48137_Description: Worn by a sentient panther for years. Its strong protective magic overshadows the smell of blood soaked into the collar.
item_48138_Description: A gaudy squid amulet worn by those that venerate Enoyos, god of the sea.
item_48139_Description: Worn by ranalon to help protect their sensitive skin from environmental harm.
item_48140_Description: A figurine of a man with an arrow in his back, who seems to be grinning.
item_48141_Description: The medallion is engraved with a pirate ship, the symbol of the goblin god Remga.
item_48143_Description: A large, reasonably discrete Enoyos amulet.
item_48145_Description: A simple chain featuring two decorative gems.
item_48146_Description: A simple chain featuring two decorative gems.

item_48180_Description: A figurine of a muscular human with a huge pack on his back. This amulet was enhanced by Moxie of the Crafting Caravan.
item_48180_Name: Enhanced Amulet of the Rugged Traveler
item_48181_Description: A silver medallion etched with a simple drawing of a smiling elf's face.
item_48182_Description: A thick, cumbersome silver medallion etched with a simple drawing of a smiling elf's face.
item_48183_Description: A gaudy squid amulet worn by those that venerate Enoyos, god of the sea.
item_48201_Description: Worn by lay followers of Strekios, the God of Self-Improvement. It's styled after the terrifying medallions worn at Strekios Camps, but this one is purely decorative, aside from a few enchantments that were added later.
item_48202_Description: A clunky necklace with an attached pouch. Made in response to Povus legging's stereotypical lack of pockets.
item_48203_Description: A modest silver medallion of elven design. It helps turn words into weapons so you can get what you want.
item_48204_Description: A modest silver medallion of elven design. It helps one focus on their problems.
item_48205_Description: A gaudy talisman of a cat with very long whiskers.
item_48206_Description: A thick gold chain with a medallion shaped like a pair of wings.
item_48207_Description: A jeweled collar given to a hippogriff by its orcish caretaker.
item_48208_Description: Made of blackened metal and etched with demonic runes.
item_48209_Description: This terrifying medallion once belonged to an Ormalan man named Fredrick, the mate of Liamu the Watchful.
item_48210_Description: This silver charm is engraved with two angelic wings inset with an azure gem in the middle. It slows the wearer's fly speed while also reducing the cost of staying in the air, ideal for one who enjoys being up among the clouds.
item_48211_Description: This silver charm is engraved with two angelic wings inset with an azure gem in the middle. It slows the wearer's fly speed while also reducing the cost of staying in the air, ideal for one who enjoys being up among the clouds.
item_48212_Description: A beautiful chain with a slightly-large purple gem. It has a multifaceted glamour that makes the crystal seem to constantly twinkle.
item_4831_Description: A terrifyingly complex device that can analyze the secret codes buried deep inside certain creatures. Warning: many cultures consider genetics study to be taboo. (Geneticists can keep this in inventory to be offered the option to gene-analyze this type of creature. There is a 5% chance that the device will break when used.)
item_4832_Description: A terrifyingly complex device that can analyze the secret codes buried deep inside certain creatures. This model is especially easy to use. Warning: many cultures consider genetics study to be taboo. (Geneticists can keep this in inventory to be offered the option to gene-analyze this type of creature. There is a 5% chance that the device will break when used.)

item_4861_Description: A simple whittling knife for beginning whittlers.
item_4862_Description: A basic whittling knife for novice whittlers.
item_4863_Description: A quality whittling knife for experienced whittlers.
item_4864_Description: A great whittling knife for expert whittlers.
item_4865_Description: A perfect whittling knife for master whittlers.
item_4892_Description: A straightforward but sophisticated tool for extracting fossils. (Keep it in your inventory and you'll be able to excavate fossil sites. There is a chance the pick will break with each use.)
item_49001_Description: This ugly purple ring magically strengthens your ankles so that falling from great heights isn't quite as painful. It's a pretty weak enchantment, really.
item_49002_Description: This criminally ugly purple ring augments your movement so that your sprint speed is slightly faster.
item_49003_Description: This cheap-looking purple ring makes poisons slightly less effective.
item_49004_Description: This shoddily-made ring improves psychic attacks.
item_49005_Description: This tough-but-ugly ring makes Punch attacks deal more damage.
item_49006_Description: This vaguely-green metal ring improves an Unarmed ability: Cobra Strike.
item_49007_Description: This slightly off-white metal ring improves a Sword ability: Many Cuts.
item_49008_Description: This ring is slightly warm to the touch.
item_49009_Description: This slightly off-white metal ring improves a Sword ability: Wind Strike.
item_49010_Description: This dull black ring improves a Psychology ability: Cause Terror.
item_49011_Description: This mottled ring improves a Werewolf ability: Claw
item_4901_Description: This bottle is nice enough for wine. Just.
item_49021_Description: This chunky blue ring augments any kind of kick, such as the Unarmed ability Kick or kicks done when in the form of a deer or other ungulate.
item_49022_Description: This ring glows a pale blue.
item_49023_Description: This thick wooden ring feels surprisingly light.
item_49024_Description: This jade ring thumps in time to the wearer's pulse, the better to tune into their mind.
item_49025_Description: This ring has little barbs all along the outside. Takes some practice to wear it safely.
item_49026_Description: A disgusting ring coated in a perpetual layer of slime. But the slime isn't acid. It's just slime.
item_49027_Description: A golden ring with a gaudy black Necromancy Gem embedded in it.
item_49028_Description: A gaudy golden ring with a purple enchantment aura.
item_49029_Description: An arrowhead is embedded in this wooden ring, making it rather uncomfortable to wear.
item_4902_Description: A fancy bottle makes wine taste better, and this is a pretty fancy bottle.
item_49030_Description: This mottled ring improves a Werewolf ability: Claw
item_49031_Description: Tyler gave this ring to his soulmate as a token of his affection.
item_49032_Description: This ugly blue ring enhances most Psychology healing effects by 50.
item_49033_Description: This ring makes you unnoticeable by monsters, unless they see you in combat.
item_49035_Description: This gaudy gold heart ring looks more like a lovelorn elf's token of affection than some powerful magical device.
item_49036_Description: Carved of bamboo and painted with magic pigments.
item_49037_Description: Made of pink wood, supposedly common in the orcish homeland of Gu.
item_49038_Description: This ring is blackened and pitted from (presumed) acid splashes.
item_49039_Description: This mottled ring improves a Werewolf ability: Smell Fear
item_4903_Description: This bottle is a true work of art. It's almost too good for wine.
item_49040_Description: This wooden ring is carved with elven sword-runes.
item_49042_Description: Jade with scrimshaw glyphs that say "Wizards Rule" in elvish. Fortunately the inscription is on the inside of the ring where nobody can see it.
item_49043_Description: This gold ring is not particularly unique, but it was clearly made by dwarves.
item_49044_Description: Made of dried root, this myconian-designed ring is not attractive, but it is comfortable.
item_49045_Description: This small metal band seems to get tighter and tighter the longer you wear it.
item_49046_Description: This ring seems ready to fly off your finger at any moment, but mercifully stays put.
item_49047_Description: So heavy! Wait, that's probably an illusion... nope, it really is super heavy. Makes it hard to hold stuff.
item_49048_Description: An ugly green ring that leaves a vaguely smelly odor behind.
item_49049_Description: This grungy ring has a little scoop on the end to help you dig skulls out of corpses. (Acts as a skull extractor.)
item_49050_Description: This piece of costume jewelry is so ugly that monsters tend to hate you just for wearing it.
item_49051_Description: A tiny ring made of an otherworldly metal.
item_49052_Description: Made of enchanted aquamarine, this ring takes the oomph out of projectiles that pierce your body.
item_49053_Description: Made in a dwarven style that controls and focuses electricity.
item_49054_Description: A trinket that augments various kinds of archery techniques.
item_49055_Description: Made of enchanted snow, this ring is quite irritating to wear. It's not cold to the touch, and it never seems to lose its shape, but it does melt continuously, creating damp sleeves and pockets.
item_49056_Description: A thick and ugly metal ring.
item_49057_Description: A heavy ring given to Gazluk Keep officers.
item_49058_Description: This ring seems to be made of stone. It's heavy.
item_49060_Description: A metal ring with a beautifully-cut vervadium centerpiece. It glows a soft purple.
item_49061_Description: It's made of some sort of beautiful metal. An inscription inside says: "B - when it falls apart, find me. -T"
item_49062_Description: A relatively understated ring worn by the cultists that worship Ilth Hale, the Goddess of Sorrow. Ilth Hale's followers believe that sorrow must be endured so that vengeance can be wrought, and this ring helps them endure suffering.

item_49080_Description: This criminally ugly, but enhanced purple ring augments your movement speed to be slightly faster. You're also better at picking things up and carrying them.
item_49081_Description: Made of extra bent wire and topped with a piece of green glass... it's made of junk. But it's still pretty, magical and more powerful than before.
item_49101_Description: Made of bent wire and topped with a piece of glass... it's made of junk. But it's still pretty, and it's magical.
item_49102_Description: Made of wire that's been bent into a sort of wave. It feels a little greasy and slimy.
item_49103_Description: A thin wire ring that smells awful.
item_49104_Description: This ring makes you faster. More alert. Jittery. Nervous. Uncomfortable. Tired. But faster!
item_49105_Description: This ring can make anyone a little more beastly, but works best on those who already were.
item_49106_Description: A rugged old bracelet-sized "ring" that can be fit to any sort of animal appendage necessary. It has runes representing a pig, a tree, and a bolt of lightning.
item_49107_Description: A rugged old bracelet-sized "ring" that can be fit to any sort of animal appendage necessary. It has a rune depicting a pig, snug in a blanket, with a cup of tea.
item_49108_Description: A common magical ring worn by ranalon. It exudes a slimy film.
item_49109_Description: A ring that magically guides the wearer's hand for better hit placement.
item_49110_Description: An uncomfortably heavy ring made of black glass. It features a necromancy gem.
item_49111_Description: A ring that magically guides the wearer's hand for better hit placement.
item_49112_Description: A thick onyx ring. Druidic symbols are etched along the inside, including the easily-recognizable symbol of Dreva: a shark.
item_49113_Description: The wearer of this elegant black ring is coated in an energy field that protects them from harm.
item_49114_Description: A strangely heavy ring that can predict where danger will come from, and subtly move your body to prevent death.
item_49115_Description: A beautiful (if simple) ring with a magical glow.

item_49145_Description: This bizarre "ring" is more the size of a bracelet, and can only fit on a pig's leg. A painful needle pierces the pig's skin while worn, somehow allowing the pig to control various chemical bottles with its mind. (Enables Battle Chemistry.)

item_49204_Description: Fun Guile happened to have this ring on when he became a fungus-man, and it allowed him to retain some pre-fungination memories. This created distrust among his brothers, leading to his cruel nickname.

item_49206_Description: Crafted by an ancient necromantic ritual and then blessed by Norala, this ugly ring smells of blood.

item_49214_Description: This brass ring numbs each of your senses just enough to make swimming in water come much easier.

item_49221_Description: Made of pink wood, supposedly common in the orcish homeland of Gu.
