item_49223_Description: This vaguely-green metal ring improves an Unarmed ability: Cobra Strike.
item_49224_Description: This vaguely-green metal ring improves an Unarmed ability: Cobra Strike.
item_49225_Description: Confusingly named, as the ring is intended to be a Warden's primary ring, not their backup ring. It is solid and pleasant to hold.
item_49226_Description: A hefty ring engraved with symbols that are probably related to the god Au-Shin.
item_49227_Description: A beautiful (if perhaps a bit gaudy) ring with a good heft to it.
item_49228_Description: It's a skull ring! It fits all finger sizes. The skull is actually a Necromancy Gem.
item_4_Description: Learn a simple word of power.
item_50001_Description: Designed to be worn at the base of a Rakshasa's tail, this improves the wearer's Righteousness, allowing them to forgo the negative effects of killing sentient creatures for longer.
item_50002_Description: Designed to be worn at the base of a Rakshasa's tail, this allows the wearer to remember two additional map spots at a time. It is inscribed, "to Sinda, the explorer supreme."
item_50003_Description: Designed to be worn in humans' pierced ears, these earrings boost the wearer's maximum capacity for enjoyment of others' company, allowing them to be alone longer without becoming miserable.
item_50004_Description: Designed to be worn in an elf's pierced navel, this small gemmed ring repels dust and dirt.
item_50005_Description: Designed to be worn in an elf's pierced navel, this small gemmed ring enhances the elf's biorhythms slightly.
item_50006_Description: Designed to be worn in an elf's pierced navel, this small gemmed ring enhances the elf's natural accuracy with a bow.
item_50007_Description: Designed to be worn at the base of a Rakshasa's tail, this slows the rate at which hydration is lost, allowing the Rakshasa to function without water for twice as long.
item_50008_Description: Designed to be worn in a cow's nose, this brass ring further enhances natural bovine strength.
item_50009_Description: Simple but high-quality, these earrings were worn by a human who lived in the desert years ago. They can be worn in humans' pierced ears.
item_50011_Description: Designed to be worn at the base of a Rakshasa's tail, this imperceptibly alters a Rakshasa's mind.
item_50012_Description: Designed to be worn in a cow's nose, this brass ring makes attractive cattle even more attractive.
item_50012_Name: Nose Ring of The Seductive Cow
item_50013_Description: Designed to be worn at the base of a deer's tail, this brass ring is very cute.
item_50014_Description: Designed to be worn at the base of a deer's tail, this brass ring enhances ferocity.
item_50015_Description: Designed to be worn in a cow's nose, this brass ring aggravates bovine sensibilities, causing violence.
item_50016_Description: Designed to be worn at the base of a Rakshasa's tail, this helps keep the wearer from dying. It is inscribed with a message that simply reads, "Remember me. - Ashk"
item_50017_Description: Designed to be worn in humans' pierced ears, these lovely earrings are enchanted with potent protection magic.
item_50018_Description: Designed for a ratkin's tail but also usable by rakshasa, this ugly ring makes a soft whistling sound when you move your tail.
item_50019_Description: Designed for a ratkin's tail but also usable by rakshasa, this small yellow ring smells like... cheese? Well, anyway, it stinks.
item_5001_Description: This is a small amount of salt.
item_50020_Description: Designed for a ratkin's nose, but perhaps also wearable by a cow or pig.
item_50021_Description: These earrings will make a neophyte mage's spells seem much more impressive. They were intended as a gift for a young girl who passed before she got a chance to wear them.
item_50022_Description: Designed to be clipped to a fairy's wings, these tassels exude a pheromone that makes many types of bees and wasps non-aggressive (unless attacked).
item_50023_Description: Designed to be clipped to a fairy's wings, these enchanted ringlets improve fae energy conductivity.
item_50024_Description: Designed to be clipped to a fairy's wings, these enchanted tassels make a fairy fly slightly faster.
item_50025_Description: A wooden ring designed to be worn at the base of a fox's tail.
item_50026_Description: A wooden ring designed to be worn at the base of a fox's tail.
item_50027_Description: Designed to be worn on the wings of magical butterflies, these rings grant the wearer access to weather witching powers.
item_50028_Description: Common among elven hunters, this bauble lets you skin corpses from further away, telepathically extending your tool reach and letting you avoid smelling the corpse up close.
item_50028_Name: Navel Ring of Stinky Corpse Avoidance
item_50029_Description: A talisman that clips behind a giant bat's ear, making it much smaller and hence less terrifying to some individuals.
item_5002_Description: Low-quality wheat flour, but cheap and abundant.
item_50030_Description: A collar to make large dogs smaller and less scary to some individuals.
item_5004_Description: This bottle is full of sugary water.
item_5005_Description: A really big, eerily preserved strawberry
item_5006_Description: A chunk of raw deer meat.
item_5007_Description: A chunk of raw pig meat.
item_5008_Description: Crusty goblin bread that stays edible and nutritious for months, though it should be noted that it never tastes good, even when fresh.
item_5009_Description:
A white-mold cheese with a pasty and nutty flavor.

Cheese Type: White
item_50101_Description: Designed to be worn in humans' pierced ears, these earrings help protect against the sickness that comes from being too close to glowy yellow crystals.
item_50102_Description: Designed to be worn in an elf's pierced navel, this ring helps protect against the sickness that comes from being too close to glowy yellow crystals.
item_50102_Name: Navel Ring of Glowy Crystal Resistance
item_50103_Description: Designed to be worn at the base of a rakshasa's tail, but also able to fit on the tail of a wolf or deer, this ring helps protect against the sickness that comes from being too close to glowy yellow crystals.
item_50103_Name: Tail Ring of Glowy Crystal Resistance
item_50104_Description: Designed to be worn in a cow or pig's nose, this ring helps protect against the sickness that comes from being too close to glowy yellow crystals.
item_50104_Name: Nose Ring of Glowy Crystal Resistance
item_50105_Description: Designed to be worn in the wings of a fairy, butterfly, or giant bat, these rings help protect against the sickness that comes from being too close to glowy yellow crystals.
item_50105_Name: Wing Rings of Glowy Crystal Resistance
item_50106_Description: Designed to be slathered onto the mandibles of a giant spider, this reusable paint helps protect against the sickness that comes from being too close to glowy yellow crystals.
item_50106_Name: Mandible Dye of Glowy Crystal Resistance
item_5010_Description: This bottle is full of cow's milk. (This is a snack. Snacks stack with meal foods; you can eat one of each and get the benefits of both.)
item_50111_Description: Designed to be worn in humans' pierced ears, these earrings offer some protection against the negative effects of Hellfire.
item_50112_Description: Designed to be worn in an elf's pierced navel, this ring offers some protection against the negative effects of Hellfire.
item_50113_Description: Designed to be worn at the base of a rakshasa's tail, this ring offers some protection against the negative effects of Hellfire.
item_50114_Description: Designed to be worn in a cow or pig's nose, this ring offers some protection against the negative effects of Hellfire.
item_50115_Description: Designed to be worn in the wings of a fairy, butterfly, or giant bat, these rings offers some protection against the negative effects of Hellfire.
item_50116_Description: Designed to be slathered onto the mandibles of a giant spider, this reusable paint offers some protection against the negative effects of Hellfire.
item_5012_Description:
A very basic mild cheddar cheese.

Cheese Type: Semi-Hard (This is a snack. Snacks stack with meal foods; you can eat one of each and get the benefits of both.)
item_5013_Description: Smooth, luscious yellow butter with a strong sweet undercurrent. Perfect for elven pastries!
item_5015_Description: A bottle of disgusting stuff used in making cheese.
item_5016_Description: A bottle of disgusting stuff used in making cheese.
item_5017_Description: A bottle of disgusting stuff used in making cheese.
item_5018_Description: This bottle contains the acidic runoff of the cheese-making process.
item_5019_Description: This bottle contains the sweet runoff of the cheese-making process.
item_5021_Description: A sour milk product that's useful in many recipes.
item_5024_Description:
A cut of meat from a wild creature that's not normally used for food. The meat is low-quality but still useful in many dishes.

(Use in Cheap Meat recipes.)
item_5026_Description: A big chunk of cow meat.
item_5027_Description: Dried "sticks" of cinnamon, perfect for spicing many dishes.
item_5028_Description: Freshly-squeezed liquid out of a cactus.
item_5029_Description: Chicken meat that causes mild paralysis when eaten. An extremely exotic ingredient in food, but a common ingredient in poisons.
item_5030_Description:
This cat meat might be fresh. Or it might not be. It's so gross that it'd be hard to tell the difference.

(Use in Cheap Meat recipes.)
item_5031_Description:
Someone gutted a mangy cat.

(Use in Cheap Meat recipes.)
item_5032_Description:
Cat meat is never particularly tasty, but this is ... edible.

(Use in Cheap Meat recipes.)
item_5033_Description:
If cat meat tasted good, this would taste good.

(Use in Cheap Meat recipes.)
item_5034_Description:
An unimpressive dog-like creature yielded this unimpressive "meat".

(Use in Cheap Meat recipes.)
item_5035_Description:
Well, someone's gone and chopped up a mangy dog.

(Use in Cheap Meat recipes.)
item_5036_Description:
It's dog meat, but at least the dog was probably healthy.

(Use in Cheap Meat recipes.)
item_5037_Description:
Dog meat is an acquired taste, but at least this particular cut looks fresh and juicy.

(Use in Cheap Meat recipes.)
item_5038_Description:
A small and unimpressive dinosaur yielded this gross thing.

(Use in Cheap Meat recipes.)
item_5039_Description:
An uninviting dinosaur flank.

(Use in Cheap Meat recipes.)
item_5040_Description:
It looks... edible. Huh.

(Use in Cheap Meat recipes.)
item_5041_Description:
A hardy beast yielded this reasonably-appetizing-looking cut.

(Use in Cheap Meat recipes.)
item_5042_Description:
A mangy, possibly-diseased beast yielded this mangy, possibly-diseased meat. Mmm.

(Use in Cheap Meat recipes.)
item_5043_Description:
Some sort of beast was used for this cut. You'd want to tenderize this. A lot.

(Use in Cheap Meat recipes.)
item_5044_Description:
This is a well-cut piece of crappy meat.

(Use in Cheap Meat recipes.)
item_5045_Description:
A large and bloody cut from a dangerous beast. Pity the meat doesn't look especially tasty.

(Use in Cheap Meat recipes.)
item_5046_Description:
This might technically be edible, but the stench is almost unbearable.

(Use in Cheap Meat recipes.)
item_5047_Description:
Stinky and stringy, but if you boiled it and hammered it... it'd still be gross.

(Use in Cheap Meat recipes.)
item_5048_Description:
This would be presentable if you were very clever with the presentation.

(Use in Cheap Meat recipes.)
item_5049_Description:
A hardy slab of insect meat. Good for dishes where the meat isn't the centerpiece.

(Use in Cheap Meat recipes.)
item_5051_Description: A large odd egg. Edible if properly prepared.
item_5052_Description: The meat of a large-sized rabbit. (Use in Cheap Meat recipes.)
item_5053_Description: An exceptional piece of meat from a rabbit or hare.
item_5054_Description: Sugar that's supernaturally sweet. A favorite of fairies.
item_5056_Description: A bunch of seaweed, edible if properly dried and prepared.
item_5057_Description: Very basic vinegar used in simple dishes.
item_5058_Description: Vinegar infused with a secret blend of spices.
item_5059_Description: This vinegar infusion is only needed in the fanciest of dishes.
item_5062_Description: The green flowers of the hop plant. Used in beer brewing.
item_5063_Description: Sweet sugarcane molasses, useful for cooking or making alcohol.
item_5064_Description: An exceptional piece of meat from a deer.
item_5065_Description: An exceptional piece of meat from a cow or bull.
item_5066_Description: An exceptional piece of meat from a deer.
item_5067_Description: An exceptional piece of meat from a cow or bull. (Use in Cheap Meat recipes.)
item_5068_Description: An exceptional piece of meat from a rabbit or hare. (Use in Cheap Meat recipes.)
item_5069_Description: A chunk of raw boar meat from an impressive animal.
item_5070_Description: An especially flavorful chicken, ready for cooking.
item_5071_Description: Food-quality vegetable oil. It burns easily so is only suitable for certain dishes.
item_5072_Description: Food-quality vegetable oil with a high burn point and a neutral taste. Good for many dishes.
item_5073_Description: Food-quality vegetable oil with a high burn point and a pleasant taste. Extremely versatile.
item_5074_Description: An edible plant with a spicy and slightly bitter flavor.
item_5075_Description: This bottle contains salty water made via a secret ranalon technique. It's used as a marinade and pickling agent.
item_5076_Description: A large biting beetle. There are probably ways to prepare this for cooking.
item_5077_Description: A bunch of greens that grow in swamps and sewers. Edible if properly dried and prepared.
item_5078_Description: A spice with a bitter, fennely taste.
item_5079_Description: A chalky root with a flowery smell. Chewed by ratkin as a snack, and featured heavily in ratkin cuisine.
item_5080_Description: This bottle is full of goat's milk. (This is a snack. Snacks stack with meal foods; you can eat one of each and get the benefits of both.)
item_5081_Description: A bottle of fairy honey, said to be more delicious than mortals can bear to taste. That's just hyperbole, but it's definitely an extremely rich and complex flavor.
item_5082_Description: The fibrous and smelly heart of a spriggan plant.
item_5083_Description: Like chicken meat, but larger, and with a more pronounced flavor.
item_5084_Description:
A small, freshly-killed rodent.

(Use in Cheap Meat recipes.)
item_5085_Description: The fin of a large shark. It's supposedly edible.
item_5086_Description: Not edible by themselves, but useful in recipes.
item_5087_Description: A snail. It's about two inches in diameter. There are probably ways to prepare this for cooking.  It can also be used in Cheap Meat recipes.
item_5088_Description: Harvested from a very young hexapus (before the meat becomes inedible). There are probably special ways to prepare this for cooking. It can also be used in Cheap Meat recipes.
item_5089_Description: Harvested from a largeish frog. A cooking ingredient that can also be used in Cheap Meat recipes.
item_5090_Description: Dried but not dead, this exotic mold will spring to life when used in cheesemaking.
item_5091_Description: This vinegar infusion is a closely-guarded secret.
item_5092_Description: The pods are too spicy to eat raw, but the powder scraped from them is a valuable spice for cooking.
item_5093_Description: Fresh soybeans, ready to be processed into edible food. They are too bitter to be eaten raw, but useful in cooking.
item_5094_Description: This packet holds a proprietary mix of mystery spices. It has a very autumn-y smell.
item_5095_Description: Like chicken meat, but larger, and with a more pronounced flavor. This bird was quite well-fed.
item_5097_Description: A chunk of raw boar meat from an amazing animal.
item_51001_Description: Good for flinging small amounts of chemicals all around you. Held in the main hand.
item_51002_Description: An extraordinary staff with an alchemical beaker on the end. You can use this for staff fighting or battle chemistry. It's not suitable for fire magic, though.
item_51003_Description: An unusual staff with an alchemical beaker on the end. Not suitable for casting Fire Magic.
item_51004_Description: A beaker for flinging chemicals in combat. Held in the main hand.
item_51005_Description: A beaker for flinging chemicals in combat. Held in the main hand.
item_51006_Description: This beaker has been carefully constructed to keep healing chemicals at a lower temperature, slightly improving their effects. Held in the main hand.
item_51007_Description: This beaker is one of Xegatis' early failed attempts at restoring flesh to Nightmare Creatures. A crude Necromancy Gem is embedded in the bottom.
item_51008_Description: The story goes that a crazed elf spent a hundred years splashing himself with different acid beakers to find the one that hurt the most. This is that beaker.
item_51009_Description: This old beaker is perfectly shaped for gulping down quick-brewed healing concoctions.
item_51010_Description: Good for flinging small amounts of chemicals all around you. Held in the main hand.
item_51011_Description: Good for flinging small amounts of chemicals all around you. Held in the off hand.
item_51012_Description: A flask for flinging chemicals in combat. Held in the off hand.
item_51013_Description: A flask for flinging chemicals in combat. Held in the off hand.
item_51014_Description: This flask has been carefully constructed to keep healing chemicals at a lower temperature, slightly improving their effects. Held in the off hand.
item_51015_Description: The great alchemists of yore supposedly used flasks like this when they fought in wars. Or perhaps that's just a tale invented to sell flasks.
item_51016_Description: This old flask is perfectly shaped for gulping down quick-brewed healing concoctions.
item_51017_Description: Good for flinging small amounts of chemicals all around you. Held in the off hand.
item_51018_Description: While dwarves are mostly known for brewing alcohol, those same techniques can also be used to brew potent offensive chemicals.
item_51019_Description: The creator of this beaker designed it around what he thought dwarves would use in combat. He was utterly wrong in every way, but the beaker is still pretty powerful.
item_51020_Description: A beaker for exploding stuff. Held in the main hand.
item_51021_Description: This cunningly made flask is slightly askew, causing chemicals to spray everywhere. Only experts can hope to use it in combat.
item_51022_Description: The sneaky crone Anhinda made this flask after decades of trial and error.
item_51023_Description: Not great for splashing people, but pretty good for delivering healing concoctions.
item_51024_Description: Shaped like a snake for some reason.
item_51025_Description: An ordinary-looking glass flask that's cold to the touch, even in hot environments.
item_51026_Description: The invention of a rakshasa alchemist who loved her pet. This flask's magic helps to alert pets when their owner is in danger.
item_51027_Description: Some aberrant chemical reaction has burned off most of the crimson, but it's otherwise in good shape.
item_51028_Description: Used by the migrant house-builders that until recently found work in Povus. While it can be used as a weapon, its primary purpose was to store powerful drink.
item_51029_Description: This strange beaker constantly drips sugar-water. It has an inscription: "Keep those bees buzzin' and those birds flying high! - Galdyr"
item_51061_Description: A beaker full of a very, very stinky substance.
item_51062_Description: It has a splash-lip as well as a drinking lip, as if you'd be so foolish as to drink potions out of the same beaker you splash acids from!
item_51063_Description: A mage's staff with a splash-beaker at the top, perfect for blasting and splashing at the same time.
item_51064_Description: This beaker has been coated in many layers of deadly chemicals. Wash your hands after touching it!
item_51065_Description: A potent chemistry tool -- or weapon. Yeah, let's go with weapon.
item_51066_Description: After barely losing the annual Statehelm archery contest, Tamoko gained fame when he returned the following year and used his newly-invented beaker to win the grand prize. The beaker design has since been replicated with his blessing, delighting archery fans but causing a terrible schism in the archery-contest establishment, many of whom consider battle chemistry to be "like cheating".
item_51067_Description: Made of utterly unbreakable glass mined from the heart of a dying star, this is no measuring tool: it is a deadly weapon of war! (Because the measurement marks rubbed off long ago.)
item_51068_Description: This enchanted beaker has been adorned with pieces of glass in a geometric pattern. It is a little unwieldy as a result.
item_51069_Description: Designed by Povus's former master alchemist, Kimeta, and sold to Statehelm in large quantity.
item_5106_Description: A raw fish. So-called because of its vaguely bluish skin, the grapefish is actually fairly bland.
item_51070_Description: A seemingly frozen beaker that enables a strange influence over the weather.
item_51071_Description: This long-handled beaker has been coated in many layers of deadly chemicals. Wash your hands after touching it!
item_5107_Description: A raw fish. Found in caves throughout the world, these blind fish are normally tiny, but can grow to surprising size in nature-magic-rich environments.
item_5108_Description: A raw fish. Also known as Orcish Labyrinth Fish, these fish are surprisingly tasty when correctly prepared, but should not be eaten raw.
item_5109_Description: A raw fish. These strange fish originate in the distant continent of Fosulf. They require expert handling before they can be eaten safely, and few fishermen in Alharth know the technique.
item_51101_Description: Enchanted to accelerate tossed liquids, this off-hand tool is perfect for chemistry-based fighting.
item_51102_Description: Enchanted to accelerate tossed liquids, this off-hand tool is perfect for chemistry-based fighting.
item_51103_Description: Enchanted to accelerate tossed liquids, this off-hand tool is perfect for chemistry-based fighting.
item_51104_Description: Enchanted to accelerate tossed liquids, this off-hand tool is perfect for chemistry-based fighting.
item_51105_Description: Enchanted to accelerate tossed liquids, this off-hand tool is perfect for chemistry-based fighting.
item_51106_Description: Enchanted to accelerate tossed liquids, this off-hand tool is perfect for chemistry-based fighting.
item_51107_Description: Enchanted to accelerate tossed liquids, this off-hand tool is perfect for chemistry-based fighting.
item_5110_Description: A raw fish. These hideous creatures are surprisingly tasty.
item_5111_Description: A raw fish. Also called a yellowfin, these mid-sized ocean fish are delicious.
item_5112_Description: A raw hideous blood-sucking parasite fish.
item_5113_Description: A lump of gummy meat. It's supposedly edible, but only expert gourmands can stomach its squishy grossness.
item_5114_Description: A big raw shrimp. It needs to be de-veined and cleaned before eating.
item_5117_Description: Too small to be filleted, but still useful as bait.
item_51_Description: WARNING! This spittle is contaminated with the Lycanthropy disease. Drinking it will make you a werewolf instantly and forever! There is NO cure.
item_52001_Description: Once a fine weapon, this bow has been mistreated for a very long time. It still shoots arrows, though.
item_52002_Description: A mass-produced longbow made for infantrymen to use in a bind. Nothing special.
item_52003_Description: An Elven bow made by someone with a lot of time on their hands. (An Elf, in other words.)
item_52004_Description: An Elven bow that's been enhanced with Goblin magic.
item_52005_Description: Although technically Orcish, these bows are made by Dwarven conscripts.
item_52006_Description: Hand-made by a bored bowyer of middling skill.
item_52007_Description: Apparently once used by Lawara, an elf you met in on an island. It's high-quality, and has a custom hand plate that provides a bit of shielding.
item_52008_Description: Tremor the Myconian took this bow from a pesky elf. Myconians can't use bows, but Tremor liked plucking the string to make a 'twang' sound. It really irritated other myconians, and Tremor liked that.
item_52009_Description: Practically oozing with enchantment, this masterpiece is rarely seen outside of Elven lands.
item_52010_Description: Dwarves aren't really great at making bows with decent range. But this bow is very sturdy!
item_52011_Description: A light bow designed for precision over power.
item_52012_Description: Crafted by goblin slaves under the watchful eye of Gyrumpa, the legendary bowyer. He hated fun, and made sure his bows were especially taxing to use.
item_52013_Description: Tremor the Myconian took this bow from a pesky elf. Myconians can't use bows, but Tremor liked plucking the string to make a 'twang' sound. It really irritated other myconians, and Tremor liked that.
item_52014_Description: The Stalwarts, like all dwarves, had to work hard overcome their prejudice against ranged weapons. They developed this bludgeoning bow which acknowledges the value of archery while still insisting that hammers are better.
item_52015_Description: A rakshasa craftsman named Malgath once used this to demonstrate his skill at crafting.
item_52016_Description: A longbow optimized for long-distance targets.
item_52017_Description: A longbow optimized for quick shots.
item_52018_Description: A fairly-new item made in the Rakshasa manner, but probably not by a Rakshasa. Still not terrible.
item_52019_Description: A competent and accurate weapon made by the elves of Sedgewick Forest.
item_5201_Description: A cleaned fish, ready for cooking.
item_52020_Description: A scarred and twisted bow, barely able to be strung. A hum of darkness can be felt from within. A tiny necromancy gem is embedded in the top.
item_52021_Description: These lightweight bows aren't actually used by the Peltasts of the Crone Hegemony, but by their support archers.
item_52022_Description: An Elven bow that's been enhanced with shoddy Goblin magic.
item_52023_Description: A standard bow design used by orcish archers throughout the world. It's resilient yet accurate.
item_52024_Description: A bow designed to channel lightning. And also arrows.
item_52025_Description: This thick-handled bow gives tiny psychic cues to the owner, helping it aim perfectly. Some inherently psychic individuals can further take advantage of this mental amplification.
item_52026_Description: Commissioned by Arianna Fangblade for a close personal friend.
item_52027_Description: A deadly masterpiece that has been disguised to look like a simple sapling bow.
item_52028_Description: An otherworldly device that can fire energy projectiles. It can also fire regular arrows.
item_52029_Description: A simple bow that can pack a lot of punch for its size.
item_5202_Description: A cleaned fish, ready for cooking.
item_52030_Description: An otherworldly device that can fire energy projectiles. It can also fire regular arrows.
item_52031_Description: An Elven bow that's been enhanced with Goblin magic and lots of sharp bits.
item_52032_Description: Clearly of Elven design, and unlikely to have been made in Povus. But Statehelm bought thousands of them, just the same.
item_52033_Description: An Elven bow that's been enhanced with Goblin magic and lots of sharp bits.
item_52034_Description: The bow given to Tamoko for his contributions to the townfolk.
item_52035_Description: Used in the first annual Gazluk Archery Competition by the titular Black Agatha, who assassinated the rightful winner with it.
item_52036_Description: A classic elven-style bow with magic to enhance distance.
item_52037_Description: Tremor the Myconian took this bow from a pesky elf. Myconians can't use bows, but Tremor liked plucking the string to make a 'twang' sound. It really irritated other myconians, and Tremor liked that.
item_52038_Description: An otherworldly device that can fire energy projectiles. It can also fire regular arrows.
item_52039_Description: A bow designed by Denton Razor using a secret bowyery technique. It's surprisingly heavy for such a sleek weapon.
item_5203_Description: A cleaned fish, ready for cooking.
item_5204_Description: A cleaned fish, ready for cooking.
item_52051_Description: A very simple elven-style hunting bow.
item_52053_Description: A particularly supple elven-style hunting bow.
item_5205_Description: Cleaned crab meat, ready for cooking
item_5206_Description: A cleaned fish, ready for cooking.
item_5207_Description: A cleaned fish, ready for cooking.
item_5208_Description: A cleaned fish, ready for cooking.
item_5209_Description: A carefully killed and prepared lungfish, ready for cooking.
item_5210_Description: A skillfully cleaned fish, ready for cooking.
item_5211_Description: A skillfully cleaned fish, ready for cooking.
item_5214_Description: A peeled and de-veined shrimp, ready for cooking. It's also delicious raw.
item_5215_Description: Expertly cleaned and ready for cooking.
item_5216_Description: A cleaned fish, ready for cooking.
item_5217_Description: A cleaned fish, ready for cooking.
item_5218_Description: A cleaned fish, ready for cooking.
item_5219_Description: A cleaned fish, ready for cooking.
item_5220_Description: A cleaned fish, ready for cooking.
item_5221_Description: A cleaned fish, ready for cooking.
item_5222_Description: A cleaned fish, ready for cooking.
item_5223_Description: A cleaned fish, ready for cooking.
item_5224_Description: A cleaned fish, ready for cooking.
item_5225_Description: A cleaned fish, ready for cooking.
item_5226_Description: A cleaned fish, ready for cooking.
item_5227_Description: A cleaned fish, ready for cooking.
item_5228_Description: A cleaned fish, ready for cooking.
item_5229_Description: A cleaned fish, ready for cooking.
item_5230_Description: A cleaned fish, ready for cooking.

item_52602_Description: A simple crossbow with nothing fancy about it.
item_52603_Description: A simple crossbow with nothing fancy about it.
item_52604_Description: Designed for use by orcish healers, this crossbow comes equipped with a vicious blade for self-defense and a series of holy symbols to allow healing.
item_52605_Description: This mean-looking weapon has a necromancy gem embedded in it.
item_52606_Description: The two bolt grooves on this crossbow allow for somewhat-faster firing, but it's hardly "speedy".
item_52607_Description: This unassuming-looking weapon hides a series of fire runes on the inside of the flight groove.
item_52608_Description: This crossbow is not actually demonic; it's just designed to look intimidating. The embedded necromancy gem was probably not intended for actual use, but it does work.
item_52609_Description: A simple but robust crossbow that handles well and can be easily restrung.
item_5260_Description: A cleaned fish, ready for cooking.
item_52610_Description: An over-engineered dwarven weapon. It requires a lot of maintenance, but most users find the accelerated reload-time worth it.
item_52611_Description: This seems like a simple evil crossbow, but in actuality is far more complex, with bizarre otherworldly magic.
item_52612_Description: One of the few "Povus Special" weapons actually made in Povus. It has classic Deftwhisper styling.
item_52613_Description: An antique style of dwarven crossbow, discontinued due to a tendency to explode while loaded. But this one seems fine.
item_52614_Description: A masterpiece dwarven crossbow further augmented by goblin mechanists. "A kiss from this rose leads to the grave" is engraved on the side.
item_5261_Description: A cleaned fish, ready for cooking.
item_5262_Description: A cleaned fish, ready for cooking.
item_5263_Description: A cleaned fish, ready for cooking.
item_52651_Description: A very simple elven-style hunting bow.
item_52653_Description: A particularly supple elven-style hunting bow.
item_52_Description: Test potion transforms you into a pig. Permanent until dispelled! WARNING: think before you drink!
item_53001_Description: Made by an amateur, this punching weapon barely fits onto your hand. But sometimes an ugly, stupid-looking weapon can inspire more fear than a good weapon. Not really. But keep thinking that while you wear these.
item_53002_Description: They've turned all green and gross. But they'll still work to make your punches more painful.
item_53003_Description: These fit over your knuckles to make your punches hurt more. Now with real faux-brass coating!
item_53004_Description: Designed to fit over leather gloves, these brass knuckles have nasty bumps to make your punches cause extra injury.
item_53005_Description: It's sturdy, but it's so simple that it'd be hard not to be sturdy. But it's not false advertising, anyway.
item_53006_Description: This simple fist weapon was fitted for a goblin's hand, but you can make it work. (Cestuses count as an empty hand for purposes of Unarmed attacks.)
item_53007_Description: This simple fist weapon will make your punches hurt more.
item_53008_Description: This simple fist weapon will make your punches hurt more.
item_53009_Description: Preferred by orcish martial artists, this black-iron fist weapon is intimidatingly heavy in the hand.
item_53011_Description: This grim fist weapon was clearly inspired by the Human legend of the Green Man, a druid who supposedly wanders the forests and punches wayward teens to death if they're out past curfew.
item_53012_Description: It was found in the gut of a giant spider. That's really the only spider-related thing going on here.
item_53013_Description: The preferred punching thingy of elven bullies.
item_53014_Description: It's a bit cramped for larger races, but this Dwarven unarmed weapon feels impressively heavy.
item_53015_Description: A piece of arm machinery from a large ancient golem.
item_53016_Description: This punching weapon has a little mechanical serpent head on it which can be used to inject poison deep into a target.
item_53017_Description: This is just a piece of metal, but it's been covered in strange substances that help protect the hand of the puncher.
item_53018_Description: Someone has carved a fist weapon out of glasstone, a rock known for its supernatural lightness. The glasstone market is in shambles now that glasstone jewelry has fallen out of fashion among humans, so new uses are becoming affordable.
item_53020_Description: This strangely-weighted punching weapon requires skill to use, but is quite deadly in the right hand.
item_53021_Description: The creepy orcish mages known as Mutterers sometimes use weapons like this, designed to bleed the enemy.
item_53022_Description: A piece of arm machinery from a large ancient golem.
item_53023_Description: Designed by blacksmith-in-hiding Torgan, this weapon was weighted by a master. It requires quite a strong arm, though.
item_53024_Description: A weapon made by ratkin martial artists. Ratkins are renowned for their use of poison.
item_53025_Description: The weighting of this weapon is awkward for non-fairies, but it works after you get used to it.
item_53026_Description: Used by ranalon martial artists to great effect.
item_53027_Description: A tool used by rakshasa housebuilders, who like to hammer nails with their fists. This one has been cleaned up and enchanted.
item_53028_Description: This fist-shaped thing was found embedded in a particularly ornery myconian.
item_53029_Description: Designed (but never used) by the slightly-mad gadgeteer Tamiq. It uses a series of magically-powered cascading motors to augment the punching power.
item_53030_Description: A piece of arm machinery from a large ancient golem.
item_5306_Description: This head of broccoli looks a lot like a little green tree.
item_5307_Description: Orange and pointy. Yup, it's a carrot.
item_5308_Description: A useful spice for cooking. It has an aromatic, warm and slightly bitter taste.
item_5310_Description: A big bunch of purple grapes.
item_5313_Description: This banana is in good shape - no brown spots yet.
item_5315_Description: A nice round gourd full of mushy stuff.
item_5320_Description: A bunch of fresh barley grass.
item_5321_Description: A bunch of sugarcane stalks. Sugarcane can be cooked into molasses and sugar.
item_5322_Description: Not really berries at all, these are actually seed cones with some bitter mushy stuff on the end. Useful as a spice and flavoring in brewing.
item_5323_Description: A fresh uncooked ear of corn. Corn is a bit of a delicacy in the Council Lands, although it's extremely common elsewhere.
item_5324_Description: Tart and sweet. A delicacy to some, but not as versatile for cooking as red apples.
item_5325_Description: Fruit from a strange plant that grows in the fae realm. While delicious, sprigganberries are incredibly poisonous unless the tiny seeds are carefully removed. (This is a snack. Snacks stack with meal foods; you can eat one of each and get the benefits of both.)
item_5327_Description: A strong but pleasant-smelling herb used in cooking.
item_5328_Description: A large sweet fruit. (This is a snack. Snacks stack with meal foods; you can eat one of each and get the benefits of both.)
item_5329_Description: Fresh green peas, ready for cooking.
item_5330_Description: Some fresh cranberries. Too bitter to be eaten raw, but useful in cooking.
item_5331_Description: A large gourd full of sticky bitter mush. (This is a snack. Snacks stack with meal foods; you can eat one of each and get the benefits of both.)
item_5332_Description: Wheat with a finer quality, preferred over Human wheat for certain dishes.
item_5333_Description: A watery fruit from the distant crone lands. (This is a snack. Snacks stack with meal foods; you can eat one of each and get the benefits of both.)
item_5334_Description: Made from orcish wheat. This has a notably superior taste to human wheat flour, and is preferred for high-end baked goods.
item_5335_Description: This bioluminescent fruit smells like death, but has a pleasant mild taste. (This is a snack. Snacks stack with meal foods; you can eat one of each and get the benefits of both.)
item_5336_Description: This is basically inedible. It has limited alchemical uses.
item_5338_Description: A big bunch of semisweet mulberries.
item_5339_Description: A chewy, hearty grain that can be milled into chewy, hearty flour.
item_5340_Description: Tundra rye that's been milled into a bakable flour.
item_5341_Description: Corn ground into meal. It's useful for cooking in this form.
item_5343_Description: An unpleasant-tasting but nutritionally dense substance.
item_5344_Description: Gross little seeds from a weed. They can't be grown, but can be ground into an edible form.
item_5345_Description: An unpleasant-tasting but nutritionally dense substance.
item_5346_Description: Dehulled oats. They can be cooked in this form or ground to oat flour.
item_5347_Description: Oats ground into a powder, ready for cooking.
item_5348_Description: Slightly magical spelt grown at night. Most useful when ground into flour.
item_5349_Description: Moonspelt ground into flour for cooking.
item_53501_Description: Some bones strapped to a handle make this off-hand martial weapon look as stupid as it feels.
item_53502_Description: Sharpened bone "blades" protrude from this off-hand martial weapon. It's silly looking, but dangerous!
item_53503_Description: This martial artist's glove is adorned with small sharpened metal protrusions. It smells like soup.
item_53504_Description: Fashionable, but a little uncomfortable to wear unless you're a Rakshasa.
item_53505_Description: A skilled metalsmith put this left-handed fighting claw together. It's a little uncomfortable to wear, though.
item_53506_Description: A skilled metalsmith put this left-handed fighting claw together. It's a little uncomfortable to wear, though.
item_53507_Description: The Rakshasa martial artist handed this weapon down to a promising pupil.
item_53508_Description: Long, deadly blades jut out from this glove-like weapon. It only fits the left hand.
item_53509_Description: When the Council demanded that the Rakshasa provide renumeration for every human that was injured at the Battle of Rahu, outraged Rakshasa tore up the treaty and killed the messengers, leading to the Sand War. The Rakshasa won the war with claws like this, fashioned after weapons from their home world.
item_53510_Description: Made of Cold Iron, this weapon is used in fae civil wars. Fairies who wield it are in constant pain from the metal... but not as much pain as their victims.
item_53512_Description: Few of the legendary Dwarven Stalwarts used unarmed weapons, but the ones that did wore claws like this.
item_53513_Description: The Rakshasa outcasts in the Ilmari Desert enchant these claws with life-saving protection from the desert elements.
item_53514_Description: A claw enchanted specifically for making horrible scars.
item_53515_Description: A claw tailored specifically for the Claw Barrage maneuver.
item_53516_Description: A claw with ugly tassels dangling from it. Being hit by this stupid-looking weapon is especially infuriating.
item_53517_Description: Another public-relations disaster by the famous Veilmoor clan of human smiths, these claws are each imbued with the soul of a kitten who would otherwise have been euthanized. While it's not technically illegal to use such small souls in enchanting, the 'save the kittens forever' campaign was not appreciated by the weapon-buying public. But as is typical with Veilmoor products, the weapon is so good that nothing else matters too much.
item_53518_Description: Carefully designed to avoid hurting you while sprinting.
item_53519_Description: Designed by blacksmith-in-hiding Torgan, this wicked weapon features hooked talons to rip into flesh.
item_53520_Description: A weapon made by a ratkin martial artist. It fits poorly to other races' hands, but it's nothing a little adhesive can't fix.
item_53521_Description: Used by some troll martial-artists that you definitely do not want to meet.
item_53523_Description: Amazingly sharp given that the blades are made of ancient bone. It exudes an uncomfortable aura.
item_5352_Description: While not the nicest way to prepare cocoa beans, this is useful for some recipes.
item_5353_Description: A powerful flavor used by master chefs.
item_53606_Description: A claw-style fighting weapon with spring-loaded pop-up mechanisms to disperse chemicals. A tricky weapon to master.
item_53607_Description: A claw-style fighting weapon with spring-loaded pop-up mechanisms to disperse chemicals. A tricky weapon to master.
item_53608_Description: A claw-style fighting weapon with spring-loaded pop-up mechanisms to disperse chemicals. A tricky weapon to master.

item_53617_Description: A deadly slashing weapon for use by expert martial artists.

item_54001_Description: A well-made but simple lute, perfect for beginners.
item_54003_Description: An ocarina made of horn. This instrument is especially easy to learn.
item_54004_Description: A brass horn of dwarven design.
item_54006_Description: A tiny harp. Sounds pretty good.
item_54007_Description: Found within a sentient weather phenomenon, this bizarre horn gives off an eerie glow.
item_54008_Description: A set of enchanted chimes that can be both plucked and strummed.

item_54240_Description: A belt adorned with an odd rune that gives the wearer a certain kind of luck. An inscription on the back in elvish reads, "To the hunter of my heart. May you always find joy in nature. -Y"
item_54241_Description: A belt adorned with numerous frog runes that gives the wearer a certain kind of luck.
item_54301_Description: A belt adorned with an odd rune that gives the wearer a certain kind of luck.
item_54302_Description: A belt adorned with an odd rune that gives the wearer a certain kind of luck.
item_54311_Description: A simple belt with a pouch.
item_54313_Description: A nice belt with lots of little pockets.
item_54314_Description: A clever belt with lots of compartments for storing stuff.
item_54325_Description: This belt has exotic shamanic runes etched into it.
item_54326_Description: A strange padded belt that helps the wearer hold their ground.
item_54331_Description: A belt with broken glass along the interior, causing the wearer intense pain. Most people would never wear such a belt, but masochists may find this pain enhances their sense of survival.
item_54332_Description: A belt covered in hooks that face inward, causing the wearer intense pain. Most people would never wear such a belt, but masochists may find this pain enhances their sense of survival.
item_54333_Description: A belt covered in small knives that impale the wearer, causing the wearer intense pain. Most people would never wear such a belt, but masochists may find this pain enhances their sense of survival.
item_54334_Description: A belt covered in terrible spikes that face inward, causing the wearer intense pain. Most people would never wear such a belt, but masochists may find this pain enhances their sense of survival.
item_54503_Description: This uncomfortable wooden spider suit was designed with the best of intentions. It just sucks. But the wood can act as a focal point for druidic spiders.
item_54505_Description: Carefully crafted of dozens of little jointed sections, this harness can hold a lot of weight. The wood can also act as a focal point for druidic spiders.
item_54507_Description: Lovingly crafted to a high standard, this wooden spider suit makes spiders even more adaptable than normal. Its wood can also act as a focal point for druidic spiders.
item_54508_Description: Lovingly crafted to a high standard, this wooden spider suit makes spiders even more adaptable than normal. Its wood can also act as a focal point for druidic spiders.
item_54509_Description: Lovingly crafted to a high standard, this wooden spider suit makes spiders even more adaptable than normal. Its wood can also act as a focal point for druidic spiders.
item_54510_Description: Lovingly crafted to a high standard, this wooden spider suit makes spiders even more adaptable than normal. Its wood can also act as a focal point for druidic spiders.
item_54511_Description: A belt buckle with a holy symbol painted on it, allowing the wearer to tap into the grace of Arisetsu. Unfortunately, the belt is extremely painful to wear, and even worse, the paint is very delicate: prolonged use in combat will cause the belt to break, requiring replacement.
item_54512_Description: Lovingly crafted to a high standard, this wooden spider suit makes spiders even more adaptable than normal. Its wood can also act as a focal point for druidic spiders.
item_54513_Description: Lovingly crafted to a high standard, this wooden spider suit makes spiders even more adaptable than normal. Its wood can also act as a focal point for druidic spiders.

A simple belt buckle depicting a hammer.

This buckle slowly decays when worn in combat until it eventually disappears.

A nice belt buckle depicting a hammer.

This buckle slowly decays when worn in combat until it eventually disappears.

A gold-inlaid belt buckle depicting a hammer.

This buckle slowly decays when worn in combat until it eventually disappears.

This buckle slowly decays when worn in combat until it eventually disappears.
item_54605_Description:
A very ornate belt buckle depicting a hammer.

This buckle slowly decays when worn in combat until it eventually disappears.
item_54606_Description:
A ridiculously ornate belt buckle depicting a hammer.

This buckle slowly decays when worn in combat until it eventually disappears.
item_54607_Description:
An elegant buckle covered in symbols. There's lots of hammers.

This buckle slowly decays when worn in combat until it eventually disappears.
item_54608_Description:
An elegant buckle covered in symbols. There's lots of hammers and clubs.

This buckle slowly decays when worn in combat until it eventually disappears.

An elegant buckle covered in symbols. There's demonic beings swinging hammers, among other things.

This buckle slowly decays when worn in combat until it eventually disappears.

An elegant buckle covered in symbols. The most prominent symbol is a god-like being swinging a hammer.

This buckle slowly decays when worn in combat until it eventually disappears.

This buckle is crudely made but conveys the basic idea that hammering things is fun.

item_55513_Description: A stick that's good for crushing.
item_55515_Description: Otis the Ogre once used this in an epic arena battle. But he gave it away after he found a better one.

item_55517_Description: A dense stick that packs a punch.

item_55519_Description: This club is coated in misleading magic. It's soaked with blood, but looks pristine!

item_55569_Description: This club is bizarrely light for being so heavy.

item_55609_Description: Asterion the minotaur customized this ridiculously heavy hammer to be even heavier. A phrase is inscribed on the shaft in common tongue: "If you cannot lift my hammer, I shall lift it for you."
